35Pɔ̀ ɔnutɔ aaʋi si, “Kitɛ biayɔ ni bekpo ʋa a, xe biadzi ligbã ɛ? Kisugya wolilu kiaze ní ba awla a?” 36Linukpaboe woli lɛ ya! Kidiliɛ gi mlɛkpɛ ɛ, kiʋídzi ligbã́ sani si kitse xe ɛ. 37Lɛ xegi mlɛkpɛ bidɔ ɛ, aní ose ɔlɔ gi aaze e, lɔ mlɛkpɛ pɔ̀ lisebi i, nyafɛ adeʋi loo esebi dzedze. 38Pɔ̀ Aya aki lɛ kilɛlɛ liɛ mɛ gi apɛ si ose lo aaze e; lɛ ki lisebi kakaa ɛ, izɛki lɛ lɛ kilɛlɛ mɛ. 39Kisugya tile elu kíma ɛki bidɔ gbagbe ʋe petee e, kisugyaɛ kitɔ kilɛ ɛki banɔ gbagbe ba, kitɔ ki baga ʋa, lɛ kitɔ ki kudzɔʋi yɔ lɛ kitɔ ki akpa la fɛ ɛ.
40Lɛ bisugya ʋɛ bilɛ ní lifunu ne fɛ, lɛ bisugya ʋɛ bilɛ ní kesea aʋa; kupedi uliɔ klɔ gi kuli ní lifunu ne bidɔ mɛ su i, kunu lide tile lɛ kupedi uliɔ gi kuli ní kesea ʋa bidɔ mɛ su i, kunu kidɔ dzedze. 41Liwo le lɛ kupedi yo kunu lide tile, ɔdzidzi yɛ yɛ kupedi yo kunu kudzedze, lɛ badzidziebi ʋa fɛ ba kupedi yo kunu kudzedze. Lɛ badzidziebi bi ʋa ba kupedi yo kulɛ lidze lidze.
42Nite litraze xe bɛyɔni bekpo ʋa a, xe bedzi ligbã́ a. Kisugyaɛ liɛ gi bizɛkpɛ ní sise se me xe gi kwitse e, kizeezizi pɔ̀ xegi bɛyɔni kɛ ɛ, kiaze ligbã́ nɛ gi kitrazizi i. 43Biavive ke kisugya gi kiizizi i, pɔ̀ biayɔni kɛ ní kusudra mɛ ɛ. Bevive ke ní kubotsobotso me pɔ̀ xe bɛyɔ ni kɛ ɛ, kiayɔ ní kigagla mɛ. 44Bevive ke kesea ʋa ya kisugya; pɔ̀ biayɔni kɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ mɛ kisugya a.
Lɛ ese linu si xegi kelea mɛ kisugyaɛ kilɛ ɛ, kɔlɔ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ mɛ kisugyaɛ fɛ kilɛ ɛ. 45Lɛ bɛŋwlimi si “Ɔnɔ tɔpu yɛ Adam edzì kidɔ gbagbe,” Pɔ̀ Adam gɔgɔɛ gi yɛ onu Kristo ye e, onu Ɔfuɛfuɛ gi izɛki ligbã́ a. 46Aní kidiliɛ gi kilɛ ní Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ mɛ kitu ba a, pɔ̀ kidiliɛ gi kilɛ ní kelea lia mɛ kisugyaɛ mɛ ɛ, lɛ kede e, lɛ kidiliɛ gi kilɛ ní Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ mɛ kɛ kigba ɛkpɛ ɛ. 47Ɔnɔ tɔpu yɛ ɛ, sise bɛkɔ ɛ ʋɔ yɛ, lɛ ádɔ kesea mɛ ɛ; pɔ̀ ɔnɔ ʋla yɛ ádɔ ní lifunu ne e. 48Banama gi bali kesea aʋa ɛ, balɛ ŋwisi ɔniliɛ klɔ gi sise se bɛkɔ ɛ ʋɔ yɛ ɛ; lɛ banama gi bali ní lifunu ne e, ba kyɛ ba lɛ ŋwisi ɔniliɛ klɔ gi ádɔ ní lifunu ne e. 49Lɛ kiliɛ gi kulɛ abla ŋwisi ɔniliɛ gi ádɔ ní kesea aʋa ɛ, niteme ke kwitraze ŋwisi ɔniliɛ gi ádɔ ní lifunu ne e.
50Mɛ benemi ba a, liboe lɛ gi mɛɛpani linu si blɔ kesea aʋa ya kisugyaɛ ni ika lɛ ɛ, bítratani ɔŋakɔ kumɔ ní Aya yɛ kaʋaŋaa mɛ ɛ. Bidɔ gi bizeezizi i, bítratani ɔŋakɔ kumɔ ní bidɔ gi bitrázizi i me.
51Mlata kutu tɔ mido liboe kpokpo gi litu banima kugyɔ ɛ si mlɔ. Blɔ petee kutrátse e, pɔ̀ bɛtrákyi blɔ petee blɔ kilɛlɛɛ mɛ ɛ. 52Liaba lɛ mɛ ní lipoe kpekpe etɔ mɛ, kilɛ gi ɔnɔ aada yɛ bimbi ʋe ʋleʋleʋle e, xegi beme kakuklui gɔgɔa ɛ. Lɛ ese linu si xegi beme kakuklui ya ɛ, banama gi betse e, biayɔni ba gi batrátse tɔ ɔ. Lɛ blɔ banama gi kuli ligbã́ nɛ ɛ, biakyi blɔ kyɛ blɔ kizezee me. 53Lɛ ese linu si lilɛ si bekyi blɔ bisugyaɛ ʋɛ gi bizezizi i, lilɛ si bekyi ʋe tɔ bidzi bisugya gi bitrazizi liwewe e; blɔ kesea aʋa ya bisugya ʋɛ gi bizeetse e, lilɛ si bekyi ʋe tɔ bidzi bisugya gi bítratse liwewe e.
54Lɛ xegi blɔ bisugyaɛ ʋɛ gi bizeezizi i, xegi bekyi ʋe e, xe bidzi bisugya gi bitrázizi liwewe e, xe kisugyaɛ liɛ gi kizeetse e, xe kidzi kisugya gi kitrátse liwewe e, kɔ Aya yɛ liboe lɛ gi bɛŋwlimi plɛ ɛ, liba lɛ mɛ si:
“Bɛmlɔ kutseo ní kaʋaŋaa mɛ ɛ!”
55“Oo Kutse nifɔ wɔ kuʋaŋaa kulɛ ɛ?
Kutseo nifɔ wɔ ome no ɔlɛ ɛ?”
56Lɛ ese linu si kutseo kɔ ome no onu kuzimizi yo, lɛ kuzimizi yo kuzɛɛzi kɔ kigaglaɛ dɔni ɔsã nɔ klɔ ɔ. 57Pɔ̀ kwido Aya ɖase gi yɛ aki si kwiŋa aʋa gani blɔ Ɔkaɛ Yesu Kristo me e!
58Lɛ loso mɛ benemi lɔlɔ ba a, mlalɛɛ gaglaa tɔ mlakíki si kididɔ kihwani mlɔ ɔ. Mlakɔ mlɔ isu ya petee aki Ɔkaɛ yɛ axwɛ na lipoe kakaa, lɛ ese linu si mlete si mlɔ axwɛ na ní Ɔkaɛ mɛ ɛ, atráze efiteete e.

english_text:
A Collection for God's People
1 When you collect money for God's people, I want you to do exactly what I told the churches in Galatia to do. 2That is, each Sunday each of you must put aside part of what you have earned. If you do this, you won't have to take up a collection when I come. 3Choose some followers to take the money to Jerusalem. I will send them on with the money and with letters which show that you approve of them. 4If you think I should go along, they can go with me.
Paul's Travel Plans
5 After I have gone through Macedonia, I hope to see you 6and visit with you for a while. I may even stay all winter, so that you can help me on my way to wherever I will be going next. 7If the Lord lets me, I would rather come later for a longer visit than to stop off now for only a short visit. 8 I will stay in Ephesus until Pentecost, 9because there is a wonderful opportunity for me to do some work here. But there are also many people who are against me.
10 When Timothy arrives, give him a friendly welcome. He is doing the Lord's work, just as I am. 11Don't let anyone mistreat him. I am looking for him to return to me together with the other followers. So when he leaves, send him off with your blessings.
12I have tried hard to get our friend Apollos to visit you with the other followers. He doesn't want to come just now, but he will come when he can.
Personal Concerns and Greetings
13Keep alert. Be firm in your faith. Stay brave and strong. 14Show love in everything you do.
15 You know that Stephanas and his family were the first in Achaia to have faith in the Lord. They have done all they can for God's people. My friends, I ask you 16to obey leaders like them and to do the same for all others who work hard with you.
17I was glad to see Stephanas and Fortunatus and Achaicus. Having them here was like having you. 18They made me feel much better, just as they made you feel better. You should appreciate people like them.
19 Greetings from the churches in Asia.
Aquila and Priscilla, together with the church that meets in their house, send greetings in the name of the Lord.
20All the Lord's followers send their greetings.
Give each other a warm greeting.
21I am signing this letter myself: PAUL.
22I pray that God will put a curse on everyone who doesn't love the Lord. And may the Lord come soon.
23I pray the Lord Jesus will be kind to you.
24I love everyone who belongs to Christ Jesus.
translation_text:
Bidɔ kɔkɔ ki Aya yɛ banima ní Yerusalem
1Abla ní mlɔ beʋiʋi ʋa ní bidɔ kɔkɔɛ ʋɛ gi bɛkpɛ likpɔ ɛki Aya yɛ banima su i, lilɛ si mlabitɛ kiliɛ gi masi ɔsɔli ha lɔ ní Galatia si babitɛ ɛ. 2Mlɔ mɛ ɔnɔ kakaa ɔbla kibɔɛ liɛ gi mlɛmɔ ɛ, kɛ kapɛatɔ ní ogle no lɔ liwe tɔpu nɛ aʋa a, atɔ likízia tɔɔ si mlaazɛ gyɔ bibɔ xe maba a. 3Xegi maba ɛ maŋwlimi kuplekpa gi kwiabu banama a, gi mlaakya ɛbasɛ ɛ, lɛ ba biakɔ mlɔ bidɔ kɔkɔ ʋɛ ɛtsini Yerusalem. 4Lɛ xegi liaziã ɛ, mɛ ni ba kwiaga kudeo trɛ.
Paulo yɛ ɔtutɔ yɛ kupɛpɛ ɔ
5Maaba mlɔ klɔ xegi mazɛga Makedonia ɛ, lɛ ese linu si mabitɛ kutrɔtrɔ si mizɛɛga Makedonia. 6Nyafɛ maaze ní mlɔ su taʋɛ, loo nyafɛ kublɔ lipoe le petee, atɔ mlaatani mɛ kupɔnɔ ní mɛ kude gaga mɛ tsinɔ níklɔ gi matradzɛ ɛ. 7Mápɛ si miba edi mlo ko o, tɔ mitsa ɛza; mapɛ si miba eze ní mlɔ su lipoe gblamadza a, xe Ɔkaɛ aki kude e. 8Maaze ni ya ní Efeso zɛkɔna ni Pentekoste liwe le aʋa. 9Lɛ ese linu si kudeonu kwidra ɛki mɛ ni ya kí axwɛ gbã kuwawa, linya ɛli lɛ mɛ si banɔ glegle bɛyɔ kedu ní mɛ su.
10Xegi Timoteo aba mlɔ klɔ ɛ, mlakíki lifu liku ye e, gi ɔlɛ ní mlɔ kusi i, lɛ ese linu si Ɔkaɛ yɛ axwɛ ɛɛwa, ŋwisi kiliɛ gi mɛ fɛ mɛɛwa la kinɛ ɛ. 11Mlakígbe si mlátrazi yɛ ɛ, boŋu i, mlapɔni yɛ tɔ odziba ní mɔ klɔ ní kusuyɔ mɛ; meedi yɛ kudeonu si aaba nì baʋaziŋa dzedze ba.
12Abla ní blɔ onemi ye Apolo su i, mɛɛkpɛ yɛ kunugu yo veve si tɔ ɔbadi mlo ni baʋaziŋa gɔgɔ ba, pɔ̀ ɔ́pɛ si yiba ní lipoe lɛ ya mɛ ɛ. Xegi amɔ kudeonu dzedze e, aaba di mlo.
13Mleze ní aŋu, tɔ mlalɛɛ mumɔ ní kuziŋaa mɛ, ɔna oze ní mlɔ lipo tɔ mleze gagla. 14Mlabitɛ kidɔ kakaa ní kulɔlɔ mɛ.
15Mlete sì Stefana ni yɛ kepea ɛ; ba beze baʋaziŋa tɔpu ba ní Axaya, lɛ bɛkɔ litɔ lɛ mɛ ɛki Aya yɛ banima a. Maki mlɔ litukpo, mɛ benemi ba si, 16mlakɔ isu akpɛ ní banɔaklɔ elu ese e, ani ɔnɔ kakaa gi ɛɛwa axwɛ na a, xe eesumu ni ba ese e. 17Isami iku me si Stefana ni Fortunato ani Axaiko bɛba lɛ ese linu si bɛmani mɛ kidiliɛ gi kíma ní mɛ awla ɛ. 18Lɛ bɛkpɛ mɛ ɔna nɔ nì mlɔ ina nɛ kyɛ isami i. Banɔaklɔ ʋa elue e, lilɛ si ba kiboebitɛɛ kiku aŋu u.
Paulo yɛ kunɔpanɔ gɔgɔɔ
19Isɔliha lɛ ní Asia kesea mɛ ɛ, ipani mlɔ, Akwila ni Priskila ani ɔha lɔ gi izɛɛtsa ní ba kepea mɛ ɛ, bɛpani mlɔ mɔmɔmɔ ni isami. 20Benemi ba pɔ́ ní ya a, bɛpani mlɔ. Mlakpɛ mlɔ ahanuglɔ ba wolaa ní sinemi me.
21Mɛ Paulo mɛ maŋwlimi kunɔpanɔ liɔ ya nì mɛ awla la.
22Xe ɔnutɔ ɔ́lɔ blɔ Ɔkaɛ ɛ, ifixoxo igbani yɛ kpɛ. Oo Ɔka ba!
23Blɔ Ɔkaɛ Yesu yɛ lipomepɛpɛ nɛ lize ni mlo.
24Mɛ kulɔlɔ ki mlɔ petee ní Kristo Yesu me e. Atɔ liba lɛ mɛ.

english_text:
1 From Paul, chosen by God to be an apostle of Jesus Christ, and from Timothy, who is also a follower.
To God's church in Corinth and to all of God's people in Achaia.
2I pray that God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you and will bless you with peace!
Paul Gives Thanks
3Praise God, the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ! The Father is a merciful God, who always gives us comfort. 4He comforts us when we are in trouble, so that we can share this same comfort with others in trouble. 5We share in the terrible sufferings of Christ, but also in the wonderful comfort he gives. 6We suffer in the hope that you will be comforted and saved. And because we are comforted, you will also be comforted, as you patiently endure suffering like ours. 7You never disappoint us. You suffered as much as we did, and we know that you will be comforted as we were.
8 My friends, I want you to know what a hard time we had in Asia. Our sufferings were so horrible and so unbearable that death seemed certain. 9In fact, we felt sure we were going to die. But this made us stop trusting in ourselves and start trusting God, who raises the dead to life. 10God saved us from the threat of death, and we are sure that he will do it again and again. 11Please help us by praying for us. Then many people will give thanks for the blessings we receive in answer to all these prayers.
Paul's Change of Plans
12We can be proud of our clear conscience. We have always lived honestly and sincerely, especially when we were with you. And we were guided by God's gift of undeserved grace instead of by the wisdom of this world. 13I am not writing anything you cannot read and understand. I hope you will understand it completely, 14just as you already partly understand us. Then when our Lord Jesus returns, you can be as proud of us as we are of you.
15I was so sure of your pride in us that I had planned to visit you first of all. In this way you would have the blessing of two visits from me— 16 once on my way to Macedonia and again on my return from there. Then you could send me on to Judea. 17Do you think I couldn't make up my mind about what to do? Or do I seem like someone who says “Yes” or “No” simply to please others? 18God can be trusted, and so can I, when I say our answer to you has always been “Yes” and never “No.” 19 This is because Jesus Christ the Son of God is always “Yes” and never “No.” And he is the one Silas, Timothy, and I told you about.
20Christ says “Yes” to all God's promises. This is why we have Christ to say “Amen” for us to the glory of God. 21And so God makes it possible for you and us to stand firmly together with Christ. God is also the one who chose us 22and put his Spirit in our hearts to show that we belong only to him.
23God is my witness that I stayed away from Corinth, just to keep from being hard on you. 24We are not bosses who tell you what to believe. We are working with you to make you glad, because your faith is strong.
translation_text:
1Keplekpaa lia ya kɛdɔ ní mɛ Paulo gi menu Kristo Yesu yɛ ɔnɔkpɛkpɛ ɛ, gani Aya yɛ kupɛ́pɛ onu ani blɔ onemi ye Timoteo klɔ. Kí Aya yɛ ɔsɔli ha lɔ ní Korinto kɔpɔni kí Aya yɛ banima petee ní Axaya kesea mɛ:
2Atɔ Aya blɔ Ɔka ani blɔ Ɔkaɛ Yesu Kristo baki mlɔ lipomepɛpɛ ni kusuyɔ.
Aya yɛ Izɛyɔ Ɔnɔ Kakaa yɛ Ɔna
3Mlaki tɔ kudo Aya gi onu blɔ Ɔkaɛ Yesu Kristo Yɛka ɖase e. Ɔka gi izedi banɔ asigaga a, xe yɛ izɛki ɔnɔ kakaa ɔnadɔdɔ ɛ! 4Yɛ izɛɛpɔni blɔ ní blɔ baxixa ʋa petee me tɔ blɔ kwitani banama gi beku baxixa ede ede na mɛ ɛ, kupɔnɔ, kiliɛ gi blɔ fɛ Aya apɔni blɔ ɛ. 5Lɛ ese linu si kiliɛ gi Kristo yɛ ɔzia mɔmɔ nɔ ɛgbigba eku kpaŋu ní blɔ agbã́ na mɛ ɛ, nite ke gani Kristo me e, blɔ ɔnadɔdɔ lɔ aagbigba. 6Xegi kuli ní kuyeʋiyeʋi me e, linu mlɔ ɔnadɔdɔ ni kubúbú; lɛ xegi bɛyɔ blɔ ɔna ɛ, linu ɔnadɔdɔ ɛki mlɔ, gi lɔ izɛɛmani litɔplɛplɛkese ní igyidodo me ki mlɔ ní ɔzia nɔ ke gi kwimɔ mɛ ɛ. 7Lɛ blɔ kudeonudidi yo ki mlɔ kuli gagla lɛ ese linu si kwite si ŋwisi kiliɛ gi mlɛɛmɔ ɔzia nɔ ɛpɔni blɔ ɛ, nite ke mlamɔ ɔnadɔdɔ ni blɔ.
8Kúpɛ si mleze ní litumu me benemi ba a, ní kupepe uliɔ gi kwiba blɔ aʋa ní Asia kesea mɛ su u. Bemi ní blɔ aʋa gbɛgbɛ gi lisani blɔ kututu si ɔna óze ni blɔ lipo tɔ ɔ, si kwiaze ligbã́ kyɛ. 9Ní anukwarɛɛ mɛ ɛ, kwimɔ egyesi si bɛtsa kutsépia ɛki blɔ. Pɔ̀ liboe lɛ ya liba lɛ mɛ nite tɔ kukíkɔ aŋu atrɔ ní blɔ isu ya aʋa a, pɔ̀ tɔ kukɔ trɔ ní Aya aʋa a, gi yɛ izɛɛyɔni bekpo ʋa ɛ. 10Yɛ ebu blo adɔ ni kuxixa ʋidi uliɔ ya mɛ ɛ, lɛ aabu blo o. Lɛ loso e, kwikɔ blɔ kudeonudidi yo ɛtrɔ ní yɛ aʋa si aatrɛ lɛ aʋa gi aabu blo o, 11kiliɛ gi mlɛpɔni blɔ ɔ, gani mlɔ Aya kuxwaxwa mɛ. Kɔ banɔ glegle biado ɖase ní blɔ litukpo kɔkpɛ lipomepɛpɛ nɛ kupɔnɔ liɔ gi bɛki blɔ ɔ, gi banɔ glegle bɛxwa Aya loso o.
Paulo Atsrɛ Yɛ Batrɔtrɔ ʋa
12Abla ɛ, liboe lɛ ya aʋa kwidɔ isu ya; blɔ ina nɛ iisi blɔ si kwivu isu ya mumɔ ní kelea lia ya mɛ. Liveve le, ní blɔ ni mlɔ siŋanɛ sɛ mɛ, ní kudradra ni anukwarɛɛ gi bidɔ ní Aya kɔ mɛ ɛ. Kwibitɛ lɛ aní gani kelea mɛ kuboete me e, pɔ̀ gani Aya yɛ lipomepɛpɛ nɛ mɛ ɛ. 13Lɛ ese linu si aní eboe dzedze gi mlatrátani la kukla a loo gi mlatrátani la ese kunu i, la kwiŋwlimi kí mlɔ. Lɛ meedi kudeonu si i 14ŋwisi kiliɛ gi mlenu blɔ ese kapɛ ni kapɛ ɛ, mlaba nu blɔ ese mɔmɔmɔ tɔ mlaatani isu kudɔ ní blɔ aʋa ŋwisi kiliɛ gi kwiadɔ isu ní mlɔ aʋa a, ní Ɔkaɛ yɛ liwe le aʋa kinɛ ɛ.
15Gi maka ní lɛ aʋa loso e, matrɔ si maaba di mlo kpɛ ɔtɔ, atɔ mlɔamɔ ebibubu taʋa. 16Matrɔ si maaba di mlo xegi mɛɛtrɛ ní Makedonia ɛ, lɛ xegi madɔ ní Makedonia ɛ, maga mlɔ klɔ tɔ mlaakpɛ mɛ kude ní mɛ kudegaga trɛ ní Yudea mɛ. 17Gi mɛɛbitɛ batrɔtrɔ ba ya ʋa ɛ, mábitɛ lɛ ŋwisi liwaʋíboe kinɛ ɛ. Lɛ mábitɛ mɛ batrɔtrɔ ʋa ní kelea lia mɛ bapɛ́pɛ ʋa onu u, xe maatani kupani ní onugu tole ɔlɔ klɔ mɛ si Ee, Ee, loo Owo, owo o.
18Pɔ̀ kikakaa elue e, gi Aya onu anukwarɛ onete e, blɔ liboe le kí mlɔ línu Ee ni Owo. 19Lɛ ese linu si Aya Yebi ye Yesu Kristo, gi Sila, Timoteo ani mɛ kwisɔli ní mlɔ kusi i, ónu ɔnɔ gi onu “Ee” ani “Owo”, boŋu i, yɛ liboe le lipoe kakaa linu si “Ee.” 20Lɛ líbitɛ liboeboe xe Aya aka aplɛ tasɛ tasɛ ɛ, la petee enu “Ee” ní Kristo me. Lɛ loso e, gani Yesu Kristo me e, kuzeedo si “Liba lɛ mɛ” ki Aya yɛ kusudra a. 21Abla ɛ, Aya izɛki si blɔ ni mlɔ kuzɛɛlɛɛ gagla ní Kristo me. Yɛ asra kumu yɔ aki blɔ 22lɛ akpɛ blɔ lixaŋu nɛ si kunu yɛ banɛ ɛ, lɛ akɔ yɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ Dradra lɔ akpɛ ní blɔ ina nɛ mɛ ɛ, tɔ bize ŋwisi bidɔ gi bɛtsa ɛplɛ ní blɔ litukpo o, gi biitrɔ likla ni kidiliɛ gi kitraba ɛ, aʋa.
23Maxwa Aya si yɛ ɔŋa katsalia ɔki mɛ si médzi aba Korinto tɔ ɔ, atɔ mibu mlo ní kunugu me. 24Aní kipɛ́ kwipɛ si kutrɔ ɛki mlɔ kidiliɛ gi kɛ aʋa mlaziŋa; pɔ̀ kwiwa axwɛ na ni mlɔ kí mlɔ isami i, lɛ ese linu si gani kuziŋaa mɛ mlalɛɛ gagla a.

english_text:
1I have decided not to make my next visit with you so painful. 2If I make you feel bad, who would be left to cheer me up, except the people I had made to feel bad? 3The reason I want to be happy is to make you happy. I wrote as I did because I didn't want to visit you and be made to feel bad, when you should make me feel happy. 4At the time I wrote, I was suffering terribly. My eyes were full of tears, and my heart was broken. But I didn't want to make you feel bad. I only wanted to let you know how much I cared for you.
Forgiveness
5I don't want to be hard on you. But if one of you has made someone feel bad, I am not really the one who has been made to feel bad. Some of you are the ones. 6Most of you have already pointed out the wrong that person did, and this is punishment enough for what was done.
7When people sin, you should forgive and comfort them, so they won't give up in despair. 8You should make them sure of your love for them.
9I also wrote because I wanted to test you and find out if you would follow my instructions. 10I will forgive anyone you forgive. Yes, for your sake and with Christ as my witness, I have forgiven whatever needed to be forgiven. 11I have done this to keep Satan from getting the better of us. We all know what goes on in his mind.
12 When I went to Troas to preach the good news about Christ, I found that the Lord had already prepared the way. 13But I was worried when I didn't find my friend Titus there. So I left the other followers and went on to Macedonia.
14I am grateful that God always makes it possible for Christ to lead us to victory. God also helps us spread the knowledge about Christ everywhere, and this knowledge is like the smell of perfume. 15-16In fact, God thinks of us as a perfume that brings Christ to everyone. For people who are being saved, this perfume has a sweet smell and leads them to a better life. But for people who are lost, it has a bad smell and leads them to a horrible death.
No one really has what it takes to do this work. 17A lot of people try to get rich from preaching God's message. But we are God's sincere messengers, and by the power of Christ we speak our message with God as our witness.
translation_text:
1Lɛ loso matrɔ lɛ si mátraba ɛkpɛ mlɔ ɔnatata tɔ ɔ. 2Lɛ ese linu si xegi makpɛ mlɔ kuyeʋiyeʋi i, nyaŋwɛ aagɔ gi yɛ aakpɛ mɛ isami i, sani mlɔ banɔaklɔ ke gi mlɔ maki si mlenu kuyeʋiyeʋi yo e? 3Lɛ loso maŋwlimi keplekpaa kiliɛ gi maŋwlimi ka ɛ, tɔ xegi maba ɛ, lítraze ɔna nyipu iiki mɛ ɛ, ní mlɔ banama gi lilɛ si mlakpɛ mɛ isami nɛ ɛ, mlɔ aŋu mɛ tɔɔ. Lɛ maka ní mlɔ petee aʋa si xegi mɛɛmɔ isami nɛ ɛ, kɔlɔ mlɔ petee fɛ, isami nɛ iaku mlo. 4Lɛ ese linu si maŋwlimi aki mlɔ ni ɔna nyipu ni ɔnatata ani kube; aní mɛ kutrɔtrɔ linu si mikpɛ mlɔ asigaga a, pɔ̀ tɔ mibu mɛ kunɔlɔɔ kɔ kidzudzu ye me basɛ mlɔ ɔ.
Paulo abasɛ bidɔ ní kiliɛ gi biakɔ eboe zimizi na kɔkyɛ su u
5Abla xe ɔnutɔ aki si ɔnutɔ ɔmɔ ɔnatata ɛ, ɔ́bitɛ lɛ mani mɛ tete su u, pɔ̀ abitɛ lɛ amani mlɔ petee su u, lɛ ní kude kutɔ onu i, mápɛ si mibitɛ lɛ si linyani isanɔ ɔ. 6Lisrɛ si mlɔ banima kapɛ ʋidi a mlehe kutu kí oniliɛ klɔ. 7Boŋu i, lilɛ si mlakɔ akyɛ yɛ tɔ mlayɔ yɛ ɔna, atɔ ɔki wɔ ní asigaga la mɛ ɛ. 8Lɛ loso e, maki mlɔ litukpo si mlatrɔ likla ní lɛ aʋa aki yɛ si mlɛlɔ yɛ. 9Kidiliɛ gi kɛ loso maŋwlimi keplekpaa lia aki mlɔ ɛ, kɛ ese kinu si, mapɛ si mite xe mlɛtani kulɛɛ ní kufekedi uliɔ ya mɛ ɛ, ani xe mlenu kutu ní liboe kakaa mɛ ɛ. 10Xegi mlɛkɔ ɔnutɔ yɛ eboe zimizi na ɛkyɛ yɛ ɛ, mɛ fɛ makɔ la kukyɛ yɛ. Lɛ kidiliɛ gi makɔ akyɛ ɛ, xe kiditɔ kilɛ gi maakɔ kyɛ ɛ, makɔ kɛ akyɛ ní Kristo aŋu mɛ kɔkpɛ mlɔ loso, 11atɔ mitsi kude kí Ozimizi ye tɔ ɔkiŋa blɔ aʋa a. Lɛ ese linu si kwite yɛ batrɔtrɔ ʋa a.
Paulo nì Kunyanya Vuvu Yɔ ní Troa
12Agi mazeku Troa si mipani Liboe Gbã nɛ ní Kristo su i, mamɔ si Ɔkaɛ adra kudeonu akí axwɛ na ní klɔ. 13Pɔ̀ mɛ begyodi ʋa mɛ bádɔ ɔ, lɛ ese linu si mámɔ mɛ onemi ye Tito ní klɔ ɔ. Lɛ loso maklá banima ní iklɔ lɛ maza atrɛ ní Makedonia ɛ.
14Pɔ̀ kwido Aya ɖase e! Gi yɛ izɛgbani blɔ lipoe kakaa a, ní ɔga nɔ mɛ ní Kristo yɛ kuʋaŋaa kukli yɔ mɛ ɛ. Lɛ gani blɔ aʋa ɛ, Aya izeehle Kristo yɛ kisuwa flafla kɛ gi kinu yɛ kuteteo ní ɔkɔ kakaa a. 15Lɛ kulɛ ki Aya ŋwisi kidɔ gi kiinyɔfu flaflafla a, gi kinu Kristo yɛ kisuwaɛ kí banama gi ba beebu i, ni banama gi ba bɛɛkyrɔ ɛ. 16Kí ɔnutɔ ɛ, kunu ɔfu zimizi gi aaki kutse e; pɔ̀ ki ɔnɔ dzedze e, kunu ɔfu flafla gi aaki ligbã a. Lɛ nyaŋwɛ aatani lixwɛ nɛ ya onu kulɛ ɛ? 17Kúma ŋwisi banɔ glegle dzedze ba ɛ, kuʋída Aya yɛ liboe le ki kibɔ ɔ, boŋu i, ní Kristo me e, kuzɛɛpani Aya yɛ liboe le ní Aya aŋu mɛ ni anukwarɛɛ ŋwisi banɔ gi bɛkpɛ dɔni Aya kɔ kinɛ ɛ.

english_text:
God's New Agreement
1Are we once again bragging about ourselves? Do we need letters to you or from you to tell others about us? Some people do need letters telling about them. 2But you are our letter, and you are in our hearts for everyone to read and understand. 3 You are like a letter written by Christ and delivered by us. But you are not written with pen and ink or on tablets made of stone. You are written in our hearts by the Spirit of the living God.
4We are sure about all this. Christ makes us sure in the very presence of God. 5We don't have the right to claim that we have done anything on our own. God gives us what it takes to do all we do. 6 He makes us worthy to be the servants of his new agreement that comes from the Holy Spirit and not from a written Law. After all, the Law brings death, but the Spirit brings life.
7 The Law of Moses brought only the promise of death, even though it was carved on stones and given in a wonderful way. Still the Law made Moses' face shine so brightly the people of Israel could not look at it, even though it was a fading glory. 8So won't the agreement the Spirit brings to us be even more wonderful? 9If something that brings the death sentence is glorious, won't something that makes us acceptable to God be even more glorious? 10In fact, the new agreement is so wonderful that the Law is no longer glorious at all. 11The Law was given with a glory that faded away. But the glory of the new agreement is much greater, because it will never fade away.
12This wonderful hope makes us feel like speaking freely. 13 We are not like Moses. His face was shining, but he covered it to keep the people of Israel from seeing the brightness fade away. 14The people were stubborn, and something still keeps them from seeing the truth when the Law is read. Only Christ can take away the covering that keeps them from seeing.
15When the Law of Moses is read, they have their minds covered over 16 with a covering that is removed only for those who turn to the Lord. 17The Lord and the Spirit are one and the same, and the Lord's Spirit sets us free. 18 So our faces are not covered. They show the bright glory of the Lord, as the Lord's Spirit makes us more and more like our glorious Lord.
translation_text:
1Kɔ isu kwikpese kukpɔ̀ dzɛ a? Loo ŋwisi banɔ dzedzeatɔ kinɛ ɛ, kizia kwizia kuplekpa gi kwipani ní blɔ su kí mlɔ ɔ, loo dɔni mlɔ klɔ ki banɔ dzedze ba na a? 2Mlɔ ɔtutɔ mlenu blɔ keplekpaa gi bɛŋwlimi ní blɔ ina nɛ mɛ ɛ, gi ɔnɔ kakaa ete e, xe ɔnɔ kakaa aatani kukla a. 3Lɛ mlɛbasɛ si mlenu keplekpa gi kɛdɔ ní Kristo klɔ ɔ, libii lɛ gi lido ɛdɔni blɔ axwɛwawa la mɛ ɛ, gi aní kiŋwlimiseʋi i, gi biatani kutsoli bɛkɔ ɛ ŋwlimi ka a, pɔ̀ Aya gbagbe yɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ ɔ, lɛ báŋwlimi ka atrɔ ní akla aʋa a, pɔ̀ bɛŋwlimi ka ɛtrɔ ní banɔ gbagbe ba ba ina nɛ aʋa a.
4Lɛ ya linu ɔna gi ɔlɛ ní blɔ lipo ní Kristo me ní Aya aŋu mɛ ɛ. 5Aní si kwiatani kiditɔ kubitɛ ní blɔ ɔtutɔ isuwla mɛ ɛ, boŋu i, kidɔ kakaa gi kuzɛɛbitɛ ɛ, kidɔ ní Aya klɔ. 6Yɛ aki blɔ blɔ kutanɔɔ tɔ kuwa yɛ axwɛ na ŋwisi yɛ banɔsumu kinɛ ɛ, ní kunyánya vuvu yɔ mɛ. Lɛ kunyanya vuvu liɔ ya ɛ, aní ní kɔ kuŋwlimi yɔ onu u, pɔ̀ ní kɔ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ mɛ onu u; lɛ ese linu si kuŋwlimi yɔ kuzɛɛmani kutse, pɔ̀ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ izɛmani ligbã́ nɛ ɛ.
Kunyanya vuvu yɔ kɔ Kudradraa
7Abla xegi Kutseo kɔ axwɛ ana gi bɛwa ŋwisi ye banɔsumu kinɛ ɛ, gi kɔ isã nɛ bɛŋwlimi trɔ ní akla na aʋa, kwimani kutse e, gi nya kɔ kudradra kwiki si Mose yɛ aŋu na mɛ ezeekle e, xe Israɛl bebi ba bázɛtani yɛ aŋu na mɛ kudi i, kɔkpɛ la kuklekleo gi nya kwiklo kyɛ xe e, 8kɔlɔ kudradra ʋidi kwiaze ní Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ axwɛwawa la mɛ sanɔ ɔ! 9Xegi kusumu liɔ gi kuzebu banɔ lipɔ su kwidra ɛ, kitɛ xe kusumu uliɔ gi kuzɛɛki banɔ su izɛdra kutrámani kudradra ɛsanɔ ɔ? 10Lɛ ese linu si kidiliɛ gi kɛsu kidra ɛzanɔ ɛ, kɛsu kídra tɔ ɔ, xegi kwikɔ kɛ srɔni kudradra ʋidi uliɔ gi kulɛ abla a. 11Lɛ xegi kidiliɛ gi kiikpã kiba ni kudradra ɛ, kitɛ kuklekleo kiaze ki kidiliɛ gi kilɛɛ tsinɔ ɔ?
12Lɛ loso gi kudeonudidi uliɔ kuli ní blɔ awla ɛ, ɔna ɔlɛ ní blɔ lipo. 13Kúma ŋwisi Mose kiniɛ, gi akɔ kavlɔʋi ewe yɛ aŋu na mɛ ɛ, tɔ Israel banɛ ma bamɔni kuklekleo kudi kɔkpɛ lipoe lɛ gi kwiakpã ɛ. 14Pɔ̀ ba begyodi ʋa mɛ bádra a, lɛ ese linu si gakɔkpɛ ni omono e, bewe bidɔ bɛklɔ xegi bɛkla kunyanya kokoo e. Báti kɔ azanɔ ɔ, lɛ ese linu si gani Kristo aʋa ko biati kɔ. 15Kɔna ni omono kruaa kyɛ ɛ, lipoe kakaa gi bɛkla Mose kuplekpaa ɛ, kavlɔʋia keedziwe ba ina nɛ dzɛ. 16Pɔ̀ xe ɔnutɔ ekyi evu Ɔkaɛ ɛ, kɔ bɛti kavlɔʋia ɛ. 17Abla ɛ, Ɔkaɛ yɛ onu Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ, lɛ niklɔ gi Ɔkaɛ yɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ ɔlɛ ɛ, niklɔ ɔwlamɔmɔ nɔ ɔlɛ. 18Blɔ banɔaya gi bɛti kavlɔʋia ní blɔ aŋu na mɛ ɛ, kwiimɔ xe kwibu Ɔkaɛ yɛ kusudraa ɛbasɛ ɛ, beekyi blo tɔ kudzi ŋwisi yɛ gi bɛki blɔ kusudra a, gi kwikeme sanɔ ɔ, gi kwidɔ ni Ɔkaɛ ɛ, gi onu Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ klɔ ɔ.

english_text:
Treasure in Clay Jars
1God has been kind enough to trust us with this work. This is why we never give up. 2We don't do shameful things that must be kept secret. And we don't try to fool anyone or twist God's message around. God is our witness that we speak only the truth, so others will be sure we can be trusted. 3If there is anything hidden about our message, it is hidden only to someone who is lost.
4The god who rules this world has blinded the minds of unbelievers. They cannot see the light, which is the good news about our glorious Christ, who shows what God is like. 5We are not preaching about ourselves. Our message is that Jesus Christ is Lord. He also sent us to be your servants. 6 The Scriptures say, “God commanded light to shine in the dark.” Now God is shining in our hearts to let you know that his glory is seen in Jesus Christ.
7We are like clay jars in which this treasure is stored. The real power comes from God and not from us. 8We often suffer, but we are never crushed. Even when we don't know what to do, we never give up. 9In times of trouble, God is with us, and when we are knocked down, we get up again. 10-11We face death every day because of Jesus. Our bodies show what his death was like, so his life can also be seen in us. 12This means that death is working in us, but life is working in you.
13In the Scriptures it says, “I spoke because I had faith.” We have this same kind of faith. So we speak 14because we know that God raised the Lord Jesus to life. And just as God raised Jesus, he will also raise us to life. Then he will bring us into his presence together with you. 15All of this has been done for you, so more and more people will know how kind God is and will praise and honor him.
Faith in the Lord
16We never give up. Our bodies are gradually dying, but we ourselves are being made stronger each day. 17These little troubles are getting us ready for an eternal glory that will make all our troubles seem like nothing. 18Things that are seen don't last forever, but things that are not seen are eternal. This is why we keep our minds on the things that cannot be seen.
translation_text:
Kunu Exo ní Sise Kufukpo me
1Lɛ loso gi gani Aya yɛ asigagadidi la mɛ ɛ, xe aki blɔ axwɛ ana ya si kuwa ɛ, blɔ ɔna ɔ́tragbɔ̀ ɔ. 2Boŋu i, kwibu eyekuboe la ni eboe kpokpo la ɛzanɔ, kuʋímeni banɔ ɔ, lɛ kuʋíkyi Aya yɛ liboe le kyɛ ɛ. Boŋu i, gi kwiibu anukwarɛɛ basɛ wee e, kwiikpɔ isu ya ɛki ɔnɔ kakaa si biate blo ní ba ina nɛ mɛ ní Aya yɛ aŋu mɛ. 3Lɛ xe liboe gbã nɛ gi kwiipani i, xe lɛ mɛ lídra kyɛ ɛ, banama gi bɛɛkyrɔ ɛ, ba ko lɛ mɛ lídra ɛkɔ ɔ. 4Ozimizi ye, kesea ʋa ya ka aya yɛ akpɛ banama gi báziŋa ɛ, ba begyodi ʋa ní litumu me. Lɛ loso baʋímɔ Liboe gbã nɛ ɛ, lɛ kusudraa kɔ kuklekleo ní Kristo su gi yɛ onu Aya yɛ kizeze me e. 5Lɛ ese linu si aní blɔ ɔtutɔ isu ya kwisɔli i; kwisɔli Yesu Kristo si onu Ɔka, lɛ blɔ ɔtutɔ lɔ kɔ ɛ, kunu mlɔ banɔsumu kɔkpɛ Yesu loso. 6Lɛ ese linu si Aya gi yɛ apani si i, “Kuklekle kudo dɔni litumu ne me e,” yɛ aki si yɛ kuklekleo kukle ní blɔ ina nɛ mɛ. Atɔ kuki si kute Aya yɛ kusudraa kɔ kuklekleo ní Kristo yɛ aŋu na mɛ ɛ.
7Pɔ̀ exo ala alɛ ní blɔ awla ní sise kufukpo me e, tɔ libasɛ si kigagla ʋidi liɛ kidɔ ní Aya kɔ lɛ aní blɔ kɔ ɔ. 8Baxixa ʋa bali ní blɔ su ní kapɛ kakaa mɛ, pɔ̀ baʋíŋa blɔ aʋa a, lɛ kuzɛɛpisa pɔ̀ kyɛ ɛ, blɔ ɔna ɔʋígbɔ̀ ɔ. 9Banima bizɛyɔ kedu ní blɔ su pɔ̀ Aya ɔ́yurɔ blɔ ɔ; bizɛpɛ si beye blo pɔ̀ kuʋítse e. 10Yesu yɛ kutseo kuli ní blɔ kisugyaɛ mɛ lipoe kakaa tɔ yɛ ligbã́ nɛ liaŋwi ní blɔ kisugyaɛ mɛ. 11Pɔ̀ blɔ gi kulɛ ligbã́ nɛ ɛ, kulɛ ní kuxixa mɛ lipoe kakaa kɔkpɛ Yesu loso, atɔ biamɔ yɛ ligbã́ nɛ ní blɔ kisugyaɛ liɛ gi kizetse e, me. 12Libasɛ si kutseo kwiiwa axwɛ na ní blɔ mɛ, pɔ̀ ligbã́ nɛ liiwa axwɛ na ní mlɔ mɛ ɛ.
13Bɛŋwlimi kpɛ ní kuŋwlimi dradraa mɛ si, “Mazi lɛ aʋa aŋa lɛ loso Mapani.” Ni kuziŋa Ɔfuɛfuɛ tole ɔlɔ klɔ ʋa ɛ, blɔ fɛ kwiziŋa lɛ loso kwiipani; 14lɛ ese linu si kwite si Aya, gi yɛ ayɔni Yesu Kristo adɔ ni bekpo ʋa mɛ ɛ, aayɔ ni blɔ fɛ kɔpɔni Yesu lɛ aakɔ̀ blɔ pɔni mlɔ, manɔ ní yɛ aŋu mɛ. 15Eboa la petee gi ɛɛba blɔ aʋa ɛ, alɛ aki mlɔ libibubu; lɛ loso Aya yɛ lipomepɛpɛ nɛ gi liina banima ní kisrɛsrɛ mɛ ɛ, liaki ɖasedodo gi aagbigba ki Aya yɛ kusudraa a.
16Lɛ loso e, ɔna oʋído ní blɔ lipo o. Linya ɛli ní lɛ mɛ si blɔ inɔse le iiblo, pɔ̀ ní Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ mɛ ɛ, beedzini blo kigagla ɛkpɛ liwe kakaa. 17Lɛ ese linu si blɔ baxixa sisami ʋa ya gi kwimɔ abla ɛ, bɛtráze lipoe kpekpemi itɔ ko, lɛ bɛɛki si kwiimɔ kusudra gi kɔ onu kutraza liwewe e, gi kwipɛ sani baxixa ya ʋa petee e. 18Lɛ loso kúkɔ blɔ aŋu na atrɔ ní kudɔkpaa liɔ klɔ gi kuzɛɛmɔ aʋa a, pɔ̀ kwikɔ la ɛtrɔ ní kudɔkpaa liɔ klɔ gi kuʋímɔ ɛ aʋa. Lɛ ese linu si kidiliɛ gi bizɛɛmɔ ɛ, kizeeze lipoe kpekpemi itɔ ko, pɔ̀ kidiliɛ klɔ gi baʋímɔ ɛ, kɛ kizeeze kɔtsini kanɔmɛ anɔmɛ.

english_text:
1 Our bodies are like tents that we live in here on earth. But when these tents are destroyed, we know that God will give each of us a place to live. These homes will not be buildings someone has made, but they are in heaven and will last forever. 2While we are here on earth, we sigh because we want to live in that heavenly home. 3We want to put it on like clothes and not be naked.
4These tents we now live in are like a heavy burden, and we groan. But we don't do this just because we want to leave these bodies that will die. It is because we want to change them for bodies that will never die. 5God is the one who makes all this possible. He has given us his Spirit to make us certain he will do it. 6So always be cheerful!
As long as we are in these bodies, we are away from the Lord. 7But we live by faith, not by what we see. 8We should be cheerful, because we would rather leave these bodies and be at home with the Lord. 9But whether we are at home with the Lord or away from him, we still try our best to please him. 10 After all, Christ will judge each of us for the good or the bad that we do while living in these bodies.
Bringing People to God
11We know what it means to respect the Lord, and we encourage everyone to turn to him. God himself knows what we are like, and I hope you also know what kind of people we are. 12We are not trying once more to brag about ourselves. But we want you to be proud of us, when you are with those who are not sincere and brag about what others think of them.
13If we seem out of our minds, it is between God and us. But if we are in our right minds, it is for your good. 14We are ruled by Christ's love for us. We are certain that if one person died for everyone else, then all of us have died. 15And Christ did die for all of us. He died so we would no longer live for ourselves, but for the one who died and was raised to life for us.
16We are careful not to judge people by what they seem to be, though we once judged Christ in this way. 17Anyone who belongs to Christ is a new person. The past is forgotten, and everything is new. 18God has done it all! He sent Christ to make peace between himself and us, and he has given us the work of making peace between himself and others.
19What we mean is that God was in Christ, offering peace and forgiveness to the people of this world. And he has given us the work of sharing his message about peace. 20We were sent to speak for Christ, and God is begging you to listen to our message. We speak for Christ and sincerely ask you to make peace with God. 21Christ never sinned! But God treated him as a sinner, so Christ could make us acceptable to God.
translation_text:
1Abla kwite si kusaa akpandɔ liɛ mɛ gi kukpasi i, gi onu blɔ kesea aʋa ya kisugyaɛ ɛ; xegi ezizi i, ligba lilɛ ní blɔ awla dɔni Aya klɔ. Ligba gi liaze ní blɔ awla ní lifunu ne e, gi aní ɔnɔ awla bɛkɔ ɛwa lɛ ɛ. 2Pɔ̀ abla ɛ, kwigù hũu u, gi kwipɛ si kwiaze ní blɔ lifunu ne kisugyaɛ mɛ ɛ. 3lɛ ese linu si xegi kwikpɛ lifunu ne kisugyaɛ ɛ, bátramɔ blɔ si kwibu liya a. 4Lɛ ese linu si gi kukpasi ní kelea mɛ kusa akpandɔ liɛ ya mɛ ɛ, kwiitàni ní isu ya mɛ, lɛ lidɔtu ʋidi litini ní blɔ aʋa a, lɛ ese linu si kúpɛ si bebu blo liya a, pɔ̀ kwipɛ si kuze ní blɔ lifunu ne kisugyaɛ mɛ atɔ kidiliɛ gi kinu kidɔ tsetse e, biakyi ke tɔ kiadzi ligbã́. 5Abla ɛ, Aya yɛ abitɛ blɔ akí liboe lɛ ya, xe aki blɔ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ ŋwisi bidɔ gi bɛtsa ɛplɛ ɛ, gi kiitrɔ likla a ní kidiliɛ gi kitraba ba ɛ, aʋa.
6Lɛ loso e, ɔna ɔlɛ ní blɔ awla lipoe kakaa, lɛ kwite si gi kuli kepe ní kisugyaɛ mɛ ɛ, kuli ní ɔdzage ní blɔ Ɔkaɛ su. 7Kuli ligbã́ nɛ ní kuziŋa mɛ aní ní kidiliɛ gi kwimɔ onu u. 8Ɔna ɔlɛ ní blɔ lipo lɛ mɛɛpani, lɛ kwiapɛ si kwiayurɔ blɔ kisugyaɛ tɔ kwiaze kepea ni Ɔkaɛ. 9Lɛ loso e, kwitrɔ lɛ si kwiabitɛ kidɔ kakaa tɔ kwiaku yɛ aŋu, xegi kulí kepe ní kisugyaɛ mɛ ni ya a, loo ní ɔdzage ni kɛ su. 10Lɛ ese linu si lilɛ si blɔ petee kwialɛɛ ní Kristo yɛ litsixwagbo le ɔtɔ nɔ, tɔ ɔnɔ kakaa aazi kidiliɛ gi kinu si ɔzɛ ɛ, kɔkpɛ kidɔ kakaa gi abitɛ ɛ, lipoe lɛ gi eze ni kisugyaɛ mɛ ɛ, xegi kinu kigbã loo kinyaninyani i.
Mlaki Aya Ɔkpɛ Blɔ nì Yɛ Likpɔ
11Abla gi kwite kidiliɛ ɛ, gi linu tɔ sé Ɔkaɛ ɛ, lɛ loso kwikeni idi tɔ kuliɔ̀ʋi banima tɔ benu blɔ ese e. Aya ete kidiliɛ gi kunu i, lɛ medi kudeonu si lɛ mɛ lidra ɛki mlɔ ina nɛ fɛ ɛ. 12Kuʋí keni idi dzɛ si kukpɔ̀ isu iiki mlɔ ɔ; pɔ̀ kwikenidi tɔ kwiaki mlɔ kudeonu tɔ mladɔ isu ní blɔ aʋa a, atɔ mlatani lɛ onu kubu kí banama gi bizeefeke isu ya a, ní kidiliɛ gi banima bizɛɛmɔ aʋa a sani kidiliɛ gi kilɛ ní ɔna nɔ mɛ ɛ aʋa. 13Xegi kwiiwle e, Aya loso kwiiwle, pɔ̀ xegi kulɛ mumɔ ɛ, kulɛ mumɔ kɔkpɛ mlɔ loso. 14Lɛ ese linu si Kristo yɛ kunɔlɔɔ kɔ kwiŋa blɔ aʋa lɛ ese linu si lɛ mɛ lidra ɛki blɔ si ɔnɔ tole etse aki ɔnɔ kakaa lɛ loso e, ɔnɔ kakaa etse. 15Etse akí banima petee, tɔ banama gi bali ligbã́ nɛ ɛ, batráze ligbã́ nɛ ɛki isu ya tɔ ɔ, pɔ̀ biaze ligbã́ kí ɔniliɛ klɔ gi yɛ etse aki ba ɛ, xe bedzini yɛ yɔnɔ ɛ.
16Lɛ loso dɔni abla ɛ, kuʋímɔ ɔnɔnɔ dɔni kelea mɛ bede ʋa onu tɔ ɔ. Nya lipoe tɔ zanɔ ɛ, nite kwizɛmɔ Kristo pɔ̀ kuʋímɔ yɛ nite tɔ ɔ. 17Lɛ loso xe ɔnutɔ akpɛ likpɔ ni Kristo e, edzì ɔnɔ vuvu; bidɔ koko ʋe biza; bidɔ vuvu ʋɛ biba. 18La petee ɛdɔ Aya klɔ lɛ gani Kristo me e, akpɛ blɔ ni Yɛ likpɔ lɛ akpɛ lixwɛ ní blɔ awla si tɔ kukpɛ Yɛ ni banɔdzedze ba kyɛ likpɔ. 19Aya ɛɛkpɛ kelea mɛ ni Yɛ isu ya likpɔ gani Kristo aʋa; lɛ Aya ɔʋíbla banima ba eboe zimizi na ɛki ba a. Lɛ akɔ likpɔkpɛkpɛ lɛ odu lo akpɛ ní blɔ awla aki banima.
20Lɛ loso kulɛɛ ní Kristo linyi ne me ŋwisi Aya eeʋi gani blɔ aʋa kinɛ ɛ, kwiki mlɔ litukpo ní Kristo yɛ linyi ne me si: Mlaki tɔ bakpɛ mlɔ ni Aya likpɔ. 21Aya aki si Kristo gi eboe zimizi áma ní yɛ su i, odzi eboe zimizi kí blɔ tɔ gani yɛ aʋa ɛ, blɔ eboe aagyɔ ní Aya aŋu mɛ ɛ.

english_text:
1We work together with God, and we beg you to make good use of God's gift of undeserved grace. 2 In the Scriptures God says,
“When the time came,
I listened to you,
and when you needed help,
I came to save you.”
That time has come. This is the day for you to be saved.
3We don't want anyone to find fault with our work, and so we try hard not to cause problems. 4But in everything and in every way we show we truly are God's servants. We have always been patient, though we have had a lot of trouble, suffering, and hard times. 5 We have been beaten, put in jail, and hurt in riots. We have worked hard and have gone without sleep or food. 6But we have kept ourselves pure and have been understanding, patient, and kind. The Holy Spirit has been with us, and our love has been real. 7We have spoken the truth, and God's power has worked in us. In all our struggles we have said and done only what is right.
8Whether we were honored or dishonored or praised or cursed, we always told the truth about ourselves. But some people said we did not. 9We are unknown to others, but well known to you. We seem to be dying, and yet we are still alive. We have been punished, but never killed, 10and we are always happy, even in times of suffering. Although we are poor, we have made many people rich. And though we own nothing, everything is ours.
11Friends in Corinth, we are telling the truth when we say there is room in our hearts for you. 12We are not holding back on our love for you, but you are holding back on your love for us. 13I speak to you as I would speak to my own children. Please make room in your hearts for us.
The Temple of the Living God
14Stay away from people who are not followers of the Lord! Can someone who is good get along with someone who is evil? Are light and darkness the same? 15Is Christ a friend of Satan? Can people who follow the Lord have anything in common with those who don't? 16 Do idols belong in the temple of God? We are the temple of the living God, as God himself says,
“I will live with these people
and walk among them.
I will be their God,
and they will be
my people.”
17 The Lord also says,
“Leave them and stay away!
Don't touch anything
that isn't clean.
Then I will welcome you
18 and be your Father.
You will be my sons
and my daughters,
as surely as I am God,
the All-Powerful.”
translation_text:
1Ŋwisi Aya yɛ baxwɛwa glɔnɛ kinɛ ɛ, kwiki mlɔ litukpo si mlakíkɔ Aya yɛ lipomepɛpɛ nɛ gi mlɛzɛ ɛ, ŋwisi kidɔ gi kítrabu libi i. 2Lɛ ese linu si apani si,
“Gi lipoe le lina si mipɛ̀ lipo me ki mlɔ ɛ, mata mlɔ kutu u,
lɛ gi liwe lɛ aʋa a, gi lilɛ si mibu mlo lina ɛ, mapɔni mlɔ.”
Mɛsi mlɔ si abla Aya yɛ lipomepɛpɛ nɛ liwe le lina, abla yɛ kubúbú yo liwe le lina.
Paulo Yɛ Baxixa ʋa
3Kuʋíplɛ likla ni mlɔ kude me si mletu kikliʋi, tɔ biamɔ kunutsatsa tɔtɔ ní blɔ axwɛwawa la su. 4Pɔ̀ ŋwisi Aya yɛ banɔsumu kinɛ ɛ, kuzɛɛkpɔ̀ isu ya ní kude kakaa onu; ní igyidodo me; ní baxixa ʋa mɛ, ní kupepe ni ɔna kugbɔ̀gbɔ̀ mɛ ɛ. 5Kuzɛɛkpɔ̀ isu ní kukusì me, ligba kuzeze me, ní kedu kuyɔyɔ mɛ; ní axwɛ gagla la kuwawa mɛ, sramɔdɔmɔdɔ elesi ni kuʋɛ mɛ; 6kɔpɔnɔ ɛ, kuzɛɛbasɛ blɔ kusudraa ni eboe ese kununu yo, litɔplɛplɛkese kese ani asigagadidi; ní Ɔfuɛfuɛ Dradra lɔ mɛ, lɛ kuzɛɛbasɛ blɔ ɔna mɛ kunɔlɔ; 7ní anukwarɛ kudodo me, ni ní Aya yɛ kigaglaɛ mɛ ɛ, kwivu kude gyɔgyɔ kɔ bigutadɔ mɛ ní kaŋawla ni kaba mɛ. 8Lɛ gani kusudra ani kuyé me e, kunutsatsa nyani nyani ni kunutsatsa gbã mɛ ɛ; anukwarɛɛ benete pɔ̀ bɛxwa blɔ si basia nɛ ɛ; 9bɛbitɛ ŋwisi béte blo o, pɔ̀ ba pɔ́ bete blo o; bɛsi si kwitse, pɔ̀ mledi kuli ligbã́ nɛ ɛ; bekusi blo pɔ̀, baʋíye blo o. 10Kuli ní asigaga mɛ, pɔ̀ lipoe kakaa kuli ní isami mɛ ɛ; kwiku lipi le, pɔ̀ kwibitɛ banɔ glegle e, gi bedzi bexonete e; kwiŋwi ŋwisi kididɔ kíma ní blɔ awla a, pɔ̀ kidɔ kakaa kilɛ ní blɔ awla a.
11Kwipani iiki mlɔ ni ɔna faã Korintoanɛ ma, lɛ kwidra blɔ ina nɛ mɛ ɛki mlɔ. 12Kúkɔ blɔ kunɔlɔɔ kokpo mlo o, pɔ̀ mlɛ kɔ mlɔ kuni yɔ ɛkɔ ɛ kpo blo o. 13Ŋwisi kutsrɛtsrɛ gbã kinɛ ɛ; mɛɛpani ki mlɔ ŋwisi kí mɛ bebi kinɛ ɛ; lɛ loso e mlɔfɛ mladra mlɔ ina nɛ mɛ aki blɔ ŋwisi kiliɛ gi kwidra blɔ ini nɛ mɛ ɛki mlɔ kinɛ ɛ.
Mlakíkpɛ ɔha ni banama gi báziŋa a
14Mlakíkpɛ likpɔ ni banama gi báziŋa a. Lɛ ese linu si siŋanɛ woli lu si lɛ ní banama gi ba bede ʋa bɛgyɔ ɔ, ni banama gi benu litukpomegagla benete kusi i? loo ɔha kpɛkpɛ woli lu ɔlɛ ní kuklekle ni litumu ne kusi i? 15Likpɔkpɛkpɛ woli lu lilɛ ní Kristo ni Belial kusi i? Liboe woli lilɛ ní ɔʋaziŋaɛ ni ɔniliɛ gi ɔ́zɛ ŋa ɛ kusi i? 16Kunyánya woli lu kulɛ ní Aya yɛ lisɔligba lɛ ni babu ʋa kusi i? Lɛ ese linu si blɔ kunu Aya gbagbee yɛ lisɔligba lɛ. Ŋwisi kiliɛ gi Aya apani si i,
“Maaze nì ba
lɛ maaga ní ba kusi,
lɛ maaze bá Aya,
lɛ biaaze mɛ banɔ.”
17“Lɛ loso e, mledò ní ba kusi
tɔ mlebú lidze,
nite Ɔkaɛ apani.
Mlakígbɔni bidɔ gi bɛsu bídra a,
atɔ maazi mlɔ.”
18“Maaze Ɔka ki mlɔ,
lɛ mlaaze mɛ bebinyime ni mɛ bebidzɛ,
nite kigaglaɛ petee Ɔkaɛ Aya apani.”

english_text:
1My friends, God has made us these promises. So we should stay away from everything that keeps our bodies and spirits from being clean. We should honor God and try to be completely like him.
The Church Makes Paul Happy
2Make a place for us in your hearts! We haven't mistreated or hurt anyone. We haven't cheated anyone. 3I am not saying this to be hard on you. But, as I have said before, you will always be in our thoughts, whether we live or die. 4I trust you completely. I am always proud of you, and I am greatly encouraged. In all my trouble I am still very happy.
5 After we came to Macedonia, we didn't have any chance to rest. We were faced with all kinds of problems. We were troubled by enemies and troubled by fears. 6But God cheers up people in need, and this is what he did when he sent Titus to us. 7Of course, we were glad to see Titus, but what really made us glad is the way you cheered him up. He told how sorry you were and how concerned you were about me. And this made me even happier.
8I don't feel bad anymore, even though my letter hurt your feelings. I did feel bad at first, but I don't now. I know that the letter hurt you for a while. 9Now I am happy, but not because I hurt your feelings. It is because God used your hurt feelings to make you turn back to him, and none of you were harmed by us. 10When God makes you feel sorry enough to turn to him and be saved, you don't have anything to feel bad about. But when this world makes you feel sorry, it can cause your death.
11Just look what God has done by making you feel sorry! You sincerely want to prove you are innocent. You are angry. You are shocked. You are eager to see that justice is done. You have proved that you were completely right in this matter. 12When I wrote you, it wasn't to accuse the one who was wrong or to take up for the one who was hurt. I wrote, so God would show you how much you do care for us. 13And we were greatly encouraged.
Although we were encouraged, we felt even better when we saw how happy Titus was, because you had shown he had nothing to worry about. 14We had told him how much we thought of you, and you did not disappoint us. Just as we have always told you the truth, so everything we told him about you has also proved to be true. 15Titus loves all of you very much, especially when he remembers how you obeyed him and how you trembled with fear when you welcomed him. 16It makes me really glad to know I can depend on you.
translation_text:
1Agi eboa la ya a gi Aya aka aplɛ aki blɔ alɛ ní blɔ awla ɛ, mɛ baʋia dimɛnɔ ba, mlaki tɔ kudra blɔ isu ya su dɔni kidɔ kakaa gi kialulu blɔ isu ya a, ni blɔ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ mɛ mɛ̀. Atɔ mlaki tɔ blɔ kudradraa kuna ki Aya yɛ kubùbù nì yɛ kusese.
Paulo Yɛ Isami nɛ
2Mlaki tɔ blɔ kyɛ kumɔ ozekɔ ní mlɔ ina nɛ mɛ. Kuʋíta ɔnɔnɔ ese e, kuʋíkyi ɔnɔnɔ ɛxla a, kuʋítafa ɔnɔnɔ ɔ. 3Maʋípani nite tɔ mikɔ ebú mlɔ lipɔ ɔ; mapani azanɔ si mleŋa kedzi ní blɔ ina nɛ mɛ si kwiaze ligba loo kwiatse ni mlo. 4Maka ní mlɔ aʋa mɔmɔmɔ; mizɛɛdɔ isu ní mlɔ aʋa. Mlɛkpɛ mɛ kigagla mɔmɔmɔ; lɛ ní blɔ baxixa ʋa petee me e, odukɔ ɔma ɔki mɛ isami nɛ ɛ.
5Lɛ gi kwiba Makedonia ɛ, blɔ kisugyaɛ liɛ ya kímɔ kusuyɔ; bɛzɛkpɛ blɔ kunugu yo ní kapɛ kakaa mɛ, gani kuboehe ní oge no, lifu ne ní kimɛ mɛ. 6Pɔ̀ Aya gi yɛ izɛɛyɔ ɔna ɛki banama a, gi ba ɔna ɛgbɔ ɛ, ayɔ blɔ ɔna gi Titus aba blɔ klɔ ɛ, 7lɛ aní yɛ kubaba tètè e, pɔ̀ ɔnayɔyɔ lɔ gi mlɛki yɛ ɛ. Asi blɔ kiliɛ gi mlɛpɛ si mlɛmɔ mɛ ɛ, mlɔ ɔna tata nɔ, kiliɛ gi mlɛkɔ lɛ mɛ ɛki mɛ ɛ, gi biki si mɛ isami nɛ isrɛ sanɔ ɔ.
8Lɛ xegi mɛ keplekpaa kákpɛ mlɔ isami kyɛ ɛ, litráve me e. Nya live me lipoe lɛ klɔ ɔkpɛ gi mamɔ si mɛ keplekpaa kákpɛ mlɔ isami kɔ ki lipoe kpekpemi itɔ fɛ ɛ. 9Pɔ̀ abla ɛ, isami iiku me, ani gi keplekpaa kákpɛ mlɔ isami loso o, pɔ̀ mlɔ asigaga la ɛki si mlekyi ɔna mɛ ɛ. Lɛ ese linu si mledzi basigaga nɛ kiliɛ gi Aya apɛ ɛ, lɛ loso e, ɔnɔnɔ ɔ́kpɛ mlɔ libi ní kude kutɔtɔ onu gani blɔ aʋa a. 10Xegi Aya aki si mleku ní asigaga mɛ ɛ, lɛ lizɛɛmani ɔna mɛ kukyikyi i, gi kɔ kuzɛɛki blɔ kubúbú yo o, xe kuʋímani “atɛsi metete” e, pɔ̀ kelea mɛ asigaga la ɛ, la izɛɛmani kutse. 11Mledi kidiliɛ gi asigaga la ya ní Aya yɛ aŋu mɛ ɛkpɛ ní mlɔ mɛ ɛ: kiliɛ gi mlɛzɛɛdu aŋu na a, kiliɛ gi mlɛzɛpɛ veve si mlɔ linɛ ligyɔ ɔ, kiliɛ gi mlezeyeʋi isu u, xegi mlemɔ liboe tɔ ɔ, gi liʋigyɔ ɛ, kiliɛ gi lifu ne lizeeku mlo o, xegi liboe zimizi litɔ liba mlɔ aʋa a, kiliɛ gi mlɛzɛpɛ si mlɛmɔ mɛ ɛ, kiliɛ gi mlɛzɛkɔ litɔ ní lɛ mɛ ɛ, kiliɛ gi mlalɛ ní kublabla mɛ si mlɛmɔ si bɛbitɛ eboe gbã a. Ni lipoe kakaa mɛ ɛ, mlebu isu ya ɛbasɛ si mláŋa lipɔ ní liboe lɛ ya mɛ ɛ. 12Loso agi maŋwlimi akí mlɔ ɛ, ani ɔniliɛ klɔ gi abitɛ liboe zimizi i, loo ɔniliɛ gi yɛ su bɛbitɛ liboe zimizi i manɔ, pɔ̀ ní Aya aŋu mɛ ɛ, mlɔ ɔtutɔ mlaamɔ kiliɛ gi mlɛkɔ isu ya ɛki blɔ ɛ. 13Gani lɛ petee me e, bɛkpɛ blɔ kigagla.
Kɔpɔni blɔ ɔtutɔ blɔ ɔnadɔdɔ lɔ ɛ, lize blɔ isami koŋ si kwimɔ Titus yɛ isami nɛ, lɛ ese linu si mlɔ petee mlɛkpɛ yɛ ɔna nɔ isami. 14Madɔ isu aki yɛ ní mlɔ su, lɛ mlákpɛ mɛ kuye e. Pɔ̀ lɛ kiliɛ gi liboe kakaaɛ lɛ ɛ, gi kwipani si mlɔ lilɛ ní lɛ mɛ ɛ, niteme ke blɔ kusudɔɔ ní mlɔ su kí Tito fɛ ɛ, kɔ kyɛ kunu anukwarɛ. 15Lɛ yɛ kudimɛnɔɔ ki mlɔ ɛ, kwikeme pɔnɔ xegi aplɛ aŋu si mlɔ petee mlenu kutu u, lɛ mlɛzi yɛ ni lifu ani kusugu u. 16Isami iku me si maaka ni mlɔ petee aʋa.

english_text:
Generous Giving
1 My friends, we want you to know that the churches in Macedonia have shown others God's gift of undeserved grace. 2Although they were going through hard times and were very poor, they were glad to give generously. 3They gave as much as they could afford and even more, simply because they wanted to. 4They even asked and begged us to let them have the joy of giving their money for God's people. 5And they did more than we had hoped. They gave themselves first to the Lord and then to us, just as God wanted them to do.
6Titus was the one who got you started doing this good thing, so we begged him to help you finish what you had begun. 7You do everything better than anyone else. You have stronger faith. You speak better and know more. You are eager to give, and you love us better. Now you must give more generously than anyone else.
8I am not ordering you to do this. I am simply testing how real your love is by comparing it with the concern that others have shown. 9You know our Lord Jesus Christ treated us with undeserved grace by giving up all his riches, so you could become rich.
10A year ago you were the first ones to give, and you gave because you wanted to. So listen to my advice. 11I think you should finish what you started. If you give according to what you have, you will prove you are as eager to give as you were to think about giving. 12It doesn't matter how much you have. What matters is how much you are willing to give from what you have.
13I am not trying to make life easier for others by making life harder for you. But it is only fair 14for you to share with them when you have so much, and they have so little. Later, when they have more than enough, and you are in need, they can share with you. Then everyone will have a fair share, 15 just as the Scriptures say,
“Those who gathered
too much
had nothing left.
Those who gathered
only a little
had all they needed.”
Titus and His Friends
16I am grateful that God made Titus care as much about you as we do. 17When we begged Titus to visit you, he said he would. He wanted to because he cared so much for you. 18With Titus we are also sending one of the Lord's followers who is well known in every church for spreading the good news. 19The churches chose this follower to travel with us while we carry this gift that will bring praise to the Lord and show how much we hope to help. 20We don't want anyone to find fault with the way we handle your generous gift. 21 But we want to do what pleases the Lord and what people think is right.
22We are also sending someone else with Titus and the other follower. We approve of this man. In fact, he has already shown us many times that he wants to help. And now he wants to help even more than ever, because he trusts you so much. 23Titus is my partner, who works with me to serve you. The other two followers are sent by the churches, and they bring honor to Christ. 24Treat them in such a way that the churches will see your love and will know why we bragged about you.
translation_text:
Paulo Akpɛ Ɔna mɛ Bidɔkɔkɔ Faã Kigagla
1Benemi ba, kwipɛ si mlete lipomepɛpɛɛ nɛ gi Aya akí Makedonia isɔliha lɛ ɛ. 2Dɔni kukenidi gagla mɛ ɛ, ba isami nɛ ni ba ɔzia nɔ bigbigba, gi bidzi bidɔkɔkɔ faã a, gi biku kumu yɔ ɔ. 3Lɛ mɛɛŋa katsalia ɛki ba si, bɛki bidɔ kiliɛ onu gi biatanɔ, sani ba kutanɔ kyɛ. Ní ba ɔtutɔ ba isuwla mɛ ɛ, 4beʋi dɔni blɔ iwla mɛ veve si kuki ba kudeonu tɔ ba kyɛ bamɔ ɔwakɔ ní lixwɛ nɛ ya mɛ ki banɔ dradra ba. 5Lɛ bábitɛ kiliɛ gi kwidi kudeonu ikɔ ɔ, pɔ̀ ní ɔtɔpu kɔ ɛ, bɛkɔ ba isu ya ɛki Ɔkaɛ, lɛ bɛkɔ isu ya ɛki blɔ ní Aya yɛ kupɛpɛɔ onu. 6Lɛ loso kwikpɛ Tito kigagla gi ekpese lixwɛ nɛ ya kooko e, tɔ aablo ni lipomepɛpɛ nɛ lixwɛ nɛ ya kyɛ ní mlɔ kapɛa mɛ ɛ. 7Pɔ̀ kiliɛ gi mlɛbitɛ sanɔ ní kidɔ kakaa mɛ ɛ, ní kuziŋaa mɛ, ní kupaniɔ mɛ, ní kuboeteo mɛ, ní anukwarɛ zizi mɛ ani mlɔ kunɔlɔɔ kí blɔ mɛ ɛ, mledi le ɔza si mlɔ fɛ mlɛbitɛ sanɔ ní lipomepɛpɛ nɛ bidɔkɔkɔɛ ʋɛ ya mɛ.
8Aní ɔsã mɛɛkpɛ ki mlɔ, pɔ̀ mapɛ si mífeke mlɔ kunɔlɔɔ kɔ anukwarɛ edi i, tɔ míkɔ kɔ ɛsrɔni banɔ gɔgɔ ba ba anukwarɛɛ. 9Lɛ ese linu si mlete blɔ Ɔkaɛ Yesu Kristo yɛ lipomepɛpɛ nɛ gi nya eze oxonete xe e, pɔ̀ kɔkpɛ mlɔ loso e, edzì opiplinete tɔ mlɔ kyɛ gani yɛ lipi le me e, mladzi bexonete.
10Lɛ mɛ kugbagba ki mlɔ ní kidiliɛ gi kisapɛ̀ ní liboe lɛ ya mɛ kinu si i, galigɔ lɛ ɛ, mlɔ mleeze banɔ tɔpu ba aní ní bidɔkɔkɔ ʋɛ mɛ tètè pɔ̀ ní kupɛ́pɛ si mlaabitɛ nite me kyɛ ɛ. 11Abla ɛ, mlebloni lixwɛ nɛ, atɔ la kublono e, kwiaze ŋwisi kiliɛ gi mlɛpɛ si mlɛbitɛ lɛ kinɛ ɛ, ní kiliɛ gi mlaatanɔ onu u. 12Lɛ xe kupɛ́pɛɔ kulɛ́ ɛ, biaazɛ mlɔ bidɔkɔkɔ ʋɛ ɛ, ní kidiliɛ gi kilɛ ni ɔnɔ kakaa awla ɛ onu, aní ní kidiliɛ gi kíma ní yɛ awla onu u.
13Aní blɔ kupɛ́pɛ linu si banɔatɔ bamɔ ɔnadɔdɔ tɔ mlɔ pɔ̀ bemi mlo akpɛ ɛ, boŋu i, tɔ mlɔ pɔ́ mleze ní kisɔsɔ mɛ. 14Ní lipoe lɛ ya mɛ ɛ, mlɔ bidɔ srɛsrɛ ʋɛ ɛ, biana ki ba baziazia ʋa atɔ xe mlɔ kyɛ mleku ní ɔzia mɛ ɛ, ba bidɔ srɛsrɛ ʋɛ biana ki mlɔ baziazia ʋa. Atɔ mlaze ni kisɔsɔ mɛ. 15Kiliɛ gi bɛŋwlimi kpɛ ni Aya liboe le me si i, “Ɔniliɛ gi atá yɛ ní kisrɛsrɛ mɛ ɛ, yɛ ɔni yɛ ɔ́sa srɛ ɛ, lɛ ɔniliɛ gi atá sisami kyɛ ɛ, yɛ ɔni yɛ ɔ́saɛ sisami.”
Bɛkpɛ Tito ní Korinto
16Medo Aya ɖase gi akpɛ lɛ ní Tito ɔna nɔ mɛ ɛ, si ɔkɔ litɔ ní lɛ mɛ ŋwisi kiliɛ gi makɔ litɔ ní lɛ mɛ aki mlɔ kinɛ ɛ. 17Lɛ ese linu si Tito ɔ́zɛ liboe le gi kwisi yɛ si ɔbitɛ tètè e, boŋu i, atrába nì yɛ ɔtutɔ yɛ kupɛpɛ vevee si tɔ yipɔni mlɔ nì yɛ ɔna nɔ petee e. 18Kɔpɔni yɛ ɛ, kwitsisi onemi ye gi yɛ isɔliha lɛ petee iikpɔ̀ kɔkpɛ yɛ ikù nɛ kukpɛkpɛɔ ki Liboe Gbã nɛ loso o. 19Lɛ kɔpɔnɔ ɛ, isɔliha lɛ iikya yɛ si ɔpɔni blɔ gi kwiikɔ ba bidɔkɔkɔ ʋɛ tsinɔ ɔ, gi blɔ ɔtutɔ kwitrɔ ɔ si kwikɔ ɛkpɛ kubùbù ní Ɔkaɛ ɔtutɔ lɔ su ani tɔ kubasɛ si kwikɔ lɛ mɛ si kwipɔnɔ ɔ.
20Kúpɛ si ɔnɔnɔ ɔki kunutsatsa zimizi utɔtɔ ní kiliɛ gi kwialɛ bidɔkɔkɔɛ ʋɛ ya a, gi bɛkɔ nì ɔna faã su u. 21Lɛ ese linu si kwipɛ si kuga kude gbã aní ní Aya tètè aŋu mɛ ɛ, pɔ̀ ní banima kyɛ aŋu mɛ ɛ.
22Kɔpɔnɔ ɛ, kwiikpɛ blɔ onemi ye nì ba kude; gi ebu abasi blɔ lipoe glegle ni ní bede glegle onu si kifu kilɛ ní yɛ mɛ ɛ, lɛ sanɔ ɛ, abasɛ si yɛ azɛ mlɔ aʋa aŋa. 23Tito kɔ ɛ, yɛ onu mɛ ɔgaglɔnɛɛ nì mɛ ɔxwɛwaglɔnɛ ní mlɔ kusi, blɔ benemi dzedze ba ɛ, ba bali ní isɔliha lɛ linyi ne me lɛ benu kubùbù kí Kristo. 24Lɛ loso mlabasɛ benyime ba ya mlɔ kunɔlɔɔ, nì kidiliɛ loso gi kuzɛɛdɔ isu ní mlɔ aʋa a, tɔ isɔliha lɛ petee biamɔ nɛ.

english_text:
The Money for God's People
1I don't need to write you about the money you plan to give for God's people. 2I know how eager you are to give. And I have proudly told the Lord's followers in Macedonia that you people in Achaia have been ready for a whole year. Now your desire to give has made them want to give. 3This is why I am sending Titus and the two others to you. I want you to be ready, just as I promised. This will prove we were not wrong to brag about you.
4Some followers from Macedonia may come with me, and I want them to find that you have the money ready. If you don't, I would be embarrassed for trusting you to do this. But you would be embarrassed even more. 5So I have decided to ask Titus and the others to spend some time with you before I arrive. This way they can arrange to collect the money you have promised. Then you will have the chance to give because you want to, and not because you feel forced to.
6Remember this saying,
“A few seeds make
a small harvest,
but a lot of seeds make
a big harvest.”
7 Each of you must make up your own mind about how much to give. But don't feel sorry that you must give and don't feel you are forced to give. God loves people who love to give. 8God can bless you with everything you need, and you will always have more than enough to do all kinds of good things for others. 9 The Scriptures say,
“God freely gives his gifts
to the poor,
and always does right.”
10 God gives seed to farmers and provides everyone with food. He will increase what you have, so you can give even more to those in need. 11You will be blessed in every way, and you will be able to keep on being generous. Then many people will thank God when we deliver your gift.
12What you are doing is much more than a service that supplies God's people with what they need. It is something that will make many others thank God. 13The way in which you have proved yourselves by this service will bring honor and praise to God. You believed the message about Christ, and you obeyed it by sharing generously with God's people and with everyone else. 14Now they are praying for you and want to see you, because God used you to bless them so very much. 15Thank God for his gift that is too wonderful for words!
translation_text:
Kupɔnɔ Kí Benemi Basɔlisɔli ba
1Nya líziã si miŋwlimi ki mlɔ ní bidɔkɔkɔɛ ʋɛ gi mlɛtsisi banɔ dradra ba su u. 2Lɛ ese linu si mete si mlɛpɛ veve si mlɛɛpɔnɔ, lɛ makɔ mlɔ adɔ isu akí Makedonia banɛ ma. Lɛ masi ba si, “Benemi ba ní Axaya ɛ, bɛbla si biaki kupɔnɔ dɔni galigɔ lɛ ke.” Mlɔ kupɛ́pɛɔ kwihwa ni bamɛ beglegle si ba kyɛ babitɛ kiditɔ. 3Abla ɛ, mɛɛtsisi benemi ba, tɔ blɔ kusufekeo ní eboa la ya mɛ ɛ, kukíze kupluplu u. Pɔ̀, tɔ mlaaze ní kublabla mɛ ni mlɔ kupɔnɔɔ kiliɛ gi mapani si mlaapɔnɔ ɛ. 4Lɛ ese linu si xegi mɛ ni banama a, gi bɛdɔ Makedonia kwiaba a, xe biamɔ si mlamá ní kublabla mɛ ɛ, kwiaŋa kuye gi nya kwika ní mlɔ aʋa gbɛgbɛ te e. Xegi kútrapani ní mlɔ kuyeo su i. 5Lɛ loso e, megyo le edi si liziã si mikpɛ benemi ba kigagla tɔ betù ɔtɔ aki mɛ tɔ biaakpɛ mlɔ bidɔkɔkɔ ʋɛ likpɔ ɔ. Ɔna mɛ faã bidɔkɔkɔɛ ʋɛ gi mleka ɛplɛ si mlaakɔ ɛ. Lɛ biakpɛ ʋɛ likpɔ xe maaba a, lɛ biabasɛ si kipɛ́ mlɛpɛ si mlɛɛki bɛ ɛ, lɛ bínu bidɔ gi bemi ní mlɔ aʋa si mlakɔ ɔ.
Mlakpɛ bidɔ ni ɔna faã
6Mlaplɛ aŋu si ɔniliɛ gi akpɛ bidɔ sisami i, yɛ aamɔ bidɔ sisami; lɛ ɔniliɛ gi akpɛ bidɔ glegle e, yɛ aamɔ bidɔ glegle. 7Mlɔmɛ ɔnɔ kakaa ɔkɔ kidiliɛ gi atrɔ ní yɛ ɔna nɔ mɛ si yɛ akɔ ɛ, aní ni aŋu mɛ nyasi nyasi loo ŋwisi kimi bemi ní yɛ aʋa kinɛ ɛ; lɛ ese linu si Aya izɛɛlɔ ɔniliɛ gi izɛɛki bidɔ ni isami i. 8Lɛ Aya aatani mlɔ yɛ lipomepɛpɛ kakaa kukɔ niteme si ní kidɔ kakaa mɛ ni lipoe kakaa mɛ ɛ, mlamɔ mlɔ baziazia ba petee lɛ mlɔ eboe gbã na aasrɛ. 9Kiliɛ gi Aya yɛ liboe le lipani si i,
“Izeehle yɛ bidɔkɔkɔ ʋɛ ɛki bazianete ba ni ɔna faã;
lɛ yɛ kude gyɔgyɔɔ kulɛ kɔtsinɔ kanɔmɛ anɔmɛ.”
10Lɛ Aya gi yɛ izɛɛki esebi la ɛki ɔnyrɔmɛdzɛɛ ni àklã na ki kidɔŋaŋa ɛ, yɛ kee aaki mlamɔ eboe gyɔgyɔ la gi aasrɛ. Lɛ aaki si kidiliɛ gi kilɛ ni mlɔ awla ɛ, kiasrɛ. 11Aaki si mlaaze bexonete ní kude kakaa onu gi mlɔ fɛ mlaaki bidɔ ni ɔna faã lipoe kakaa a, lɛ gani blɔ aʋa ɛ, mlɔ bidɔkɔkɔ ʋɛ biaki si banima biado Aya ɖase.
12Lixwɛ nɛ gi mlɛwa ɛ, liʋíki Aya yɛ banima ba bidɔ ziazia ʋɛ tètè ko, boŋu i, lɛ liiki si banɔ glegle bedo Aya ɖase. 13Lɛ kɔkpɛ kidiliɛ gi mlɔ axwɛwawa la ebu ibasɛ ɛ, banima bíakɔ kukpɔ̀kpɔ̀ ki Aya kɔkpɛ mlɔ isu ya kukɔkpɛ ní Kristo yɛ Liboe Gbã nɛ mɛ ɛ, gi mleenu kutu loso o. Lɛ ya lipɔni mlɔ bidɔkɔkɔ ni ɔna faã gi mlɛlɛ ʋɛ ni ba ni ɔnɔ kakaa fɛ ɛ. 14Lɛ loso, ní ba Aya kuxwaxwa ní mlɔ litukpo me e, ba ina nɛ iigyo mlo edi kɔkpɛ Aya yɛ lipomepɛpɛ ʋidi lɛ gi aki mlɔ loso o. 15Mlaki tɔ kudo Aya ɖase ki yɛ bidɔkɔkɔɛ ʋɛ gi ɔnɔnɔ ɔ́tratani kusrɔ loso o.

english_text:
Paul Defends His Work for Christ
1Do you think I am a coward when I am with you and brave when I am far away? Well, I ask you to listen, because Christ himself was humble and gentle. 2Some people have said we act like the people of this world. So when I arrive, I expect I will have to be firm and forceful in what I say to them. Please don't make me treat you that way. 3We live in this world, but we don't act like its people 4or fight our battles with the weapons of this world. Instead, we use God's power that can destroy fortresses. We destroy arguments 5and every bit of pride that keeps anyone from knowing God. We capture people's thoughts and make them obey Christ. 6And when you completely obey him, we will punish anyone who refuses to obey.
7You judge by appearances. If any of you think you are the only ones who belong to Christ, then think again. We belong to Christ as much as you do. 8Maybe I brag a little too much about the authority that the Lord gave me to help you and not to hurt you. Yet I am not embarrassed to brag. 9And I am not trying to scare you with my letters. 10Some of you are saying, “Paul's letters are harsh and powerful. But in person, he is a weakling and has nothing worth saying.” 11Those people had better understand that when I am with you, I will do exactly what I say in my letters.
12We won't dare compare ourselves with those who think so much of themselves. But they are foolish to compare themselves with themselves. 13We won't brag about something we don't have a right to brag about. We will only brag about the work God has sent us to do, and you are part of that work. 14We are not bragging more than we should. After all, we did bring the message about Christ to you.
15We don't brag about what others have done, as if we had done those things ourselves. But I hope as you become stronger in your faith, we will be able to reach many more of the people around you. That has always been our goal. 16Then we will be able to preach the good news in other lands where we cannot take credit for work someone else has already done. 17 The Scriptures say, “If you want to brag, then brag about the Lord.” 18You may brag about yourself, but the only approval that counts is the Lord's approval.
translation_text:
Paulo Aŋwlɛ Yɛ Axwɛwawa la litukpo
1Gani Kristo yɛ kusububɔ ni yɛ litɔplɛplɛkese kese me e, mɛ Paulo, gi mlɛzɛɛsɛ si menu ɔnɔ gi onu ofufluse e, xegi malɛ ní mlɔ kusi i, pɔ̀ xe maʋíse ɔnɔ aŋu mɛ ɛ, xegi malɛ ni ɔdzage e, meʋi dɔni mlɔ iwla mɛ si 2mɛɛki mlɔ litukpo si xegi maba mlɔ klɔ ɛ, litrázia si mizeehe eboe gagla a, ni banama gi begyo le edi si kelea lia mɛ bede ʋa onu kulɛ ligbã́ nɛ ɛ. 3Gi kulɛ ní kelea mɛ kyɛ ɛ, kuʋíta kigũ ŋwisi kelea mɛ kinɛ ɛ. 4Bigutadɔɛ mɛ su axwɛ gi kwiiwa ɛ, bínu kelea ya mɛ bigutadɔ pɔ̀ binu Aya yɛ bigutadɔ gi kigagla kiliɛ ní bɛ awla a, gi biazizi ahwafiɔnɛ yɛ izekɔ gagla lɛ ɛ. 5Kuzeezizi kuboehe kakaa; ni imɔkpɛkpɛ kakaa gi iyɔ kedu ya ní Aya kuteteo su u; kuzeevu kugyodi kakaa tɔ kuki si kukpɛ litukpo ní Kristo ese. 6Lɛ kuli ní kublabla mɛ si kwiahe kutu kí kutu monumonu kakaa xegi mlɛbasɛ si mlalɛ ní kutununu me petee ɛki Kristo e.
7Mleedi eboa la gi alɛ ní mlɔ aŋu na mɛ ɛ. Xegi mlɔmɛ onutɔ aka ní yɛ isu aʋa si yenu Kristo ɔnɛ ɛ, lilɛ si odzini le kutapo odi mumɔ si blɔ kyɛ ɛ, kunu Kristo banɛ ŋwisi yɛ ɔtutɔ lɔ kiniɛ na a. 8Lɛ ese linu si xegi maasa awla kakɔ ɔ, ní kigaglaɛ liɛ gi Ɔkaɛ aki blɔ si tɔ kukɔ gani mlɔ tɔ mletsi i sani si tɔ kuzizi mlo kyɛ ɛ, kuye kútraku me e. 9Mápɛ si miŋwi ŋwisi meekeni idi si míkpɛ mlɔ lifu ni mɛ kuplekpaa a, gi maŋwlimi aki mlɔ mɛ ɛ. 10Lɛ ese linu si banɔatɔ bɛɛpani si, “Paulo yɛ kuplekpaa gi aŋwlimi i, kɔmɛ eboe la alɛ nyipu lɛ alɛ gagla a, pɔ̀ xe yɛ ɔtutɔ ɔlɛ ní blɔ su i, ónu ɔnɔ gagla a, lɛ yɛ eboe la ɛ, enu kididɔ ɔ.” 11Linu si banɔ ba klɔ ba bamɔ egyesi si lidzegaga ɛtɔtɔ líma ní kidiliɛ gi kwiŋwlimi ní blɔ kuplekpaa mɛ ɛ, xe kúma ní mlɔ su i, ani kidiliɛ gi kwiabitɛ ɛ, xe kuli ní mlɔ kusi i me.
12Kútrakeni idi si kúkla isu ya kpɛ loo tɔ kúkɔ isu ya ɛsrɔni banama gi bizɛkpɔ̀ isu ya a. Gi beegyigye batutɔ ba isu ya a nì ba isu ya, xe bɛɛkɔ ba isu ya ɛsrɔni ni ba ahanuglɔ ba ɛ, bétè liboe. 13Blɔ kɔ ɛ, blɔ kusudɔɔ kútradɔ kudeo ɛza a; pɔ̀ blɔ kusudɔɔ kwiaze ní axwɛ ana gi Aya akpɛ ní blɔ awla ɛ aʋa, lɛ axwɛ ana klɔ ɛna mlɔ kyɛ ɛ. 14Lɛ kuʋísa kutrɛ ní blɔ kusudɔɔ mɛ ɛ, kiliɛ gi liaze e, xegi kúba mlɔ klɔ ni Liboe Gbã nɛ ní Kristo su u. Lɛ ese linu si kwitani mlɔ kuna ni Kristo yɛ Liboe Gbã nɛ. 15Lɛ kuʋídɔ kudeo ɛza si kwidɔ isu ní axwɛ gi banɔ dzedze bɛwa aʋa a. Blɔ kudeonudidi kunu si kiliɛ gi mlɔ kuziŋaa kwiitsi ipɔnɔ ɛ, blɔ axwɛ na kètu ya ni mlɔ kusi kiane sanɔ. 16Kɔlɔ kwiatani Liboe Gbã nɛ kupani ní kuse dzedzeo aʋa. Lɛ ese linu si kúpɛ si kwidɔ isu u ní axwɛ gi bɛwa kokoo ní ɔnɔ dzedze yɛ kètu ya mɛ ɛ aʋa.
17Pɔ̀ Aya yɛ liboe le lipani si i, “Ɔniliɛ gi apɛ si yidɔ isu ya ɛ, lilɛ si ɔdɔ isu ya ní kidiliɛ gi Ɔkaɛ abitɛ aʋa a.” 18Lɛ ese linu si aní ɔniliɛ gi ɛɛkpɔ isu ya ɛ, yɛ onu ɔnɔ gbã ɛ, pɔ̀ ɔniliɛ gi Ɔkaɛ akpɔ̀ ɛ.

english_text:
Paul and the False Apostles
1Please put up with a little of my foolishness. 2I am as concerned about you as God is. You were like a virgin bride I had chosen only for Christ. 3 But now I fear that you will be tricked, just as Eve was tricked by that lying snake. I am afraid that you might stop thinking about Christ in an honest and sincere way. 4We told you about Jesus, and you received the Holy Spirit and accepted our message. But you let some people tell you about another Jesus. Now you are ready to receive another spirit and accept a different message. 5I think I am as good as any of those super apostles. 6I may not speak as well as they do, but I know as much. And this has already been made perfectly clear to you.
7Was it wrong for me to lower myself and honor you by preaching God's message free of charge? 8I robbed other churches by taking money from them to serve you. 9 Even when I was in need, I still didn't bother you. In fact, some of the Lord's followers from Macedonia brought me what I needed. I have not been a burden to you in the past, and I will never be a burden. 10As surely as I speak the truth about Christ, no one in Achaia can stop me from bragging about this. 11And it isn't because I don't love you. God himself knows how much I do love you.
12I plan to go on doing just what I have always done. Then those people won't be able to brag about doing the same things we are doing. 13Anyway, they are no more than false apostles and dishonest workers. They only pretend to be apostles of Christ. 14And it is no wonder. Even Satan tries to make himself look like an angel of light. 15So why does it seem strange for Satan's servants to pretend to do what is right? Someday they will get exactly what they deserve.
Paul's Sufferings for Christ
16I don't want any of you to think I am a fool. But if you do, then let me be a fool and brag a little. 17When I do all this bragging, I do it as a fool and not for the Lord. 18Yet if others want to brag about what they have done, so will I. 19And since you are so smart, you will gladly put up with a fool. 20In fact, you let people make slaves of you and cheat you and steal from you. Why, you even let them strut around and slap you in the face. 21I am ashamed to say we are too weak to behave in such a way.
If they can brag, so can I, but it is a foolish thing to do. 22Are they Hebrews? So am I. Are they Jews? So am I. Are they from the family of Abraham? Well, so am I. 23 Are they servants of Christ? I am a fool to talk this way, but I serve him better than they do. I have worked harder and have been put in jail more times. I have been beaten with whips more and have been in danger of death more often.
24 Five times my own people gave me 39 lashes with a whip. 25 Three times the Romans beat me with a big stick, and once my enemies stoned me. I have been shipwrecked three times, and I even had to spend a night and a day in the sea. 26 During my many travels, I have been in danger from rivers, robbers, my own people, and foreigners. My life has been in danger in cities, in deserts, at sea, and with people who only pretended to be the Lord's followers.
27I have worked and struggled and spent many sleepless nights. I have gone hungry and thirsty and often had nothing to eat. I have been cold from not having enough clothes to keep me warm. 28Besides everything else, each day I am burdened down, worrying about all the churches. 29When others are weak, I am weak too. When others are tricked into sin, I get angry.
30If I have to brag, I will brag about how weak I am. 31God, the Father of our Lord Jesus, knows I am not lying. And God is to be praised forever! 32 The governor of Damascus at the time of King Aretas had the city gates guarded, so he could capture me. 33But I escaped by being let down in a basket through a window in the city wall.
translation_text:
Paulo Ni Asia na Banɔkpɛkpɛ ba
1Megyo le edi si mlaazi mɛ xegi mɛɛdɔ kuga taʋɛ fɛ ɛ; Mlɛɛbitɛ lɛ nite kokoo! 2Mɛɛgɔni kìfu ní mlɔ onu lɛ kìfui liɛ gi mɛgɔni i, kidɔ ní Aya klɔ. Mlalɛ ko ŋwisi ɔdisami gi makpɛ kuwlawla akɔ akí onyime tole e, Kristo kinɛ. 3Meese lifu ne si biapisa mlɔ begyodi ʋa ŋwisi kiliɛ gi ɔgblagaɛ akɔ ɔdaye lo emeni Eva kiniɛ ɛ, lɛ mlɔ begyodi ʋa biasɛ ní mlɔ ɔna mɛ dradra ni kulɔlɔ petee aki Kristo su u. 4Lɛ ese linu si xegi mlezenu banama a, gi bɛba ɛzɛpani ní Yesu dzedze su liboe ɛki mlɔ ɔ, gi liga lidze ní Yesu liɛ ɛ, gi yɛ su liboe kwiba ɛpani ɛki mlɔ ɛ, loo mlɛzi Ɔfuɛfuɛ dzedze gi ɛga lidze ní lɔ klɔ ɔ, gi mlɛzɛ su i, loo mlenu Liboe Gbã gi liga lidze ní Liboe Gbãɛ nɛ gi mlɛzɛ su i, mlalɛ ní kublabla mɛ si mlazi lɛ kyɛ!
5Mámɔ lɛ si “banɔkpɛkpɛ dzi aŋu mɛ” ba klɔ ʋa, bɛɛpɛ sani mɛ ɛ. 6Nyafɛ béhe me ŋwisi ɔnɔ gi aapani ní banɔ kusi i, pɔ̀ kuboesenu kulɛ ní mɛ awla a; lɛ kwidra lɛ mɛ ki mlɔ lipoe kakaa gani kude kakaa onu u.
7Liboe zimizi linu si mibubɔ isu tɔ mimuni mlo gani Aya yɛ liboe le gi mapani aki mlɔ faã a, gi mázi kibɔ ní mlɔ awla na a? 8Lize ŋwisi mazeezuru isɔliha dzedze le gi mazi kupɔnɔ adɔ ni ba klɔ ɔ, xe makɔ kɔ apɔni mlɔ ɛ. 9Lɛ gi mazɛɛwa axwɛ na ní mlɔ kusi i, xe mazia kiditɔ ɛ, méze lidɔtu iiki ɔnɔnɔ ɔ, lɛ ese linu si benemi ba ya gi bɛdɔ ní Makedonia ɛ, bɛzɛɛki mɛ kidiliɛ gi mazia ɛ. Medi le aʋa si méze lidɔtu iiki mlɔ ní kude utɔtɔ onu u, lɛ maadi le aʋa si maatrɛ ní lɛ aʋa nite e. 10Ní Kristo yɛ anukwarɛɛ liɛ gi ɔlɛ ní mɛ mɛ̀ onu i, ɔnɔnɔ ni Axaya kesea mɛ ɛ, ɔ́tratsi kude ɛki mɛ kusufeke uliɔ ya a. 11Ege loso e? kilɔ málɔ mlɔ loso a? Aya ete si malɔ mlɔ!
12Matratrɛ ní lɛ aʋa tɔ mibitɛ kidiliɛ gi kɛ mɛɛbitɛ abla ɛ, atɔ mitsi kudeonu ikí banɔkpɛkpɛ ba klɔ gi bɛɛpɛ kudeonu u, si tɔ bamɔ ba si blɔ ni ba kwisɔ ní eboa la gi bɛɛpani i mɛ. 13Lɛ ese linu si banɔ báklɔ ba ɛ, benu asia banɔkpɛkpɛ, benu baxwɛwa gi bizeemeni banɔ ɔ, gi bekyi isu ya a, ŋwisi Kristo yɛ banɔkpɛkpɛ kinɛ ɛ. 14Lɛ likítu mlo kugyɔ ɔ, lɛ ese linu si Satana ɔtutɔ lɔ ɛ, abitɛ yɛ isu ya ŋwisi Aya yɛ ɔxwɛdzɛ gi izekle kinɛ ɛ! 15Lɛ loso e, línu liboe gi liatu ɔnɔ kugyɔ ɔ si yɛ baxwɛdzɛ ba fɛ, bɛtsrɛ ba isu ya tɔ baŋwi ŋwisi banɔsumu gi ba bede ʋa bɛgyɔ ɔ. Ba oblono kɔ aaze kidiliɛ ɖii gi ba kiboebitɛɛ kiʋi i.
Paulo adɔ isu ya ní yɛ Ɔzia mɔmɔ nɔ aʋa
16Meedzini ipani si: ɔnɔnɔ ɔki gyo le edi si menu kanukpaʋi i. Pɔ̀ xe wɔzi lɛ nite e, lɛ kɔ mlazi mɛ ŋwisi kanukpaʋi atɔ midɔ̀ isu ya taʋɛ. 17Ní mɛ kusudɔɔ liɔ ya mɛ ɛ, máʋipani ŋwisi kiliɛ gi Ɔkaɛ aapani i, boŋu i, mɛɛpani ŋwisi kanukpaʋi kinɛ ɛ. 18Agi banɔ glegle bɛɛdɔ isu ya ní kelea mɛ bede ʋa onu i, mɛ fɛ maadɔ isu ya. 19Mlɛplɛ litɔ kese ki kunukpaʋiɔ ni isami lɛ ese linu si mlete liboe koŋ! 20Ní anukwarɛɛ mɛ ɛ, mlɛzɛɛzi ɔnɔ kakaa elue gi aabitɛ mlɔ yɛ banɔdzidzi loo ɔnɔ gi aakpɛ mlɔ klanɔ ɛ, loo amɔ ɔŋakɔ ní mlɔsu u, loo izeefeke isu ní mlɔ aʋa a, loo aasa mlɔ bidɔ ní aŋu na mɛ ɛ. 21Linu mɛ kuye si mizɛ lɛ si kwize befufluse gbɛgbɛ nite gi kwibitɛ eboa la ya ɛ!
Pɔ̀ xe ɔnutɔ aakenidi si yidɔ isu ya ní kiditɔ su i, mɛ fɛ maadɔ isu ní kɛ su u; mɛɛpani ŋwisi kanukpaʋi kinɛ. 22Hebrianɛ benu na a? Mɛ fɛ Hebriɔnɛ menu. Israelbanɛ benu na a? Mɛ fɛ Israelɔnɛ menu. Abraham yɛ besoebi benu na a? Mɛ fɛ Abraham yɛ osobi menu. 23Kristo yɛ banɔsumu benu na a? Masani ba. (Mɛɛpani ŋwisi ɔnɔ gi yɛ aŋu mɛ eezizi kinɛ ɛ). Mawa axwɛ gagla sani ba, meze ligba lipoe glegle, bekusi me mɔmɔɔmɔ, lɛ meze kutse iwla mɛ lipoe glegle e. 24Yudanɛ ma bɛfiaʋi mɛ biseʋi avitata ni ɔgɔtile afiaʋi tetsu zizi; 25Tatá zizi bɛfiaʋi mɛ biseʋi ʋe; lɛ bɛsa mɛ akla tule e. Onitsahu lo ɛyaʋi nì blɔ tatá, gi mawɔ ní axu la mɛ elesi zizi nì liwe tile e. 26Mazɛ aklanɔ lipoe kakaa, lɛ meze kuxixa mɛ ɛ, dɔni inipe le kɔ, dɔni banama gi bɛtsa bede ʋa ɛki mɛ kɔ ɔ, dɔni blɔ ɔtutɔ blɔ akɔanɛ ma iwla mɛ ni banama gi bénu Yudanɛ ma ba iwla mɛ ɛ, baxixa ʋa beze ní ima ʋidi ne me, ni kumami yɔ mɛ, ní axu la aʋa, ani banama gi benu asia baʋaziŋa ba klɔ ɔ. 27Mawa axwɛ ni kupèpè; gi lipoe glegle e, mázadɔ sra; Kuʋɛ ni olile eku me; lɛ lipoe glegle e, máŋa kididɔ ɔ, lɛ kublɔ kwizeku me gi mámɔ ɔ ewu gi maakpɛ fɛ ɛ. 28Lɛ ní la klɔ ʋa kede e, mɛ begyodi ʋa mɛ báʋidɔ liwewe gi mɛ aŋu me éple kese kí isɔli ha lɛ petee loso o. 29Nyaŋwɛ ebotso xe mɛ mébotso o; lɛ xegi bɛgbani ɔnutɔ ɛkpɛ ní eboe zimizi me e, mizɛɛnu kuyeʋiyeʋi ní mɛ isu ya mɛ.
30Xe linu si midɔ̀ isu ya ɛ, maadɔ isu ní kudɔkpaa liɔ gi kwibasɛ si kigagla kíma ní mɛ awla ɛ su. 31Aya gi onu Ɔka kí Ɔkaɛ Yesu u, gi yɛ biakpɔ̀ tsinɔ ní kanɔmɛ ɛ, ete si máʋisɛ asia a. 32Agi meze ní Damasko e, ɔma nɔ ɔʋadi ye gi eze ní Okusi Areta ese e, akpɛ banɔpe ba ní ɔma ʋidi no lɔ kuvunu yo tɔ bevu me e. 33Pɔ̀ bepleni me ní kasɔʋi mɛ gani ligli le fɛsrɛ ɔtɔ me, lɛ mese ní yɛ su i.

english_text:
Visions from the Lord
1I have to brag. There is nothing to be gained by it, but I must brag about the visions and other things that the Lord has shown me. 2I know about one of Christ's followers who was taken up into the third heaven 14 years ago. I don't know if the man was still in his body when it happened, but God certainly knows.
3As I said, only God really knows if this man was in his body at the time. 4But he was taken up into paradise, where he heard things too wonderful to tell. 5I will brag about that man, but not about myself, except to say how weak I am.
6Yet even if I did brag, I would not be foolish. I would simply be speaking the truth. But I will try not to say too much. That way, none of you will think more highly of me than you should because of what you have seen me do and say. 7Of course, I am now referring to the wonderful things I saw. One of Satan's angels was sent to make me suffer terribly, so that I would not feel too proud.
8Three times I begged the Lord to make this suffering go away. 9But he replied, “My gift of undeserved grace is all you need. My power is strongest when you are weak.” So if Christ keeps giving me his power, I will gladly brag about how weak I am. 10Yes, I am glad to be weak or insulted or mistreated or to have troubles and sufferings, if it is for Christ. Because when I am weak, I am strong.
Paul's Concern for the Lord's Followers at Corinth
11I have been making a fool of myself. But you forced me to do it, when you should have been speaking up for me. I may be nothing at all, but I am as good as those super apostles. 12When I was with you, I was patient and worked all the powerful miracles and signs and wonders of a true apostle. 13You missed out on only one blessing the other churches received. That is, you didn't have to support me. Forgive me for doing you wrong.
14I am planning to visit you for the third time. But I still won't make a burden of myself. What I really want is you, and not what you have. Children are not supposed to save up for their parents, but parents are supposed to take care of their children. 15So I will gladly give all I have and all I am. Will you love me less for loving you too much? 16You agree that I wasn't a burden to you. Maybe that's because I was trying to catch you off guard and trick you. 17Were you cheated by any of those I sent to you? 18I urged Titus to visit you, and I sent another follower with him. But Titus didn't cheat you, and we felt and behaved the same way he did.
19Have you been thinking all along that we have been defending ourselves to you? Actually, we have been speaking to God as followers of Christ. But, my friends, we did it all for your good.
20I am afraid when I come, we won't be pleased with each other. I fear that some of you may be arguing or jealous or angry or selfish or gossiping or insulting each other. I even fear that you may be proud and acting like a mob. 21I am afraid God will make me ashamed when I visit you again. I will feel like crying because many of you have never given up your old sins. You are still doing things that are immoral, indecent, and shameful.
translation_text:
Eboa la gi Ɔkaɛ Ebu Abasi Paulo e
1Lilɛ si mitrɛ ní lɛ aʋa tɔ midɔ̀ isu ya, xegi lítrabu libi fɛ ɛ. Maapani ní eboa la gi Ɔkaɛ aki si mamɔ ní kubububasɛ uliɔ gi aki mɛ ɛ su. 2Mete onyime ɔtɔ ní Kristo me gi befeke tsinɔ ní lifunu ne ɔtanɛ klɔ agɔ liɔfɔ ni tene la ya. Méte xe kisugyaɛ mɛ eze loo Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ mɛ eze e, xe liboe lɛ ya liba lɛ mɛ ɛ, pɔ̀ Aya ko ete e. 3Lɛ mete si ɔniliɛ ya ɛ, xe kisugyaɛ mɛ eze loo óze ní kisugyaɛ mɛ ɛ, méte; Aya ko ete. 4Lɛ mete si onyime liɛ ya ɛ, bɛtsini yɛ ní Paradiso ye me, lɛ enu eboa la su gi kinemi kitrátani kupani i, eboa la su gi báki ɔnɔ gbagbe kude si ɔpani i. 5Ɔniliɛ ya elu sú maadɔ isu ya, pɔ̀ mátradɔ̀ isu ya ní mɛ ɔtutɔ lɔ mɛ isu ya su u, sani si bidɔ bɛklɔ gi biabasɛ si mebotso e. 6Xe nya mapɛ si midɔ̀ isu ya ɛ, mátraze kanukpaʋi i, lɛ ese linu si maazɛpani anukwarɛɛ. Pɔ̀ mátradɔ̀ isu ya a, lɛ loso ɔnɔnɔ ɔ́tramɔ mɛ ɔnɔ ʋidi sani kiliɛ gi amɔ mɛ kidiliɛ gi mabitɛ ɛ, ni eboa la gi mapani i.
7Pɔ̀ tɔ mikíze ní kusufekeo ni kusudɔɔ mɛ ɛ, kɔkpɛ eboe ʋidi a la gi bebu ibasi mɛ loso ɛ, bɛkpɛ kunya gi kulɛ ŋwisi kibu kinɛ ɛ, ní mɛ isu ya mɛ, dɔni Ozimizi ye yɛ ɔxwɛdzɛ klɔ gi kwiwa axwɛ na tɔ mikifeke isu ya a. 8Tatá zizi maxwa Ɔkaɛ si ɔti lidɔtu i lɛ ya ní mɛ aʋa. 9Pɔ̀ ebu le onu si; “Mɛ lipomepɛpɛ nɛ lisrɛ ɛki wɔ, lɛ ese linu si mɛ kigaglaɛ kizɛɛna kɛ ɔna klɔ ní kubotsobotso me.” Lɛ loso maadɔ isu isanɔ nì isami ní mɛ kubotsobotsoo me e, atɔ Kristo yɛ kigaglaɛ kiaze ní mɛ aʋa. 10Lɛ ɔkpɛ Kristo loso e, lilɛ mɛ ole xegi mebotso o, xegi beekpini me e, xegi malɛ ní kuxixa mɛ ɛ, xegi bɛyɔ kedu ní mɛ su u, xegi eboe la aʋítrɛ lɛ aʋa ɛki mɛ mumɔ ɛ. Lɛ ese linu si, xegi mebotso e, kɔ malɛ gagla ɛ.
Paulo Akɔ litɔ lɛ mɛ akí Korintoanɛ ma
11Mabitɛ isu ya kanukpaʋi, pɔ̀ mlɔ mlɛkɔ ɔ, xe mabitɛ nite e. Mlɔ nya lilɛ si mlakpɔ mɛ xe e, Lɛ ese linu si aní ɔnɔ fiteete menu, xe bɛkɔ mɛ srɔni “banɔkpɛkpɛ gbã ba,” gani lɛ mɛ si ménu kididɔ fɛ ɛ. 12Kidiliɛ gi kizɛkpɛ banɔkpɛkpɛ ba gyesi i, gi kinu lixaŋu nɛ, enugutseboe, ni eboe mamɔ edi i, mabitɛ ʋɛ ní mlɔ kusi ni litɔplɛplɛkese kese glegle. 13Kiboebitɛ kitɔtɔ kilɛ gi mabitɛ aki isɔliha dzedze le e, gi mábitɛ aki mlɔ sani si mákɔ isu atrɔ ní mlɔ aʋa na a? Mlakɔ kesetataa lia akyɛ mɛ!
14Abla ɛ, malɛ ní kublabla mɛ si míba edi mlo kutanɛ, lɛ mátraze lidɔtu iiki mlɔ ɔ, lɛ ese linu si mlɔ ɔtutɔ lɔ mɛɛpɛ, aní kidiliɛ gi kiliɛ ní mlɔ awla a. Líziã si banuvɔ ba babla bidɔ aplɛ aki ba bapɔnɛ ba a, pɔ̀ linu si bapɔnɛ ba babla bidɔ aplɛ akí ba bebi ba. 15Liaze mɛ isami si miwa mɛ kidɔ kakaa su axwɛ aki mlɔ, kɔpɔni mɛ ɔtutɔ mɛ isu ya kyɛ. Lɛ xegi malɔ mlɔ asanɔ ɛ, mlaatí ní mlɔ kulɔlɔɔ kimɛ aʋa na a?
16Kiliɛ gi lilɛ ɛ, mlɛdimɛ si ménu lidɔtu aki mlɔ. Pɔ̀ ɔmɔnɛɛ gi menu i, mapunu mlɔ lɛ mevu mlo e. 17Maba mlɔ gani benyime ba ya gi makpɛ mlɔ klɔ aʋa na a? 18Makpɛ litukpo kese akí Tito si ɔba mlɔ klɔ, lɛ makpɛ mɛ onemi ye nì yɛ kude e. Tito ɔ́ba mlɔ, loo aba mlɔ na a? Aní Ɔfuɛfuɛ tole ni kude tule aʋa kwiga na?
19Kibu mleebu si gakɔna ni abla ɛ, isu litukpo kwiŋwlɛ ki mlɔ na a? Aní nite, kwipani ní Aya aŋu mɛ ŋwisi banɔ gi balɛ ní Kristo me e; lɛ baʋia dimɛnɔ ba, kidɔ kakaa gi kwibitɛ ɛ, kwibitɛ kɛ ɛki mlɔ kigagla kukpɛkpɛ. 20Lɛ ese linu si mese lifu si xegi meku ni klɔ ʋa ɛ, mátramɔ mlɔ kiliɛ gi maapɛ si mleze e, lɛ mlɔ fɛ ɛ, mlátramɔ mɛ kiliɛ gi mlaapɛ si mize e. Meese lifu ne si maamɔ avitɛ, kaŋuzá, kusuyeʋi, kulɛlɛ, banɔ su kuzizi, kunugu kugugu ní banɔ su, kusudɔ ni kapisa ní mlɔ ɔma nɔ mɛ ɛ. 21Meese lifu si xegi maba mlɔ klɔ dzɛ ɛ, mɛ Aya aaple nì me ní mlɔ aŋu mɛ, lɛ ɔna aata mɛ ɔkpɛ banɔ glegle ba ya gi bɛta kese zanɔ ɛ, xe békyi ɔna mɛ ní ba anyaniboe la mɛ, ba kihasi ye ni kuda ŋwɛŋwɛ kpaŋukpaŋu mɛ ɛ.

english_text:
Final Warnings and Greetings
1 I am on my way to visit you for the third time. And as the Scriptures say, “Any charges must be proved true by at least two or three witnesses.” 2During my second visit I warned you that I would punish you and anyone else who doesn't stop sinning. I am far away from you now, but I give you the same warning. 3This should prove to you that I am speaking for Christ. When he corrects you, he won't be weak. He will be powerful! 4Although he was weak when he was nailed to the cross, he now lives by the power of God. We are weak, just as Christ was. But you will see that we will live by the power of God, just as Christ does.
5Test yourselves and find out if you really are true to your faith. If you pass the test, you will discover that Christ is living in you. But if Christ isn't living in you, you have failed. 6I hope you will discover we have not failed. 7We pray you will stop doing evil things. We don't pray like this to make ourselves look good, but to get you to do right, even if we are failures.
8All we can do is to follow the truth and not fight against it. 9Even though we are weak, we are glad that you are strong, and we pray you will do even better. 10I am writing these things to you before I arrive. This way I won't have to be hard on you when I use the authority the Lord has given me. I was given this authority, so I could help you and not destroy you.
11Goodbye, my friends. Do better and pay attention to what I have said. Try to get along and live peacefully with each other.
Now I pray that God, who gives love and peace, will be with you. 12Give each other a warm greeting. All God's people send their greetings.
13I pray that the Lord Jesus Christ will bless you and be kind to you! May God bless you with his love, and may the Holy Spirit join all your hearts together.
translation_text:
Paulo yɛ Kugbágba Gɔgɔɔ
1Kutánɛ kɔya gi matradi mlo o. Kwite si “Liboe kakaa ɛ, linu si banɔ tiaʋa loo tiata beze gi biaŋa katsalia ki lɛ ɛ.” 2Magba mlɔ lipoe lɛ gi maba mlɔ klɔ kuʋla a.
