15Liaze si kidiliɛ loso gi Onesimo asɛ ní wɔ su lipoe kpekpemi itɔ ko linu si tɔ wɔadzini ye kuzɛ lipoe kakaa. 16Lɛ abla ɛ, ónu ɔnɔdzidzi tɔ ɔ, pɔ̀ asani ɔnɔdzidzi; onu onemi gi azɛ kedzi ní Kristo me e. Onu ɔnɔ gi azɛ kedzi aki mɛ! Lɛ aaze ɔnɔ gi azɛ kedzi aki wɔ ŋwisi ɔnɔdzidzi ani onemi ní Ɔkaɛ mɛ ɛ!
17Lɛ loso, xe wɔkɔ mɛ ŋwisi wɔ ɔha nɛ ɔxwɛwa kinɛ ɛ, kɔlɔ dzini ye zɛ kiliɛ gi wɔazɛ mɛ ɛ. 18Xegi ata wɔ ese loo adɔ liʋɛɛtɔ ní wɔ su i, bú le mɛ ní liʋɛ nɛ mɛ. 19Ni ya ɛ, mɛɛŋwlimi liboe lɛ ya nì mɛ ɔtutɔ mɛ awla la si: mɛ Paulo maatsa liʋɛɛ nɛ klɔ ki wɔ. Líziã si miplɛ aŋu kí wɔ xe e, plɛ aŋu si wɔ kyɛ wɔdɔ liʋɛ ni mɛ su kí wɔ ligbã́ nɛ ŋwisi ɔʋaziŋa kinɛ ɛ. 20Lɛ loso mɛ onemi ye, maki wɔ litukpo tɔ mikpɛ wɔ kunugu taʋɛ kɔkpɛ Ɔkaɛ loso; ŋwisi onemi ní Kristo me kinɛ ɛ, ki tɔ mimɔ isami!
21Maka lɛ aʋa si, gi mɛɛŋwlimi lɛ ya ɛ, wɔabitɛ kidiliɛ gi meʋi i, lɛ mete si wɔabitɛ sanɔ kiliɛ gi meʋi i. 22Kɔpɔnɔ ɛ, lulɔ kugbake kutɔ gi maaze plɛ ki mɛ ɛ, lɛ ese linu si meedi kudeonu si Aya aadra kudeonu kí yɛ kuxwaxwa gi mlɔ petee mlɛxwa ɛ, tɔ aki tɔ maadzi ba mlɔ klɔ.
Baʋaziŋa dzedze ba kunɔpanɔɔ
23Epafra, gi ɔlɛ ligba nì mɛ kɔkpɛ Kristo Yesu i, atsisi wɔ yɛ kunɔpanɔɔ, 24nite ke mɛ ahanuglɔ baxwɛwa ba, Marko, Aristarxo, Demas ani Luka kyɛ bɛpani wɔ.
25Tɔ lipomepɛpɛ nɛ gi lidɔ blɔ Ɔkaɛ Yesu Kristo klɔ ɛ, lize ni mlɔ petee.

english_text:
1Long ago in many ways and at many times God's prophets spoke his message to our ancestors. 2 But now at last, God sent his Son to bring his message to us. God created the universe by his Son, and everything will someday belong to the Son. 3 God's Son has all the brightness of God's own glory and is like him in every way. By his own mighty word, he holds the universe together.
After the Son had washed away our sins, he sat down at the right side of the glorious God in heaven. 4He had become much greater than the angels, and the name he was given is far greater than any of theirs.
God's Son Is Greater than Angels
5 God has never said
to any of the angels,
“You are my Son, because today
I have become your Father!”
Neither has God said
to any of them,
“I will be his Father,
and he will be my Son!”
6 When God brings his first-born Son into the world, he commands all of his angels to worship him.
7 And when God speaks about the angels, he says,
“I change my angels into wind
and my servants
into flaming fire.”
8 But God says about his Son,
“You are God,
and you will rule
as King forever!
Your royal power
brings about justice.
9You loved justice
and hated evil,
and so I, your God,
have chosen you.
I appointed you
and made you happier
than any of your friends.”
10 The Scriptures also say,
“In the beginning, Lord,
you were the one
who laid the foundation
of the earth
and created the heavens.
11They will all disappear
and wear out like clothes,
but you will last forever.
12You will roll them up
like a robe
and change them
like a garment.
But you are always the same,
and you will live forever.”
13 God never said to any
of the angels,
“Sit at my right side
until I make your enemies
into a footstool for you!”
14 Angels are merely spirits sent to serve people who are going to be saved.
translation_text:
Aya apani aki blɔ gani Yebi ye aʋa
1Ni kana mɛ ɛ, Aya egu kunugu yo epoe glegle nì gani bede glegle aʋa aki blɔ baka tsitsi ba gani yɛ beboedoplɛ ba aʋa. 2Pɔ̀ ní ewe gɔgɔa la ya mɛ ɛ, egu kunugu aki blɔ gani Yebi ye aʋa a. Yɛ Aya akya si ɔŋa kidɔ kakaa aʋa, lɛ yɛ onu ɔniliɛ gi yɛ aʋa Aya aga a, xe abitɛ kelea a. 3Aya Yebi ye yɛ onu Aya yɛ kusudraa gì kwidɔ ní Aya yɛ kuklekleo me e, lɛ yɛ kee onu Aya yɛ kizezee me tutu gi yɛ aki kelea kalɛ gani yɛ liboe le kigaglaɛ mɛ ɛ. Lɛ gi akɔ isu ya akɔ aki Aya a, ŋwisi bidɔ pɔʋi pɔʋi kinɛ ɛ, xe eplo eboe zimizi na azanɔ pɔ́ ɛ, odi ni Aya yɛ kigaglaɛ petee kɛ kaŋawla mɛ ní lifunu ne.
Aya Yebi ye emu asani Aya yɛ baxwɛdzɛ ba
4Lɛ loso Aya abitɛ yɛ si emu asani Aya yɛ baxwɛdzɛ ba petee ŋwisi kiliɛ gi linyii nɛ ɛ, gi aki yɛ ɛ, liŋa kedzi sani ba enyi na petee. 5Lɛ ese linu si
Aya yɛ baxwɛdzɛ ba mɛ owoli bɛsi edi si,
“Mobi wonu,
omono liwe le medzi wɔ Ɔka a”?
Xe asi yɛ dzɛ si i,
“Mɛ maadzi Ɔka ki yɛ,
lɛ yɛ fɛ ɛ, aadzi mɛ obinyime e”?
6Lɛ xegi Aya edzini yebinyime e gi ɔlɛ ɛ, xe ableke bebu kidɔ kakaa, amani kelea mɛ ɛ, Aya apani si,
“Lilɛ si Aya yɛ baxwɛdzɛ ba petee
bakpɛ litukpo kese aki yɛ.”
7Lɛ Aya apani ní yɛ baxwɛdzɛ ba su si,
“Aya abitɛ yɛ baxwɛdzɛ ba ŋwisi liwɔ lɛ kinɛ ɛ,
lɛ abitɛ yɛ banɔsumu ba ŋwisi kifu binemi kinɛ ɛ,”
8lɛ apani ní Yebi ye su si,
“Aya wɔ kaʋaŋaa kiaze tsinɔ kanɔmɛ anɔmɛ.
wɔaŋa wɔakɔanɛ ma aʋa ní
kude gyɔgyɔ aʋa.
9Wɔdimɛ ni eboa la gi ɛgyɔ; lɛ wɔ́dimɛ ni eboe nyani nyani i.
Lɛ loso Aya wɔ Aya, akya wɔ
xe aki wɔ ozekɔ gi emu isani
izekɔɛ lɛ klɔ gi aki wɔ
ahanuglɔ ba, lɛ wɔmɔ̀ isami gi Aya asra kumu yɔ aki wɔ ɛ.”
10Apani kyɛ si,
“Oo Ɔkaɛ, wɔ wɔbitɛ kesea dɔni okpese klɔ,
lɛ lifunu ne linu wɔ iwla onu axwɛwawa.
11Bidɔ bɛ ya petee bɛ onu biaza, pɔ̀ wɔkɔ ɛ, wɔaze tsinɔ;
lɛ bɛpɔ́ biahá ŋwisi kusa kinɛ ɛ,
12Wɔaklɔʋi bɛ ŋwisi kiliɛ gi bizɛklɔʋi liwu kinɛ ɛ; lɛ wɔatsrɛ ʋɛ ŋwisi kiliɛ gi bizɛɛtsrɛ basa koko kinɛ ɛ.
pɔ̀ wɔkɔ ɛ, nite ko wɔlɛ,
lɛ wɔ agɔ la onu atráza a.”
13Aya yɛ ɔxwɛdzɛ woli Aya asɛ edi si,
“Ze ní mɛ kaŋaa mɛ,
kɔzɛna ni lipoe lɛ gi maakɔ wɔ ahwafianɛ ma kpɛ ní wɔ kuklise o?”
14Lɛ ege Aya yɛ baxwɛdzɛ ba benu u? Ba petee aní ifuɛfuɛ gi izesumu Aya a, xe izɛɛkpɛ lɛ si izɛɛpɔni banama gi biamɔ kubúbú na a?

english_text:
This Great Way of Being Saved
1We must give our full attention to what we were told, so we won't drift away. 2The message spoken by angels proved to be true, and all who disobeyed or rejected it were punished as they deserved. 3So if we refuse this great way of being saved, how can we hope to escape? The Lord himself was the first to tell about it, and people who heard the message proved to us that it was true. 4God himself showed that his message was true by working all kinds of powerful miracles and wonders. He also gave his Holy Spirit to anyone he chose to.
The One Who Leads Us To Be Saved
5We know that God did not put the future world under the power of angels. 6 Somewhere in the Scriptures someone says to God,
“What makes you care
about us humans?
Why are you concerned
for weaklings such as we?
7You made us lower
than the angels
for a while.
Yet you have crowned us
with glory and honor.
8And you have put everything
under our power!”
God has put everything under our power and has not left anything out of our power. But we still don't see it all under our control. 9What we do see is Jesus, who for a little while was made lower than the angels. Because of God's gift of undeserved grace, Jesus died for everyone. And now that Jesus has suffered and died, he is crowned with glory and honor!
10Everything belongs to God, and all things were created by his power. So God did the right thing when he made Jesus perfect by suffering, as Jesus led many of God's children to be saved and to share in his glory. 11Jesus and the people he makes holy all belong to the same family. This is why he isn't ashamed to call them his brothers and sisters. 12 He even said to God,
“I will tell them your name
and sing your praises
when they come together
to worship.”
13 He also said,
“I will trust God.”
Then he said,
“Here I am with the children
God has given me.”
14We are people of flesh and blood. This is why Jesus became one of us. He died to destroy the devil, who had power over death. 15But he also died to rescue all of us who live each day in fear of dying. 16 Jesus clearly did not come to help angels, but he did come to help Abraham's descendants. 17He had to be one of us, so he could serve God as our merciful and faithful high priest and sacrifice himself for the forgiveness of our sins. 18And now that Jesus has suffered and was tempted, he can help anyone else who is tempted.
translation_text:
Aya yɛ aabitɛ lɛ si banima biamɔ kubúbú
1Lɛ ya loso lilɛ ɛki blɔ si kuvù anukwarɛɛ liɛ gi kwinu ní awla gaglaa sanɔ ɔ, atɔ kukílila ní kudeo aʋa a. 2Lɛ ese linu si, xegi eboa la a, gi Aya yɛ baxwɛdzɛ ba bɛpani i, ki blɔ baka koko ɛnya ɔnɔ kakaa ɛ, xe banama a, gi bábitɛ ní la aʋa loo nu kutu ki la a, bɛɛzi kutuhehe uliɔ gi kwigyeno e, 3kitɛ kwiabitɛ xe kwiase ní lɛ onu u, xe kwiyurɔ kubúbú ʋidi uliɔ ya a? Ɔkaɛ ɔtutɔ lɔ aklá ní kubúbú uliɔ ya su lɛ banama gi benu yɛ liboe le e, bɛtrɔ likla ní lɛ aʋa ɛki blɔ si linu anukwarɛ 4gi liitrɛ lɛ aʋa ɛ, Aya ɔtutɔ fɛ atrɔ ba liboe le aʋa a, gi abitɛ enugutseboe ni eboe mamɔ edi i, gi aki banima Ɔfuɛfuɛ Dradra lɔ mɛ bidɔ glegle ní yɛ ɔtutɔ yɛ kupɛpɛ onu u.
Ɔniliɛ gi aaki blɔ Kubúbú i
5Aya ɔ́kɔ kelea lia gi kɛtraba ɛ ɔ́kɔ ka akpɛ ní yɛ baxwɛdzɛ ba awla si baŋa ka aʋa a, kelea lia gi ka su kwipani i. 6Pɔ̀ ɔnutɔ aŋa katsalia ní ɔkutɔ si i,
“Ege ɔnɔ gbagbe onu xe wuzɛɛplɛ aŋu ní yɛ aʋa a,
kelea lia mɛ obi, xe wɔkɔ litɔ ní lɛ mɛ ɔki yɛ ɛ?
7Wɔki si yɛ ozeklɔ eple taʋɛ sani wɔ baxwɛdzɛ ba ɔni nɔ ɔ,
wɔki yɛ kusudra ni kubùbù lɛ wɔkɔ yɛ odu wɔ ɔwla lɔ onu axwɛwawa la petee onu i,
8wɔki si ɔŋa kidɔ kakaa aʋa a.”
Gi Aya abitɛ yɛ si ɔŋa kidɔ kakaa aʋa ɛ, kididɔ kígɔ gi Aya ɔ́kɔkpɛ ni yɛ ese e. Pɔ̀ kuʋímɔ si yɛ ɛɛŋa kidɔ kakaa aʋa abla a. 9Pɔ̀ kwimɔ Yesu, ɔniliɛ gi yɛ ozekɔ bepleno esani Aya yɛ baxwɛdzɛ ba, atɔ gani Aya yɛ lipomepɛpɛ nɛ mɛ ɛ, otse ní ɔnɔ kakaa litukpo. Abla ɛ, kwimɔ yɛ si bɛki yɛ kusudra ni kubùbù kɔkpɛ yɛ kutseo mɛ gi aga ɛ.
10Lipɛ si Aya gi yɛ abitɛ kidɔ kakaa ɛ, xe yɛ aki si kidɔ kakaa kize kiliɛ ɛ, gi kilɛ ɛ, ɔki tɔ ɔniliɛ gi yɛ onu ba kubúbú yo kɔ ɔxɔ lɔ ɛ, ɔna gani ɔziã mɛ atɔ yɛ ɔgbani bebi glegle manɔ ní yɛ kusudraa mɛ. Lɛ ese linu si Yesu yɛ onu ɔniliɛ gi yɛ aagbani ba tsinɔ ní kubúbú yo me e.
11Lɛ ese linu si ɔniliɛ gi izɛɛdra banima su i, kɔpɔni banama su gi bɛdra ɛ, ba petee bɛdɔ lipo tile me, lɛ loso e, kuye kúʋiku Yesu si ɔxwa ba si yɛ benemi i, 12Apani kyɛ si,
“maadra wɔ linyi ne su ki mɛ benemi ba;
lɛ ní baʋaziŋa ba ɔha lɔ kusi i, maakpɔ wɔ gani idzi zàzà mɛ.”
13Lɛ apani dzɛ si,
“Maaka ní yɛ su aʋa”
Lɛ edzì apani si,
“Imɔ mɛ, ni bebi ba ya gi Aya aki mɛ ɛ.”
14Lɛ gi bebi ba benu kisugya ni ika mɛ banɔ ɛ, Yesu ɔtutɔ kyɛ edzì ŋwisi ba kiniɛ lɛ eze ligbã́ ni kisugyaɛ mɛ ba kiniɛ. Abitɛ lɛ nite tɔ gani yɛ kutseo me e, aazizi ɔniliɛ gi yɛ awla kutseo kigaglaɛ kilɛ ɛ, gi yɛ onu ozimizi ye e. 15Lɛ gani yɛ kutseo me e, aabu banama petee gi ní ba ligbã́ nɛ mɛ ɛ, beze ŋwisi banɔdzidzi ki ozimizi ye ɔkpɛ gi bezeese se kutseo loso o. 16Lɛ mɛ lidra si, aní Aya yɛ baxwɛdzɛ ba apɔnɔ ɔ, pɔ̀ Abraham yɛ besoebi ba apɔnɔ ɔ. 17Ɔkpɛ lɛ ya loso e, lilɛ si Aya ɔbitɛ yɛ ŋwisi yɛ benemi ba ní kude kakaa onu, tɔ aadzi Aya yɛ kusumu yo ɔka ʋidi ye, gi aadi banima asigaga a, anukwarɛ onete ní kiliɛ gi izeesumu Aya mɛ ɛ, gi aakɔ̀ isu ya kukyɛ ní banima litukpo si biakɔ ba eboe zimizi na kɔ kyɛ ba. 18Lɛ abla ɛ, aatani banama gi beekeni idi i, kupɔnɔ lɛ ese linu si yɛ ɔtutɔ lɔ fɛ bekeni ye edi lɛ amɔ ɔziã a.

english_text:
Jesus Is Greater than Moses
1My friends, God has chosen you to be his holy people. So think about Jesus, the one we call our apostle and high priest! 2 Jesus was faithful to God, who appointed him, just as Moses was faithful in serving all God's people. 3But Jesus deserves more honor than Moses, just as the builder of a house deserves more honor than the house. 4Of course, every house is built by someone, and God is really the one who built everything.
5Moses was a faithful servant and told God's people what would be said in the future. 6But Christ is the Son in charge of God's people. And we are those people, if we keep on being brave and don't lose hope.
A Rest for God's People
7 It is just as the Holy Spirit says,
“If you hear God's voice today,
8don't be stubborn!
Don't rebel like those people
who were tested
in the desert.
* 9 For forty years your ancestors
tested God and saw
the things he did.
10“Then God got tired of them
and said,
‘You people never
show good sense,
and you don't understand
what I want you to do.’
11God became angry
and told the people,
‘You will never enter
my place of rest!’ ”
12My friends, watch out! Don't let evil thoughts or doubts make any of you turn from the living God. 13You must encourage one another each day. And you must keep on while there is still a time that can be called “today.” If you don't, then sin may fool some of you and make you stubborn. 14We were sure about Christ when we first became his people. So let's hold tightly to our faith until the end. 15 The Scriptures say,
“If you hear his voice today,
don't be stubborn
like those who rebelled.”
16 Who were those people that heard God's voice and rebelled? Weren't they the same ones that came out of Egypt with Moses? 17Who were the people that made God angry for 40 years? Weren't they the ones that sinned and died in the desert? 18And who did God say would never enter his place of rest? Weren't they the ones that disobeyed him? 19We see that those people did not enter the place of rest because they did not have faith.
translation_text:
Yesu emu asani Mose
1Lɛ loso mɛ benemi dradra ba, mlɔ banama kyɛ gi Aya axwa a, mlakɔ mlɔ aŋu na atrɔ ní Yesu aʋa, ɔniliɛ gi Aya akpɛ ɛ, si oze kusumu yo Ɔka ʋidi ye, gi yɛ aapɔni blɔ ní kidɔ kakaa mɛ ɛ, gi yɛ su kwidra ni blɔ inugu le e. 2Abitɛ kidɔ kakaa ní kude gbã onu gi Aya a, gi yɛ akya yɛ ɛ, asi yɛ si ɔbitɛ ŋwisi kiliɛ gi Mose abitɛ ní kude gbã onu ní Aya yɛ kepea petee me kinɛ ɛ. 3Lɛ ese linu si kubùbù glegle kulɛ ní Yesu su i, sani Mose ŋwisi kiliɛ gi ɔgbawaɛ izɛzi kubùbù isani kepea ɔtutɔ lɔ ɛ. 4Lɛ ese linu si ligba kakaa ɛ, ɔnɔ izɛɛwa lɛ, pɔ̀ Aya yɛ izɛɛbitɛ kidɔ kakaa a.
5Mose abitɛ kidɔ kakaa ní kude gbã onu ní Aya yɛ kepea petee me ŋwisi ɔnɔsumu kinɛ ɛ, lɛ apani ní eboe gi la su Aya aapani ní ɔtɔ nɔ mɛ ɛ su. 6Pɔ̀ Kristo abitɛ kidɔ kakaa ní kude gbã onu ŋwisi obi gi yɛ iwla mɛ Aya yɛ kepea kalɛ ɛ kinɛ. Lɛ blɔ kunu yɛ kepea, xegi kwivu blɔ ina nɛ ani kudeonudidi yo awla ní kidiliɛ gi kɛ loso kwifeke isu ya ɛ.
Bɛgba Israel bebi ba ní kumɔziŋa su
7Lɛ loso kiliɛ gi Aya yɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ ɛpani i si,
“xegi mlenu Aya yɛ ole lo omono e,
8mlɔ ina nɛ mɛ ikízee gagla,
kiliɛ gi mlɔ bakatsi ba bɛbitɛ gi bɛyɔ kedu ya ní mɛ su i,
kiliɛ gi beze liwe lɛ klɔ ní ɔyaya klɔ ɔ, gi bekeni me edi kinɛ ɛ.
9Niklɔ gi mlɔ baka ba bekeni me edi i, xe befeke me edi i,
gi bɛɛmɔ kidiliɛ gi mabitɛ agɔ avitene na kyɛ ɛ.
10Lɛ loso e, meyeʋi isu ní kigyɔgyɔɛ mɛ liɛ klɔ aʋa
lɛ mapani si ‘ba ina nɛ imá ni mɛ kude
lɛ béte mɛ bede ʋa’
11Lɛ loso maka kitamu aki isu ní mɛ kusuyeʋio me si,
‘Batrátiiku ní mɛ kusuyɔɔ mɛ liwewe!’ ”
12Benemi ba, mledi isu aʋa mumɔ tɔ ɔna zimizi gi ɔ́zɛ ɔŋa ɛ, okize ní mlɔ mɛ ɛtɔtɔ mɛ, tɔ mláʋakyi kede kpɛ Aya gi yɛ izɛki kidɔ kakaa ligbã nɛ ɛ. 13Boŋu i, mlazɛɛkpɛ mlɔ ahanuglɔ ba kigaglaɛ liwe kakaa liwe, gi bɛɛxwa lɛ si omono e, atɔ eboe zimizi atɔtɔ akímeni mlɔ mɛ ɔtɔtɔ. 14Lɛ ese linu si mlaamɔ ɔŋakɔ nì Kristo, xegi mlevu kidiliɛ ɛ, gi kɛ kudeonu kwidi i, lipoe lɛ gi kwizi ŋa ɛ, kɛ mɛ kɔzɛna nì ɔgukɔ ɛ. 15Ŋwisi kiliɛ gi bɛpani zanɔ si i,
“Xegi mleenu Aya yɛ ole lo omono e,
mlɔ ina nɛ ikízee gagla
ŋwisi mlɔ bakatsi ba kiniɛ ɛ, gi bɛyɔ kedu ní mɛ su i.”
16Lɛ nyaŋwɛ ba benu Aya yɛ ole lo o, xe bɛyɔ kedu ya ní yɛ su u? Aní banama petee gi Mose agbani adɔ nɔ ní Egipte na a? 17Kɔ nyaŋwɛ ba su Aya eyeʋi isu agɔ avitene zizi na ɛ? Aní banama gi bɛbitɛ eboe zimizi na a, xe betse ní ɔyaya klɔ ɛ. 18Lɛ nyaŋwɛ ba Aya aka yɛ kitamu yɛ akɔ si, batrátiiku ní yɛ kusuyɔɔ mɛ liwewe e, xe ani banɔ ba ya gi bénu kutu aki yɛ ɛ? 19Lɛ kwiba ɛmɔ egyesi si, bátani kesea mɛ kuti iku kɔkpɛ gi báziŋa loso o.

english_text:
1The promise to enter the place of rest is still good, and we must take care that none of you miss out. 2We have heard the message, just as they did. But they failed to believe what they heard, and the message did not do them any good. 3 Only people who have faith will enter the place of rest. It is just as the Scriptures say,
“God became angry
and told the people,
‘You will never enter
my place of rest!’ ”
God said this, even though everything has been ready from the time of creation. 4 In fact, somewhere the Scriptures say that by the seventh day, God had finished his work, and so he rested. 5 We also read that he later said, “You people will never enter my place of rest!” 6This means that the promise to enter is still good, because those who first heard about it disobeyed and did not enter. 7 Much later God told David to make the promise again, just as I have already said,
“If you hear his voice today,
don't be stubborn!”
8 If Joshua had really given the people rest, there would not be any need for God to talk about another day of rest. 9But God has promised us a Sabbath when we will rest, even though it has not yet come. 10 On that day God's people will rest from their work, just as God rested from his work.
11We should do our best to enter the place of rest, so none of us will disobey and miss going there, as they did. 12God's word is alive and powerful! It is sharper than any double-edged sword. His word can cut through our spirits and souls and through our joints and marrow, until it discovers the desires and thoughts of our hearts. 13 Nothing is hidden from God! He sees through everything, and we will have to tell him the truth.
Jesus Is the Great High Priest
14We have a great high priest, who has gone into heaven, and he is Jesus the Son of God. This is why we must hold on to what we have said about him. 15Jesus understands every weakness of ours, because he was tempted in every way that we are. But he did not sin! 16So whenever we are in need, we should come bravely before the throne of our merciful God. There we will be treated with undeserved grace, and we will find help.
translation_text:
1Lɛ loso gi Aya yɛ kakaplɛa kedzia lɛ si kwiatiku ní yɛ kusuyɔɔ mɛ ɛ, mlaki tɔ kudi isu aʋa mumɔ, tɔ likíbase si mlɔ mɛ ɔtɔ ebu isu ní lɛ mɛ ɛ. 2Lɛ ese linu si bɛpani Liboe Gbã nɛ si blɔ fɛ, ŋwisi banɔ baklɔ ba ke; pɔ̀ liboe le gi benu i, líbu libiitɔtɔ ɛki ba banɔ baklɔ, lɛ ese linu si banama gi benu le e, bákɔ kuziŋa apɔni lɛ ɛ. 3Lɛ loso blɔ banama gi kwizi ŋa ɛ, kwiatiku kusuyɔ liɔ klɔ mɛ, ŋwisi kiliɛ gi Aya aka aplɛ ɛ si, “Lilɛ ko ŋwisi kiliɛ gi maka kitamu yɛ ní mɛ kusuyeʋio mɛ si; ‘batrátiiku ní mɛ kusuyɔɔ mɛ liwewe.’ ” Pɔ̀ kyɛ Aya yɛ lixwɛ nɛ liblo dɔni kelea okpese klɔ ke. 4Lɛ ese linu si bɛpani ní yɛ Liboe le kapɛ katɔ mɛ ní liwe gloele liwe le su si, “Lɛ ní liwe gloele aʋa ɛ, Aya eze amɔni ní yɛ axwɛ na petee me e.” 5Lɛ bɛpani niklɔ dzɛ si, “batrátiiku ní mɛ kusuyɔɔ mɛ liwewe e.”
6Kudeonu kudzi ulɛ ki banɔ batɔ si beti iku ní kɔ mɛ, Pɔ̀ banama gi beedo Liboe Gbã nɛ ese kpɛ ɔtɔ ɛ, bázɛ kuzemɔni yɔ kɔkpɛ gi bénu kutu loso o. 7Lɛ loso Aya atrɔ liwe dzedze dzɛ gi bɛxwa si omono o, gi ní lipoe gblamadzaɛtɔ kede e, apani lɛ su agani Dawid aʋa ní Aya Liboe le kapɛa lia su gi kwipani kooko si i;
“Omono xe mlenu Aya yɛ ole lo e,
mlɔ ina nɛ mɛ ikíze gagla a.”
8Xegi nya Yosua aki ba kuzemɔni uliɔ ya ɛ, nya Aya ɔ́trapani lɛ kede ní liwe dzedze etɔtɔ su tɔɔ ɔ. 9Lɛ loso Lizemɔniwe lilɛ ɛki Aya yɛ banima. 10Lɛ ese linu si banɔ baklɔ gi bɛzi kuzemɔni uliɔ klɔ ɔ gi Aya aka aplɛ ɛ, biazemɔni ní batutɔ axwɛwawa la mɛ ŋwisi kiliɛ gi Aya eze amɔni ní yɛ ani na mɛ kinɛ ɛ.
11Lɛ loso e, mlaki tɔ kuga kude kakaa onu tɔ kutiku ní kuzemɔni uliɔ klɔ mɛ tɔ blɔ mɛ ɔnɔnɔ ɔkido ní lɛ mɛ ŋwisi banɔ baklɔ ba kɔkpɛ ba kutu gaglaa loso o.
12Lɛ ese linu si Aya yɛ Liboe le lilɛ ligbã́, lɛ lizɛwa lɛ axwɛ na kise ise, lɛ lilɛ nunu isani ihɛ inugu tiʋa kakaa a. Lɛ lizɛɛtsa bidɔ ɛtsinɔ kimɛ ɛmɛ ke, kɔzɛna ni esobo la ni Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ ɔtsaklɔ, ani axwa la ni kumu yɔ itsaklɔ ke e. Lɛ Aya yɛ Liboe le lizɛɛxwa etsi ni ɔna nɔ mɛ bapɛ́pɛ ʋa ni begyodi ʋa kyɛ ɛ. 13Kidɔbitɛbitɛ ɛtɔtɔ kítratani kukpo ní Aya aŋu mɛ ɛ, kidɔ kakaa gi bɛbitɛ ɛ, kifini liya lɛ kifini ni yɛ aŋu mɛ ɛ. Lɛ yɛ blɔ petee kwitraki akɔnta ni isu ya su ikɔ ɔ.
Yesu yɛ onu blɔ Kusumu yo Ɔka ʋidi ye
14Lɛ loso gi kusumu yo Ɔka ʋidi ye e, gi ɔlɛ ní blɔ awla a, yɛ atrɛ ní bekpo ʋa mɛ xe atrɛ ní lifunu ne ní Aya aŋu mɛ ɛ, Yesu Aya Yebi ye, mlaki tɔ kuvù blɔ kuziŋaa liɔ gi kwibasɛ banima ɛ mɛ gaglaa. 15Lɛ ese linu si aní Ɔka ʋidi gi ɔ́tranu kuyeʋiyeʋi kí blɔ kubotsobotsoo ɔlɛ ní blɔ awla a, boŋu i, onu kusɔliɔ Ɔka ʋidi gi befeke ye edi gani kude kakaa onu ŋwisi kiliɛ gi bizeefeke blo edi kinɛ ɛ, pɔ̀ eboe zimizi eze ní yɛ su u. 16Lɛ loso e, mlaki si ɔna oze ní blɔ lipo atɔ kutipɔni Aya yɛ lipomepɛpɛ nɛ likusigbo le, tɔ blɔ ɔwla lɔ ɔka yɛ asigagadidi la, atɔ kwiamɔ yɛ lipomepɛpɛ nɛ gi liapɔni blɔ ɔ, xe kwizia yɛ kupɔnɔ ɛ.

english_text:
1Every high priest is appointed to help others by offering gifts and sacrifices to God because of their sins. 2A high priest has weaknesses of his own, and he feels sorry for foolish and sinful people. 3 This is why he must offer sacrifices for his own sins and for the sins of others. 4 But no one can have the honor of being a high priest simply by wanting to be one. Only God can choose a priest, and God is the one who chose Aaron.
5 This is how it was with Christ. He became a high priest, but not just because he wanted the honor of being one. It was God who told him,
“You are my Son, because today
I have become your Father!”
6 In another place, God says,
“You are a priest forever
just like Melchizedek.”
7 God had the power to save Jesus from death. And while Jesus was on earth, he begged God with loud crying and tears to save him. He truly worshiped God, and God listened to his prayers. 8Jesus is God's own Son, but still he had to suffer before he could learn what it really means to obey God. 9Suffering made Jesus perfect, and now he can save forever all who obey him. 10This is because God chose him to be a high priest like Melchizedek.
Warning against Turning Away
11Much more could be said about this subject. But it is hard to explain, and all of you are slow to understand. 12 By now you should have been teachers, but once again you need to be taught the simplest things about what God has said. You need milk instead of solid food. 13People who live on milk are like babies who don't really know what is right. 14Solid food is for mature people who have been trained to know right from wrong.
translation_text:
1Kusumu yo Ɔka ʋidi kakaa, gi bebu idɔnɔ ní banima kusi i, bedu ye si oze ní ba onu tɔ osumu Aya tɔ ɔki Aya bidɔ pɔʋi pɔʋi ni bidɔ kɔkɔ ní ba eboe zimizi na litukpo. 2Agi yɛ ɔtutɔ kyɛ ebotso ní bede glegle me e, aatani litɔ kuplɛ kese ki banama gi báʋite e, xe bizɛɛta kese e. 3Lɛ loso gi ebotso e, lilɛ si ɔpɔʋi bidɔ ɔki Aya aní ní banima eboe zimizi na tètè loso o, pɔ̀ ki yɛ ɔtutɔ yɛ eboe zimizi na kyɛ loso o.
4Lɛ ɔnɔnɔ ɔ́ʋikya kusumu yo Ɔka ʋidi ye yɛ kubùbù yo ɔki isu u, Aya ko izɛɛxwa ɔnɔ si oze kusumu yo Ɔka ʋidi ŋwisi kiliɛ gi axwa Aron kinɛ ɛ. 5Nite ke Kristo fɛ ɔ́kɔ kubùbù yo ɔ́ki isu ya ŋwisi kusumu yo Ɔka ʋidi kinɛ ɛ, boŋu i, Aya asi yɛ si,
“Mɛ obi wonu;
Omono e, medzi wɔ Ɔka.”
6Apani ní ɔkɔ dzedze kyɛ si,
“Wɔaze kusumu yo Ɔka kɔtsinɔ kanɔmɛ anɔmɛ
ŋwisi kiliɛ gi Melxizedek eze kinɛ ɛ.”
7Ni yɛ kesea aʋa ligbã́ nɛ mɛ ɛ, Yesu azaaxwa Aya lɛ azɛɛkɔ yɛ beʋiʋi ʋa aki yɛ ni ozi gagla ni kube e, gi yɛ aatani yɛ kubu dɔni kutse me e. Lɛ gi abùbɔ isu ya a, xe ɔkɔ kubùbù aki yɛ onu i, Aya ata yɛ kutu. 8Lɛ gi onu Aya Yebi kyɛ ɛ, akpasi kutununu gani yɛ ɔziã nɔ gi amɔ ɛ, mɛ. 9Lɛ gi abitɛ kidɔ kakaa a, gi lilɛ si ɔbitɛ pɔ́ ɛ, edzì ɔniliɛ gi yɛ aʋa liga a, xe kwimɔ ligbã́ moblo le, blɔ banama petee gi kuzeenu kutu ki yɛ ɛ. 10Lɛ Aya axwa yɛ si yɛ onu kusumu yo Ɔka ʋidi ye ní kudeo liɔ aʋa gi Melxizedek eze ŋwisi kusumu yo Ɔka kinɛ ɛ.
Mlakídzɛ kede ní kuziŋaa su
11Eboe glegle alɛ gi kwiapani ní liboe lɛ ya su u, pɔ̀ lɛ ese kununu yo kwiazee gagla ki mlɔ ɔ, lɛ ese linu si mlaʋítani eboe la ese kunu kise ise. 12Lɛ ese linu si nya lipoe le lina gi lilɛ si mledzi badɔbasɛ xe e, pɔ̀ mledzizia si ɔnutɔ ɔbasɛ mlɔ eboe tɔpu na ní Aya yɛ Liboe le su dzɛ ɛ. Gi nya mlaazɛŋa bidɔ nyipu abla ɛ, mledzizia kumwani yɔ dzɛ. 13Lɛ ese linu si ɔnɔ kakaa gi izɛɛmwa ɛ, odzi onu obivigye lɛ ɔ́ʋina niklɔ gi aanu kudɔbasɛ yɔ ní kiliɛ gi aaze ligbã́ ní kude gyɔgyɔ aʋa su u. 14Pɔ̀ bidɔ nyipu ʋe e, bilɛ ɛki banɔtsitsi ba gi gani eboa la mɛ gi bɛɛga mɛ ɛ, xe bɛzɛɛkɔ ehe isu ya lipoe kakaa ɛ, betè lidzegaga ní eboe gbã ni eboe zimizi kusi.

english_text:
1We must try to become mature and start thinking about more than just the basic things we were taught about Christ. We shouldn't need to keep talking about why we ought to turn from deeds that bring death and why we ought to have faith in God. 2And we shouldn't need to keep teaching about baptisms or about the laying on of hands or about people being raised from death and the future judgment. 3Let's grow up, if God is willing.
4-6But what about people who turn away after they have already seen the light and have received the gift from heaven and have shared in the Holy Spirit? What about those who turn away after they have received the good message of God and the powers of the future world? There is no way to bring them back. What they are doing is the same as nailing the Son of God to a cross and insulting him in public!
7A field is useful to farmers, if there is enough rain to make good crops grow. In fact, God will bless such a field. 8 But land that produces only thornbushes is worthless. It is likely to fall under God's curse, and in the end it will be set on fire.
9My friends, we are talking this way. But we are sure that you are doing those really good things people do when they are being saved. 10God is always fair. He will remember how you helped his people in the past and how you are still helping them. You belong to God, and he won't forget the love you have shown his people. 11We wish each of you would always be eager to show how strong and lasting your hope really is. 12Then you would never be lazy. You would be following the example of those who had faith and were patient until God kept his promise to them.
God's Promise Is Sure
13No one is greater than God. So he made a promise in his own name when he said to Abraham, 14 “I, the Lord, will bless you with many descendants!” 15Then after Abraham had been very patient, he was given what God had promised. 16When anyone wants to settle an argument, they make a vow by using the name of someone or something greater than themselves. 17So when God wanted to prove for certain his promise to his people could not be broken, he made a vow. 18God cannot tell lies! And so his promises and vows are two things that can never be changed.
We have run to God for safety. Now his promises should greatly encourage us to take hold of the hope that is right in front of us. 19 This hope is like a firm and steady anchor for our souls. In fact, hope reaches behind the curtain and into the most holy place. 20 Jesus has gone there ahead of us, and he is our high priest forever, just like Melchizedek.
translation_text:
1Lɛ loso mlaki tɔ kutrɛ ɔtɔ nɔ ní kudɔbasɛ uliɔ gi kwidzi ɔtɔ mɛ ɛ, atɔ kuyurɔ bidɔbasɛbasɛ tɔpu imɛ gi kwikɔ ekpese liboe le ní Kristo su u. Líma si kudzini ɔxɔ vuvu kukpɛ ɛ, gi onu kukyikyi dɔni eboe zimizi i, gi izɛmani kutse me e, ani Aya aʋa kuziŋa mɛ. 2Kɔpɔnɔ ɛ, kudɔbasɛ ní kunikpɛkpɛ ni awla kutrɔtrɔ banɔ aʋa su, kuyɔyɔ dɔni bekpo ʋa mɛ, ni kiliɛ gi Aya yɛ litsixwaxwa nɛ ɛ, ni yɛ litsi le kutsàtsaa kútrakyi i, su. 3Xe Aya aki blɔ kudeonu i, nite kwitrabitɛ.
4Límaɛ plɔplɔ si bedzini banama a, gi ba aŋu na ɛdra edi i, amanɔ si bekyi ɔna mɛ dzɛ, lɛ ese linu si beze ní Aya yɛ kuklekleo me edi, lɛ bɛzi lifunu ne bidɔkɔkɔ ʋɛ edi, lɛ bɛzi ba kapɛa ni Ɔfuɛfuɛ Dradra lɔ mɛ ɛ, 5lɛ bete kidɔ gbãɛ liɛ gi kilɛ ní Aya yɛ liboe le me e, ani kigaglaɛ liɛ gi kiaba gani Aya yɛ kaʋaŋaa lia a, gi kɛtraba mɛ ɛ, 6pɔ̀ xe bɛɛdzi kede ní ba kuziŋaa mɛ ɛ, límaɛ plɔplɔ si bedzini ba amanɔ si bekyi ɔna mɛ dzɛ, lɛ ese linu si bɛɛkla Aya Yebi ye ní ose lo su dzɛ, lɛ bɛɛkpɛ yɛ kakpaa ní ɔha kusi i.
7Aya yɛ izɛɛkrira kesea lia gi kizɛɛŋwɛ dzeni ye kuni yo o, gi kuzɛɛdɔ ɛbali ka aʋa a, xe kizɛɛyɛ bidɔ kpɛkpɛɛ ʋɛ ɛ, gi bilɛ veve ki banama a, gi ba loso bɛkpɛ ʋɛ ɛ. 8Pɔ̀ kesea gi kɛyɛ bibu ni siwa sɛ ɛ, kɔ kébu libi tɔtɔ, lɛ kalɛ ní kuxixa mɛ si Aya aaxo ifi kpɛ ka kɔ gɔgɔ ɛ, kifu biakpɛ ka.
9Pɔ̀ mɛ baʋia dimɛnɔ ba a, xe kwipani nite kyɛ ɛ, ní mlɔ kapɛa mɛ ɛ, kwite si eboe gbã gi aaki mlɔ kubúbú i, aagbani mlɔ kpɛ. 10Lɛ ese linu si Aya ɔlɛ ní yɛ kude gyɔgyɔɔ aʋa, lɛ ɔ́tradze axwɛ ana gi mlɛwa a, loo kiliɛ gi mlɛlɔ yɛ ɛ, gani kupɔnɔ liɔ gi mlɛkɔ ɔ, xe mlɛɛki baʋaziŋa ba a. 11Linu blɔ kupɛ́pɛ ʋidi si mlɔ mɛ ɔkakaa okeni odi nì litɔplɛplɛkese zɛɛkɔna ni lɛ ɔgù kɔ tɔ mlaamɔ kidiliɛ gi kɛ kudeonu mleedi i. 12Kúpɛ si mledzi basranɛ ɛ, boŋu i, mlakpasi banama gi bɛziŋa a, xe bɛplɛ litɔ kese e, tɔ biazɛ kidiliɛ gi kɛ Aya aka aplɛ ɛ.
Aya yɛ kakaplɛa kiaba ka mɛ
13Lipoe lɛ gi Aya aka aplɛ aki Abraham i, lɛ gi ɔnɔ dzedze ɔtɔtɔ ɔ́ma a, gi emu asani yɛ ɛ, gi yɛ linyi ne aakɔ̀ ka kitamu yɛ onu i, aka kitamu yɛ ní yɛ ɔtutɔ lɔ linyi ne me, 14lɛ apani si, “Maakrira wɔ kikakaa elue, lɛ maaki wɔ besoebi glegle.” 15Lɛ gi Abraham aplɛ litɔ kese e, xe epe Aya lipoe zizi i, Aya yɛ kakaplɛa kɛka yɛ awla.
16Xegi banima bɛka kitamu i, bizɛɛka kɛ ni linyi nɛ gi limu isani ba lini nɛ ɛ, kɔ kuboehe kakaa kwitsa ɛ.
17Lɛ ki banama gi biazɛ Aya yɛ kakaplɛa ɛ, Aya apɛ si yidra lɛ mɛ si yɛ ɔ́trakyi yɛ batrɔtrɔ ʋa liwewe e, lɛ loso aka kitamu yɛ apɔni yɛ kakaplɛ a. 18Aya abitɛ eboe taʋa la ya, gi atrátani kukyi i, lɛ ese linu si Aya ɔ́tratani asia kusɛ ɛ. Lɛ loso blɔ banɔaya gi kwise trɛ ní yɛ kɔ ɔ, si kuvu kudeonudidi uliɔ gi bɛkɔ ɛplɛ ní blɔ aŋu mɛ awla ɛ, liakpɛ ɔna ní blɔ lipo.
19Kudeonudidi uliɔ ya kulɛ ní blɔ awla gi kunu ŋwisi ɔxɔ ki blɔ agbã́ na a. Kulɛ mumɔ lɛ kulɛ gagla gi kuzɛɛtani lisɔligba lɛ mɛ kusaa mɛ kuga a, gi kwiatiku ní ɔdradra klɔ ke e. 20Niklɔ gi Yesu eku blɔ ɔtɔ atrɛ ní blɔ onu u, gi yɛ edzì kusɔliɔ Ɔka ʋidi ye ní Melxizedek yɛ ɔga nɔ onu kɔtsinɔ kanɔmɛ anɔmɛ.

english_text:
The Priestly Family of Melchizedek
1 Melchizedek was both king of Salem and priest of God Most High. He was the one who went out and gave Abraham his blessing, when Abraham returned from killing the kings. 2Then Abraham gave him a tenth of everything he had.
The meaning of the name Melchizedek is “King of Justice.” But since Salem means “peace,” he is also “King of Peace.” 3We are not told he had a father or mother or ancestors or beginning or end. He is like the Son of God and will be a priest forever.
4Notice how great Melchizedek was! Our famous ancestor Abraham gave him a tenth of what he had taken from his enemies. 5 The Law teaches that even Abraham's descendants must give a tenth of what they possess. And they are to give this to their own relatives, who are the descendants of Levi and are priests. 6Although Melchizedek wasn't a descendant of Levi, Abraham gave him a tenth of what he had. Then Melchizedek blessed Abraham, who had been given God's promise. 7Everyone agrees a person who gives a blessing is greater than the one who receives the blessing.
8Priests are given a tenth of what people earn. But all priests die, except Melchizedek, and the Scriptures teach that he is alive. 9Levi's descendants are now the ones who receive a tenth from people. We could even say that when Abraham gave Melchizedek a tenth, Levi also gave him a tenth. 10This is because Levi was born later into the family of Abraham, who gave a tenth to Melchizedek.
11Even though the Law of Moses says the priests must be descendants of Levi, those priests cannot make anyone perfect. So there needs to be a priest like Melchizedek, rather than one from the priestly family of Aaron. 12And when the rules for selecting a priest are changed, the Law must also be changed.
13The person we are talking about is our Lord, who came from a tribe that had never had anyone to serve as a priest at the altar. 14Everyone knows he came from the tribe of Judah, and Moses never said priests would come from that tribe.
15All of this becomes clearer, when someone who is like Melchizedek is appointed to be a priest. 16That person wasn't appointed because of his ancestors, but because his life can never end. 17 The Scriptures say about him,
“You are a priest forever,
just like Melchizedek.”
18In this way a weak and useless command was put aside, 19because the Law cannot make anything perfect. At the same time, we are given a much better hope, and it can bring us close to God.
20-21 God himself made a promise when this priest was appointed. But he did not make a promise like this when the other priests were appointed. The promise he made is,
“I, the Lord, promise that you
will be a priest forever!
And I will never
change my mind!”
22This means that Jesus guarantees us a better agreement with God. 23There have been a lot of other priests, and all of them have died. 24But Jesus will never die, and so he will be a priest forever! 25He is forever able to save the people he leads to God, because he always lives to speak to God for them.
26Jesus is the high priest we need. He is holy and innocent and faultless, and not at all like us sinners. Jesus is honored above all beings in heaven, 27 and he is better than any other high priest. Jesus doesn't need to offer sacrifices each day for his own sins and then for the sins of the people. He offered a sacrifice once for all, when he gave himself. 28The Law appoints priests who have weaknesses. But God's promise, which came later than the Law, appoints his Son. And he is the perfect high priest forever.
translation_text:
Melxizedek Kusɔliɔ Ɔkaɛ
1Melxizedek liɛ ɛ, yɛ eze Salem ɔma nɔ okusi ye ani Aya gi emu asani ɔnɔ kakaa ɛ, yɛ kusɔliɔ Ɔkaɛ, yɛ azɛɛtsani Abraham lipoe lɛ klɔ mɛ gi edzì adɔ ni kigũ yɛ ɛ, agi azɛɛŋa bekusi ba aʋa a, lɛ akrira yɛ ɛ. 2Lɛ Abraham akɔ bidɔ bɛ ya petee gi amɔ adɔ ni ní kigũ yɛ mɛ ɛ, bɛ kapɛ liɔfɔa aki yɛ. Melxizedek yɛ linyi ne ese ní ɔtɔpu kɔ ɛ, linu si “Okusi gi ɔlɛ ní kude gyɔgyɔ aʋa a”, Lɛ gi onu okusi ni Salɛm ɔma nɔ aʋa loso e, yɛ linyi ne ese kibubu dzedze kinu si “Kusuyɔ Okusi”. 3Lɛ báŋwlimi Melxizedek yɛka loo yene, loo yɛkatsitsi ɔtɔtɔ liboe aplɛ ɛ, lɛ kididɔ kíma ní yɛ kupɔpɔ loo kutse su u. Ɔlɛ ko ŋwisi Aya Yebi gi onu yɛ kusɔliɔ ɔka kɔtsinɔ kanɔmɛ anɔmɛ ɛ.
4Lɛ mledi ɔnɔ ʋidi liɛ elu gi Melxizedek eze e, blɔ ɔkatsitsi ye Abraham akɔ bidɔɛ mɛ gi amɔ adɔ ni kigũ yɛ ɛ, bɛ kapɛ liɔfɔa akɔ aki yɛ. 5Lɛ Lewi lipo le me bebia ba gi benu kusɔliɔ baka ba ɛ, ɔsã nɔ ɛɛki ba kude si bazɛ bidɔ mɔmɔ ʋɛ kapɛ liɔfɔa dɔni banima awla gi benu ba benemi i. Sro si ba kyɛ benu Abraham yɛ lipo me bebi xe e. 6Melxizedek ónu Levi yɛ lipo me obi i, pɔ̀ azɛ Abraham yɛ bidɔ mɛ kapɛ liɔfɔa a, lɛ akrira Abraham i, yɛ ɔniliɛ gi Aya aka aplɛ akɔ ɛ. 7Lɛ mɛ lidra si ɔniliɛ gi izɛɛkrira ɛ, ekeme asani ɔniliɛ gi bɛɛkrira ɛ. 8Ní kapɛ tiele me e, bidɔ mɛ kapɛ liɔfɔa ɛ, banama gi bizɛɛzi bɛ fɛ ɛ, betse. Pɔ̀ ní Melxizedek kapɛa mɛ ɛ, ɔniliɛ gi ɔlɛ ligbã́ nɛ kɔtsinɔ ɛ, yɛ azɛ bɛ. 9Lɛ loso biatani kupani si; gi Abraham atsa bidɔ mɛ kapɛ liɔfɔa ɛ, Lewi gi yɛ izɛɛzi kapɛ liɔfɔa ɛ, yɛ fɛ atsa bɛ gani Abraham aʋa a. 10lɛ ese linu si gi bápɔ Lewi kyɛ ɛ, eze ní Abraham yɛ lipo le me lipoe lɛ gi Abraham ni Melxizedek bɛtsa ɛ.
11Lewi yɛ lipo me bebi ba ba kusumu yo kuka ŋaŋaa kɔ onu bɛki ɔsã nɔ. Lɛ xegi nya Lewianɛ ma ba kusumu yo kuka ŋaŋaa kwina ɛ, nya lítrazia si kusumu yo ɔka dzedze ɔŋwi i, gi onu kusumu yo Ɔka ʋidi ní Melxizedek yɛ ɔga nɔ onu u, lɛ ani Aron yɛ lipo me bebi ba ɔni nɔ onu u. 12Lɛ ese linu si xegi bɛtsrɛ kusumu yo kuka ŋaŋaa ɛ, kɔ lilɛ si batsrɛ ɔsã nɔ fɛ. 13Lɛ ɔniliɛ gi yɛ su bɛpani liboe lɛ ya ɛ, ádɔ ní limade dzedze me, lɛ ɔnɔnɔ ɔ́dɔ limade lɛ ya mɛ ɔ́ŋa kusumu yo Ɔka ní ɔdɔpɔʋisuʋɔ lɔ aʋa oʋídi i. 14Lɛ ese linu si kwite le mɔmɔmɔ si bɛpɔ blɔ Ɔkaɛ ní Yuda limade ne me e, lɛ Mose ɔ́xwa limade lɛ ya linyi lipoe lɛ gi azaapani ní kusumu yo baka ba su u.
Yesu onu kusumu yo Ɔka dzedze ŋwisi Melxizedek kiniɛ
15Lɛ liboe lɛ gi kwipani i, lɛ mɛ liadra ɛsanɔ; kusumu yo Ɔka dzedze aŋwi gi ɔlɛ ŋwisi Melxizedek kinɛ ɛ. 16Onu kusumu yo Ɔka aní gani ɔsã ní ɔnɔ kigyɔgyɔɛ mɛ onu, pɔ̀ gani Ligbã́ nɛ kigagla a, gi bátratani kuzizi me e. 17Lɛ ese linu si bɛpani lɛ wee si,
“Wonu kusumu yo Ɔka kɔtsinɔ kanɔmɛ anɔmɛ ní Melxizedek ɔga nɔ onu.”
18Lɛ loso bɛyurɔ ɔsã koko lo eplɛ kapɛ agi kigagla kíma ní lɔ su u, xe oʋíbu libiitɔtɔ loso o. 19Lɛ ese linu si ɔsã nɔ ɔ́tani kididɔ kubitɛ si kina a, pɔ̀ abla ɛ, bɛki blɔ kudeonudidi gi kwipɛ sanɔ ɔ, gi gani kɔ aʋa ɛ, kwitipɔni Aya.
20Kɔpɔnɔ ɛ, Aya yɛ kitamu yɛ kyɛ kilɛ. Kitamu kiliɛ kíze lipoe lɛ klɔ gi bedu banɔdzedze ba kusumu yo baka a. 21Pɔ̀ Yesu kɔ edzì kusumu yo Ɔka gi
Aya aka kitamu si,
“Aya aka aplɛ lɛ ɔ́trakyi yɛ begyodi ʋa a:
‘Wonu kusumu yo Ɔka kɔtsinɔ kanɔmɛ anɔmɛ.’ ”
22Kɔkpɛ kitamu kiliɛ ya loso e, Yesu edzì ɔniliɛ gi ɔlɛɛ kede ɔki blɔ ní kunyanya a, gi kwipɛ sanɔ mɛ ɛ.
23Abla ɛ, Kusumu yo baka báklɔ ba bɛsrɛ lɛ ese linu si betse lɛ batrátani ba axwɛ na aʋa kutrɛ tɔ ɔ. 24Pɔ̀ gi Yesu kɔ ɔlɛ ligbã́ kɔtsinɔ kanɔmɛ anɔmɛ ɛ, yɛ kusumu yo ɔka kuzeze e, kútratrɛ ní ɔnɔ dzedze ɔtɔtɔ kɔ ɔ. 25Lɛ loso aatani kuzɛ lipoe kakaa ɔki banama gi bɛga yɛ aʋa a, xe bɛba Aya klɔ ɔ, lɛ ese linu si ɔlɛ ligbã lipoe kakaa gi aazi ba onu ní Aya aŋu mɛ ɛ.
26Kusumu yo Ɔka ʋidi ŋwisi Yesu i, yɛ aatani blɔ baziazia ʋa aʋa kubitɛ ɔki blɔ. Yɛ su ɛɛdra, kesetataa tɔtɔ káma ní yɛ su u, lɛ eboe zimizi áma ní yɛ su u; bebu ye lidze ní banɔ zimizi ba su, lɛ bemuni ye gi emu asani lifunu ne e. 27Ɔ́ma ŋwisi kusumu yo baka ʋidi dzedze ba a, gi liziã si bapɔʋi bidɔ aki Aya liwe kakaa ki ba tutɔ ba eboe zimizi na kpɛ ɔtɔ ɔ, xe ableke biapɔʋi bidɔ ɔki Aya ni banɔ dzedze ba onu u. Aki yɛ bidɔ pɔʋi pɔʋi ʋɛ tule ní banima petee litukpo gi akɔ isu ya akɔ ɛ. 28Ɔsã nɔ izɛɛkɔ banama gi bána ɛ, du si beze kusumu yo baka a. Pɔ̀ Aya yɛ kakaplɛa a, gi aka aplɛ ni kitamu i, xe kiba ní ɔsã nɔ kede e, yɛ akya Yebi ye gi abitɛ si obloni yɛ axwɛ na a kɔtsinɔ kanɔmɛ anɔmɛ.

english_text:
A Better Promise
1 What I mean is we have a high priest who sits at the right side of God's great throne in heaven. 2He also serves as the priest in the most holy place inside the real tent there in heaven. This tent of worship was set up by the Lord, not by humans.
3Since all priests must offer gifts and sacrifices, Christ also needed to have something to offer. 4If he were here on earth, he would not be a priest at all, because here the Law appoints other priests to offer sacrifices. 5 But the tent where they serve is just a copy and a shadow of the real one in heaven. Before Moses made the tent, he was told, “Be sure to make it exactly like the pattern you were shown on the mountain!” 6Now Christ has been appointed to serve as a priest in a much better way, and he has given us much assurance of a better agreement.
7If the first agreement with God had been all right, there would not have been any need for another one. 8 But the Lord found fault with it and said,
“I tell you the time will come,
when I will make
a new agreement
with the people of Israel
and the people of Judah.
9It won't be like the agreement
that I made
with their ancestors,
when I took them by the hand
and led them out of Egypt.
They broke their agreement
with me,
and I stopped caring
about them!
10“But now I tell the people
of Israel
this is my new agreement:
‘The time will come
when I, the Lord,
will write my laws
on their minds and hearts.
I will be their God,
and they will be
my people.
11Not one of them
will have to teach another
to know me, their Lord.’
“All of them will know me,
no matter who they are.
12I will treat them with kindness,
even though they are wicked.
I will forget their sins.”
13When the Lord talks about a new agreement, he means that the first one is out of date. And anything that is old and useless will soon disappear.
translation_text:
Yesu yɛ onu Kusumu yo Ɔka ʋidi ye kí Kunyanya Vuvu yɔ
1Liboe lɛ gi lɛ su kwipani i, lɛ linu si: kusumu yo Ɔka ʋidi liɛ gi ɔlɛ ní blɔ awla a, eze ní Lifunu ne kukusi yo kɔ likusigbo le kaŋawla mɛ. 2Eesumu ní Ɔdradra klɔ ní lisumugba lɛ ɔtutɔ lɔ gi Aya awa mɛ ɛ, gi aní banɔ gbagbe awla bɛkɔ ɛwa lɛ ɛ.
3Kusumu yo Ɔka kakaa ɛ, Aya akya yɛ si ɔki bidɔ tɔ ɔpɔʋi baga aki Aya, lɛ loso blɔ kusumu yo Ɔka ʋidi ye kyɛ ɛ, lilɛ si kiditɔ kize ní yɛ awla gi aakɔ ɛ. 4Xegi kesea lia ya aʋa ɔlɛ ɛ, nya ɔ́traze kusumu yo Ɔka koŋ, lɛ ese linu si kusumu yo baka ba balɛ gi bizɛɛki bidɔ ní ɔsã nɔ onu u. 5Axwɛ ana gi bizɛɛwa ní Ɔdradra klɔ ɔ, ŋwisi kusumu yo baka kinɛ ɛ, enu kidiliɛ gi kilɛ ní lifunu ne e, kɛ liŋwitsɛ nì kɛ kuyeo ko. Mose, lipoe lɛ gi atráwa lisɔligba lɛ ɛ, Aya aki yɛ ɔsãa nɔ si, “Di le aʋa si wɔbitɛ kidɔ kakaa ŋwisi kiliɛ gi bɛbasɛ wɔ ní lito le aʋa kinɛ ɛ.”
6Pɔ̀ abla ɛ, Aya akɔ kusumu yo axwɛ na gi emu isani ba ani na ɛ, aki Yesu ŋwisi kiliɛ gi kunyanya liɔ ɔ, gi abitɛ ní Aya ni yɛ banima kusi i, kwipɛ sani kutɔpu yɔ kinɛ, lɛ ese linu si bɛbitɛ kɔ ɛtrɔ ni kakaplɛ ki bidɔ gi bipɛ sanɔ aʋa a.
7Atɛsi kesetata káma ní kunyanya tɔpu yɔ su i, nya kunyanya ʋla kutrázia tɔ ɔ. 8Pɔ̀ Aya amɔ kesetata ní yɛ banima su gi apani si i,
“Me Aya mɛɛpani si: Lipoe le litrába,
gi maabitɛ kunyanya vuvu ni Israɛl kepea ani Yuda kepea a.
9Kútraze ŋwisi kunyanyaa liɔ
gi mabitɛ ni ba bakatsi ba a,
liwe lɛ gi mevu ba ɔwla lɔ ɔ,
xe magbani ba adɔnɔ ní Egipte e.
Béɖi anukwarɛɛ aki kunyanyaa liɔ gi mabitɛ ni ba a,
lɛ loso mekyi ní ba su, nite Ɔkaɛ apani.
10Lɛ loso, kunyanyaa liɔ ya gi mátrabitɛ ni Israelbanɛ ma
ni ewe a la gi ɛtrába mɛ ɛ, nite Ɔkaɛ apani.
Maakpɛ mɛ isã nɛ ní ba begyodi ʋa mɛ
lɛ maaŋwlimi nɛ ɔkpɛ ní ba ina nɛ mɛ.
Maaze ba Aya,
lɛ biaze mɛ banɔ.
11Lítrazia si bamɛ ɔtɔtɔ ɔbasɛ yɛ ɔhanuglɔ bidɔ ɔ,
loo ɔnɔnɔ ɔsi yenemi si, ‘Te Ɔkaɛ,’
lɛ ese linu si ba petee biate me,
dɔni ɔgɔgɔɛ aʋa zɛɛkɔna ni ɔnɔ ʋidi ye aʋa ɛ,
12Maakɔ ba eboe zimizi na kɔkyɛ ba
lɛ mátraplɛ aŋu ní ba kesetata atɔtɔ aʋa tɔ ɔ.”
13Gi apani ní kunyanya vuvu yɔ su i, Aya abitɛ lɛ si kunyanya tɔpu yɔ kwidzi kukoko, lɛ kidiliɛ gi kitsi i, xe kikpã ɛ, kialila kpekpemii.

english_text:
The Tent in Heaven
1The first promise included rules for worship and a tent for worship here on earth. 2 The first part of the tent was called the holy place, and a lampstand, a table, and the sacred loaves of bread were kept there.
3 Behind the curtain was the most holy place. 4 The gold altar for burning incense was in this holy place. The gold-covered sacred chest was also there, and inside it were three things. First, there was a gold jar filled with manna. Then there was Aaron's walking stick that sprouted. Finally, there were the flat stones with the Ten Commandments written on them. 5 On top of the chest were the glorious creatures with wings opened out above the place of mercy.
Now isn't the time to go into detail about these things. 6 But this is how everything was when the priests went each day into the first part of the tent to do their duties. 7 However, only the high priest could go into the second part of the tent, and he went in only once a year. Each time he carried blood to offer for his sins and for any sins that the people had committed without meaning to.
8All of this is the Holy Spirit's way of saying no one could enter the most holy place while the tent was still the place of worship. 9This also has a meaning for today. It shows we cannot make our consciences clear by offering gifts and sacrifices. 10These rules are merely about such things as eating and drinking and ceremonies for washing ourselves. And rules about physical things will last only until the time comes to change them for something better.
11 Christ came as the high priest of the good things that are now here. He also went into a much better tent that wasn't made by humans and that doesn't belong to this world. 12Then Christ went once for all into the most holy place and freed us from sin forever. He did this by offering his own blood instead of the blood of goats and bulls.
13 According to the Law of Moses, those people who become unclean are not fit to worship God. Yet they will be considered clean, if they are sprinkled with the blood of goats and bulls and with the ashes of a sacrificed calf. 14But Christ was sinless, and he offered himself as an eternal and spiritual sacrifice to God. This is why his blood is much more powerful and makes our consciences clear. Now we can serve the living God and no longer do things that lead to death.
15Christ died to rescue those who had sinned and broken the old agreement. Now he brings his chosen ones a new agreement with its guarantee of God's eternal blessings! 16In fact, making an agreement of this kind is like writing a will. This is because the one who makes the will must die before it is of any use. 17In other words, a will doesn't go into effect as long as the one who made it is still alive.
18Blood was also used to put the first agreement into effect. 19 Moses told the people all the Law said they must do. Then he used red wool and a hyssop plant to sprinkle the people and the book of the Law with the blood of bulls and goats and with water. 20He told the people, “With this blood God makes his agreement with you.” 21 Moses also sprinkled blood on the tent and on everything else used in worship. 22 The Law says that almost everything must be sprinkled with blood, and no sins can be forgiven unless blood is offered.
Christ's Great Sacrifice
23These things are only copies of what is in heaven, and so they had to be made holy by these ceremonies. But the real things in heaven must be made holy by something better. 24This is why Christ did not go into a tent made by humans and was only a copy of the real one. Instead, he went into heaven and is now there with God to help us.
25Christ did not have to offer himself many times. He wasn't like a high priest who goes into the most holy place each year to offer the blood of an animal. 26If he had offered himself every year, he would have suffered many times since the creation of the world. But instead, near the end of time he offered himself once and for all, so he could be a sacrifice that does away with sin.
27We die only once, and then we are judged. 28 So Christ died only once to take away the sins of many people. But when he comes again, it will not be to take away sin. He will come to save everyone who is waiting for him.
translation_text:
Aya yɛ Kusumu yo ní Kesea aʋa Lisumugba lɛ mɛ
1Kunyanya tɔpu yɔ ɛ, kɔ isã nɛ ki kusumu yo kwize, lɛ Ɔdradra kɔ kyɛ eze gi bɛbitɛ ní kesea ya aʋa a. 2Bɛwa Aya yɛ lisumugba lɛ gi linu kusa akpandɔ ɔ. Bɛlɛ lisumugba lɛ ní kupɛ tuʋa mɛ. Ní kapɛ tɔpu ya gi bɛɛxwa si Ɔdradra klɔ mɛ ɛ, kani ose, ani ɔkplɔ gi lɔ aʋa bɛzɛɛtrɔ aklã na a, gi bɛdra la su iiki Aya ɛ. 3Lɛ ní ligba lɛ mɛ kutsasa ʋlaa kede e, níklɔ bizɛɛxwa si Ɔdradra klɔ petee. 4Ní niklɔ ɛ, bedu sika yɛ ɔdɔpɔʋisuʋɔ gi lɔ aʋa bizɛɛkpɛ lifi ye kifu yɛ, ani sika yɛ kunyanya adaka yɛ, gi yɛ me sika kefukpo a, gi kamɛ bɛkpɛ mana yɛ keze e. Aron yɛ ose vuga vuga lɔ gi ɛɛta awlakpa la ɛ, kɔpɔni akla na gi la aʋa bɛŋwlimi Mose ɔsã nɔ ɛtrɔ ɛ, la kyɛ akpasi ní adaka yɛ mɛ. 5Ní adaka yɛ aʋa ɛ, Aya yɛ baxwɛdzɛ ba gi bizɛxwa si Kerubim ba a, gi bɛɛbasɛ si Aya ɔlɛ ní niklɔ ɛ balɛ. Ba siwlasasa sɛ ɛ, siwe niklɔ gi bizɛɛkɔ eboe zimizi na ɛkyɛ ɛ. Pɔ̀ kutrátani eboa la petee me kutrɛ kpaŋu abla a.
6Xegi bedu bidɔ mɛ nite pɔ́ ɛ, Kusumu yo baka ba bizeetiku ní lisumugba lɛ kapɛ tɔpu ya mɛ liwe kakaa lɛ bizɛwa ba axwɛ na. 7Pɔ̀ Kusumu yo Ɔka ʋidi ye ko izeetiku ní lisumugba lɛ kimɛ ɛmɛ, tule ní ligɔ lɛ mɛ. Izeevu ikaɛ lɛ gi izɛɛki Aya ní yɛ ɔtutɔ ni banɔ dzedze ba eboe zimizi na gi béte si bɛɛbitɛ ɛ onu ní awla. 8Lɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ Dradra lɔ ebu le basɛ tratratra dɔni la ya mɛ si gi batrɔtrɔ ba ya balɛ onu i, baʋíbu kudeo onu ɛki niklɔ gi Ɛsadra petee ɛki ɔnɔnɔ gi lisumugba tɔpu nɛ lidzi lɛ ɛ. 9Lɛ ya linu lixaŋu gi libasɛ kidiliɛ ɛ, gi kiiba kɛ mɛ omono o. Libasɛ si bidɔ kɔkɔ ni bagaa ʋa gi bɛɛpɔʋi ɛki Aya ɛ, bítraki si ɔniliɛ ɛ, gi yɛ ɛɛki Aya bidɔ mɛ ɛ, yɛ ɔna nɔ mɛ aadra petee, 10lɛ ese linu si binu kiŋaŋa ni kiŋwɛŋwɛ ni kusuplo egoʋi ede ede ko. Bɛ ya petee binu aŋu mɛ isã ko gi ilɛ lɛ ozekɔ ɔ, kɔna ni lipoe lɛ gi Aya aamani yɛ kutrɔtrɔ vuvu yɔ ɔ.
Yesu yɛ ika lɛ iplo blɔ eboe zimizi na ɛzanɔ
11Pɔ̀ gi Kristo aba ŋwisi kusumu yo Ɔka ʋidi ye e, gi yɛ aki bidɔ gbã ɛ mɛ ɛ, gi bilɛ niya kooko e, aga lisɔligba ɛlɛ gi liŋa kedzi sanɔ ɔ, xe lina ɛsanɔ mɛ ɛ. Lisumugba ɛlɛ ya ɛ, aní banɔ awla bɛkɔ ɛwa lɛ ɛ, lɛ ese linu si línu kesea aʋa ya bidɔ bitɛbitɛ ɛ. 12Gi eti eku ní Ɔdradra klɔ petee tule uliɔ klɔ ʋa ɛ, ɔ́tsini bamu ni egumenyime ika a, boŋu i, akɔ yɛ ɔtutɔ yɛ ika lɛ, lɛ azɛ kubúbú gi kútrablo ɛki blɔ ɛ. 13Bamu ʋa ni egumenyime na ika lɛ ani egumedzɛ la, la kusuʋɔtɔɔ bizeehle ʋe ɛbali ní banama gi basu ɔ́dra ní ɔkɔ lɔ onu i, aʋa kɔ basu ɛdra ní ɔkɔ lɔ onu i. 14Xegi lilɛ ní lɛ mɛ nite e, ege kɛ kiakɔ si Kristo yɛ ika lɛ, gi akɔ isu aki Aya a, ŋwisi bidɔ pɔʋi pɔʋi gi bina kinɛ ɛ, gani Ɔfuɛfuɛ moblo lo me e, ítraplo blɔ begyodi ʋa su dɔni eboa la gi izɛɛmani kutse me e, gi la aaki si kwiawa axwɛ na ki Aya gbagbe e?
15Kɔkpɛ liboe lɛ ya loso e, Kristo yɛ onu ɔniliɛ gi yɛ onu ɔguvu ye ní kunyanya vuvu yɔ onu u, tɔ banama gi Aya axwa ɛ, biazɛ kaŋase moblo alia gi Aya aka aplɛ ɛ. Lɛ gi etse ní banima litukpo e, yɛ kutseo kwikɔ si banima bɛmɔ kubúbú idɔni ba eboe zimizi na mɛ ní lipoe lɛ mɛ gi kunyanya tɔpu yɔ kwizɛɛwa kɔ axwɛ na ɛ.
16Lɛ ese linu si ní kupɛpɛɔ liɔ gi bizɛŋwlimi plɛ onu i, liziã si batrɔ likla lɛ aʋa si ɔniliɛ gi aŋwlimi kɔ aplɛ ɛ, etse xe e. 17Lɛ ese linu si gi ɔniliɛ klɔ ɔlɛ ligbã́ nɛ ɛ, xe aŋwlimi kɔ akpɛ ɛ, kigaglaɛ tɔtɔ kíma yɛ kupɛpɛɔ awla a, gakɔ kpɛ ni lipoe lɛ gi etse e, xe ableke kwiawa lixwɛ ɛ. 18Lɛ ya loso kunyanya tɔpu yɔ kura kyɛ ɛ, bɛwa ika lɛ su axwɛ kede, xe ableke kwikpese kɔ axwɛ na kuwa a. 19Gi Mose apani ní isã nɛ su ŋwisi kiliɛ gi bɛkpɛ lɛ ɛki banima petee kede e, akɔ egume na, ni bamu ʋa ika lɛ gi bɛpisa ni kuni yo o, lɛ efuflu ʋe ní ɔsã nɔ keplekpaa ni banima petee aʋa ni hisope yɛ awlakpa la gi bɛnya ni sigapi kyikyi sɛ ɛ. 20Lɛ apani si, “Bɛ ya binu ika lɛ gi ibasɛ kunyanya liɔ gi kɔ ɔsã Aya akpɛ aki mlɔ si mlevu u.” 21Niteme ke Mose akɔ ika lɛ ehle ní Aya yɛ lisumugba lɛ ni bisumudɔ kakaa aʋa. 22Lɛ ní anukwarɛɛ mɛ ní ɔsã nɔ onu i, kidɔ kakaa kloe kiziã si beplo kɛ su ni ika, lɛ ese linu si kukɔkyɛ kúma xegi ika íbali kese e.
Yesu Yɛ isu kukɔkyɛ kuzebu eboe zimizi na zanɔ
23Liziã si beplo bidɔ bɛ ya gi bilɛ ŋwisi lifunu ne bidɔ mɛ su gani bidɔpɔʋipɔʋi bɛ ya onu u, pɔ̀ lifunu ne bidɔ mɛ bɛ ɔtutɔ lɔ ɛ, biziã bidɔpɔʋipɔʋi gi bipɛ sani bɛ ya ɛ. 24Lɛ ese linu si Kristo óti iku ní Ɔdradra kɔ gi bɛkɔ awla la ɛwa a, gi ɔlɛ ŋwisi lifunu ne ɔni nɔ ɔtutɔ lɔ ɔ. Eti eku ní lifunu ne ɔtutɔ lɔ, niklɔ gi ɔlɛ abla ní Aya aŋu mɛ ní blɔ onu u. 25Kristo óti iku si yikɔ isu ikɔ tasɛ tasɛ ŋwisi kusumu yo Ɔka ʋidi ye gi izeetiku ní Ɔdradra klɔ tule ní ligɔ kakaa mɛ ɛ, ni ika gi ínu yɛ ɔtutɔ yɛ inɛ ɛ. 26Xegi nite lilɛ ɛ, nya lilɛ ki yɛ si ɔzɛmɔ ɔziã nɔ tasɛ tasɛ dɔni kelea okpese klɔ kee; pɔ̀ abla ní lipoe lɛ gi kelea kɛtrɛ ní ka ɔgù kɔ ɛ, aba tule si tɔ yɛ aabu eboe zimizi na zanɔ gani yɛ isu kukɔkyɛ mɛ. 27Ŋwisi kiliɛ gi bɛtrɔ lɛ ɛki ɔnɔ si, aatse tule kinɛ ɛ, lɛ kede e, biaxwa litsi ni yɛ, 28niteme ke Kristo fɛ, akɔ isu akyɛ tule tɔ yibu banɔ glegle ba eboe zimizi na ɛzanɔ. Aadzi ba kuʋla aní si tɔ yimɔ kini kpɛ eboe zimizi na tɔ ɔ, boŋu i, si tɔ yibu banama gi beepe ye e.

english_text:
1The Law of Moses is like a shadow of the good things to come. This shadow isn't the good things themselves, because it cannot free people from sin by the sacrifices offered year after year. 2If there were worshipers who already have their sins washed away and their consciences made clear, there would not be any need to go on offering sacrifices. 3-4But the blood of bulls and goats cannot take away sins. It only reminds people of their sins from one year to the next.
5 When Christ came into the world, he said to God,
“Sacrifices and offerings
are not what you want,
but you have given me
my body.
6No, you are not pleased
with animal sacrifices
and offerings for sin.”
7Then Christ said,
“And so, my God,
I have come to do
what you want,
as the Scriptures say.”
8The Law teaches that offerings and sacrifices must be made because of sin. But why did Christ mention these things and say that God did not want them? 9Well, it was to do away with offerings and sacrifices and to replace them. This is what he meant by saying to God, “I have come to do what you want.” 10So we are made holy because Christ obeyed God and offered himself once for all.
11 The priests do their work each day, and they keep on offering sacrifices that can never take away sins. 12 But Christ offered himself as a sacrifice that is good forever. Now he is sitting at God's right side, 13and he will stay there until his enemies are put under his power. 14By his one sacrifice he has forever set free from sin the people he brings to God.
15The Holy Spirit also speaks of this by telling us that the Lord said,
16 “When the time comes,
I will make an agreement
with them.
I will write my laws
on their minds and hearts.
17 Then I will forget
about their sins
and no longer remember
their evil deeds.”
18When sins are forgiven, there is no more need to offer sacrifices.
Encouragement and Warning
19My friends, the blood of Jesus gives us courage to enter the most holy place 20by a new way that leads to life! And this way takes us through the curtain that is Christ himself.
21We have a great high priest who is in charge of God's house. 22 So let's come near God with pure hearts and a confidence that comes from having faith. Let's keep our hearts pure, our consciences free from evil, and our bodies washed with clean water. 23We must hold tightly to the hope we say is ours. After all, we can trust the one who made the agreement with us. 24We should keep on encouraging each other to be thoughtful and to do helpful things. 25Some people have given up the habit of meeting for worship, but we must not do that. We should keep on encouraging each other, especially since you know that the day of the Lord's coming is getting closer.
26No sacrifices can be made for people who decide to sin after they find out about the truth. 27 They are God's enemies, and all they can look forward to is a terrible judgment and a furious fire. 28 If two or more witnesses accused someone of breaking the Law of Moses, that person could be put to death. 29 But it is much worse to dishonor God's Son and to disgrace the blood of the promise that made us holy. And it is just as bad to insult the Holy Spirit, who shows us mercy. 30 We know that God has said he will punish and take revenge. We also know that the Scriptures say the Lord will judge his people. 31It is a terrible thing to fall into the hands of the living God!
32Don't forget all the hard times you went through when you first received the light. 33Sometimes you were abused and mistreated in public, and at other times you shared in the sufferings of others. 34You were kind to people in jail. And you gladly let your possessions be taken away, because you knew you had something better, something that would last forever.
35Keep on being brave! It will bring you great rewards. 36Learn to be patient, so you will please God and be given what he has promised. 37 As the Scriptures say,
“God is coming soon!
It won't be very long.
38The people God accepts
will live because
of their faith.
But he isn't pleased
with anyone
who turns back.”
39We are not like those people who turn back and get destroyed. We will keep on having faith until we are saved.
translation_text:
Yesu akɔ isu akyɛ tule ko ní ɔnɔ kakaa litukpo
1Ɔsã nɔ ɛ, eboe gbã ana gi ɛtrába ɛ, la kuyeo ko ɛbasɛ, aní eboe la kizeze me ɔtutɔ lɔ. Ní ɔsã lɔ klɔ ese e, bizɛɛkɔ bidɔpɔʋipɔʋi bɛ klɔ edzini kɔ ligɔ kakaa. Pɔ̀ bítratani banama gi beti apɔnɔ tɔ beesumu Aya ɛ, basu kudra xe biana a. 2Xegi bidɔpɔʋipɔʋi ʋɛ klɔ biatani banama gi beesumu Aya su kudra sugba ɛ, nya bɛmɔni bidɔ mɛ kupɔʋi, lɛ ese linu si banama gi ba beesumu Aya ɛ, basu ɛdra petee lɛ ba ina nɛ ítrabu ba lipɔ ɔ, kɔkpɛ ba eboe zimizi na loso. 3Boŋu i, bidɔpɔʋipɔʋi ʋɛ bilɛ ligɔ kakaa si bizɛɛplɛ aŋu ní banima eboe zimizi na aʋa ɛki ba. 4Lɛ ese linu si egume na ni bamu ʋa ika lɛ ɛ, ítratani eboe zimizi na kubu zanɔ liwewe.
5Lɛ ya loso gi Kristo aaba kelea mɛ ɛ, asi Aya si,
“Wɔ́ziã bidɔpɔʋipɔʋi ni bidɔkɔkɔ ɔ,
pɔ̀ wɔki mɛ kisugya.
6Baga zizi gi bɛpɔʋi ní ɔdɔpɔʋisuʋɔ lɔ aʋa ni bidɔ kɔkɔ
gi biabu eboe zimizi zanɔ kyɛ ɛ, bíku wɔ aŋu u.
7Lɛ mapani si, ‘Imɔ mɛ, bɛŋwlimi kpɛ ní keplekpaa mɛ ní mɛ su si
Oo Aya, maba si mibitɛ ní wɔ kupɛpɛɔ aʋa a.’ ”
8Ní ɔtɔpu kɔ ɛ, apani si “Bidɔkɔkɔ ni bidɔpɔʋipɔʋi loo baga gi bɛpɔʋi ní ɔdɔpɔʋisuʋɔ lɔ aʋa a, loo bidɔkɔkɔ ki eboe zimizi bíku wɔ aŋu lɛ wɔ́pɛ ʋɛ ɛ.” Apani nite gi nya ɔsã nɔ ɛki si bazaaki bidɔ bɛ ya xe e. 9Lɛ apani si, “Imɔ mɛ maba si mibitɛ ní wɔ kupɛpɛɔ aʋa” Lɛ loso Aya ebu kunyanya tɔpu yɔ azanɔ lɛ akɔ kunyanya ʋlaa akpɛ ní kɔ ozeklɔ ɛ. 10Agi Yesu Kristo abitɛ kidiliɛ gi Aya apɛ si ɔbitɛ ɛ, blɔ petee isu idra dɔni blɔ eboe zimizi na mɛ ɔkpɛ isu ya kukɔkyɛ uliɔ gi abitɛ tuleo liɔ klɔ loso o.
11Kusɔliɔ ɔka kakaa izɛɛwa yɛ axwɛ na liwe kakaa, lɛ izɛɛkɔ bidɔpɔʋipɔʋi ʋɛ lide tilee lɛ klɔ lipoe kakaa pɔ̀ bidɔpɔʋipɔʋii ʋɛ klɔ bítratani eboe zimizi na kubu zanɔ liwewe e. 12Pɔ̀ gi Kristo aki bidɔ mɛ tule ki eboe zimizi na ɛ, bidɔkɔkɔɛ ʋɛ klɔ biwà axwɛ kɔtsinɔ kanɔmɛ anɔmɛ, lɛ eze ní Aya yɛ kaŋaa mɛ. 13Lɛ dɔni lipoe lɛ klɔ aʋa kɔtsinɔ ɛ, eepe Aya gi yɛ aakɔ̀ yɛ ahwafianɛ ma kpɛ yɛ kukliseo e. 14Nì bidɔkɔkɔ tule e, aki si banama su gi iidra dɔni ba eboe zimizi na mɛ ɛ, bɛna kɔtsinɔ kanɔmɛ anɔmɛ.
15Lɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ Dradra lɔ kyɛ ɛɛŋa katsalia ɛki blɔ. Ní ɔtɔpu kɔ ɛ, ɛpani si,
16“Lɛ ya linu kunyanya liɔ gi maabitɛ nì ba a,
ní ewe ala ya gi ɛtrába mɛ ɛ, nite Ɔkaɛ apani,
Maakpɛ mɛ isã nɛ ní ba ina nɛ mɛ,
lɛ maaŋwlimi nɛ kpɛ ní ba begyodi ʋa mɛ ɛ.”
17Lɛ apani dzɛ si,
“Mátraplɛ aŋu liwewe
ní ba eboe zimizi na ni ba ɔsã nɔ ese kutataa tɔtɔ aʋa tɔ ɔ.”
18Lɛ loso xegi bɛkɔ kiboebitɛ liɛ ya ɛkyɛ bá ɛ, bidɔpɔʋipɔʋi gi biabu eboe zimizi zanɔ ɛ, bíziã tɔ ɔ.
Mlaki tɔ kukpɛ ikù nɛ
19Lɛ loso mɛ benemi ba a, agi kudeonu kulɛ si kwiatiku ní Ɔdradra klɔ gani Yesu kutseo me e, 20yɛ adra kude vuvu gi kwiaki blɔ ligbã́ a, gani kusa uliɔ gi kwitsa ligba lɛ mɛ ɛ, gi kunu yɛ ɔtutɔ yɛ kisugyaɛ mɛ ɛ. 21Gi kusumu yo Ɔka ʋidi ɔlɛ ní blɔ awla a, gi yɛ odi ní Aya yɛ lisɔligba lɛ onu i, 22mlaki tɔ kuti pɔni Aya ni ɔna gi lɔ mɛ ɛdra ɛ, ni kuziŋa zizi nì ina gi lɛ su idra ɛ, gani Yesu yɛ ika lɛ gi ehle abali ní blɔ aʋa a, xe iiki si blɔ su ɛdra dɔni lipɔ kububu me e, nì blɔ kisugya gi eplo nì kuni i gi kɔmɛ kwidra ɛ. 23Mlaki tɔ kuvu kudeonudidi uliɔ gi kɔ su kwipani ki banima a, si kulɛ ní blɔ awla gagla, lɛ ese linu si kwiatani kuka ní Aya aʋa si aabitɛ ní yɛ kakaplɛa aʋa. 24Mlaki tɔ kupɛ bede ʋa ya onu gi kwiakpɛ kifu ní blɔ ahanuglɔ ba mɛ, tɔ balɔ ani tɔ babitɛ eboe gbã. 25Lɛ mlakíkɔ si kuyurɔ blɔ batsátsa ʋa ŋwisi kiliɛ gi banɔ batɔ bɛɛbitɛ kinɛ ɛ, boŋu i, mlaki tɔ kukpɛ ɔna ní blɔ ahanuglɔ ba lipo sanɔ, abla gi kwimɔ si Ɔkaɛ yɛ liwe le liiti pɔnɔ ɛ.
26Lɛ ese linu si xegi kwitrɛ ní blɔ eboe zimizi na aʋa ɔŋwadzu i, bidɔkɔkɔɛ tɔtɔ bíma a, gi bɛ biabu eboe zimizi na zanɔ ɔ, abla gi mlɛzɛ anukwarɛɛ ɔtutɔ lɔ ɛ. 27Kɔlɔ kidiliɛ gi kigɔ ko kinu si kuze ní lifu kusese me kí litsixwaxwa ɛnɛ ɛ, gi lɛ kudeonu kwidi i, ani kifu ʋidi iliɛ gi kitrazizi banɔ ba ya gi benu Aya yɛ ahwafianɛ ma ɛ! 28Ɔniliɛ gi ɔ́dimɛ si yibitɛ ní Mose ɔsã nɔ aʋa ɛ, bizeye ye asigaga modi xegi bɛtsa litsi ki yɛ si aŋa lipɔ ɔ, xegi banɔ tiaʋa loo sani nite bɛŋa katsalia ɛki liboe le e.
29Kɔ kitɛ liaze ki ɔniliɛ gi akpɛ Aya Yebi ye kakpaa ɛ? Ɔniliɛ gi akɔ Aya yɛ kunyanya vuvu yɔ ika lɛ ŋwisi biwaʋídɔ gi bɛ bizɛɛdra yɛ su idɔní yɛ eboe zimizi na mɛ ɛ? Gi yɛ izeekpini Aya yɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ ɔ, gi lɔ izɛki lipomepɛpɛ nɛ ɛ? Mlegyo le edi kiliɛ gi kutuheheo liɔ ɔ, gi kwigyeni ye, kwianyani sanɔ ɛ! 30Lɛ ese linu si kwite ɔniliɛ klɔ gi apani si, “Maabitɛ ɔkpɛ lɛ onu, maadzini liʋɛ nɛ kutsa,” gi yɛ apani dzɛ si i, “Ɔkaɛ aaxwa litsi ni yɛ banima a.” 31Linu lifu liboe si ɔnutɔ ɔwɔli okú ní Aya gi ɔlɛ ligbã́ nɛ ɛ, yɛ iwla mɛ.
32Mlaplɛ aŋu kiliɛ gi lize ɛki mlɔ ní ewe ala klɔ mɛ ɛ, gi Aya aki mlɔ yɛ kuklekleo e, kiliɛ gi mlɛmɔ kini glegle e, pɔ̀ xe mlɔ kuŋwlɛŋwlɛɔ kúŋa mlɔ aʋa a. 33Lipoe tɔ onu i, bekpini mlo, lɛ bɛzɛɛwa mlɔ nyɔnyɔɛ ní ɔha lɔ kusi i, lɛ ní lipoe dzedze me e, mlɛlɛ nì banɔ baklɔ gi ba bɛzɛɛwa nyɔnyɔɛ ɛ. 34Mlɔ kyɛ mlemɔ ɔziã nɔ ɛpɔni ba ŋwisi banama gi bɛkpɛ ligba lɛ ɛ, lɛ gi bɛɛzi mlɔ bidɔmɔmɔ ʋɛ petee kyɛ ɛ, mlɛzi lɛ ni isami, lɛ ese linu si mlɔ ɔtutɔ mlete si bidɔ gi bipɛ sanɔ ɛ, bilɛ ní mlɔ awla gi bitrázizi liwewe e.
35Lɛ loso ɔna okído ní mlɔ lipo o, lɛ ese linu si biamani mlɔ libibubu ʋidi. 36Liziã si mlaplɛ litɔ ní kese atɔ xe mlɛbitɛ Aya yɛ kupɛpɛɔ aʋa ɛ, mlazi kidiliɛ gi kɛ aka aplɛ ɛ. 37Ní lipoe kpekpemi itɔ mɛ ɛ,
“Ligɔ taʋɛ ko ɔniliɛ gi atrába ɛ aaba, lɛ ɔ́trawɔ kede e.
38Pɔ̀ mɛ banama gi ba bede ʋa bɛgyɔ ɛ, biaze ligbã́ ní ba kuziŋaa mɛ.
pɔ̀ xe bamɛ ɔtɔ ekyi kede akpɛ mɛ ɛ,
isami itraku me ni ye e.”
39Kúma ní banɔ baklɔ gi bizekyi kede e, xe bizɛɛkyrɔ kusi i. Boŋu i, kulɛ ní banɔ baklɔ gi bɛziŋa a, xe Aya ebu i, ba kusi.

english_text:
The Great Faith of God's People
1Faith makes us sure of what we hope for and gives us proof of what we cannot see. 2 It was their faith that made our ancestors pleasing to God.
3 Because of our faith, we know that the world was made at God's command. We also know that what can be seen was made out of what cannot be seen.
4 Because Abel had faith, he offered God a better sacrifice than Cain did. God was pleased with him and his gift, and even though Abel is now dead, his faith still speaks for him.
5 Enoch had faith and did not die. He pleased God, and God took him up to heaven. This is why his body was never found. 6But without faith no one can please God. We must believe that God is real and rewards everyone who searches for him.
7 Because Noah had faith, he was warned about something that had not yet happened. He obeyed and built a boat that saved him and his family. In this way the people of the world were judged, and Noah was given the blessings that come to everyone who pleases God.
8 Abraham had faith and obeyed God. He was told to go to the land that God had said would be his, and he left for a country he had never seen. 9 Because Abraham had faith, he lived as a stranger in the promised land. He lived there in a tent, and so did Isaac and Jacob, who were later given the same promise. 10Abraham did this, because he was waiting for the eternal city God had planned and built.
11 Even when Sarah was too old to have children, she had faith that God would do what he had promised, and she had a son. 12 Her husband Abraham was almost dead, but he became the ancestor of many people. In fact, there are as many of them as there are stars in the sky or grains of sand along the seashore.
13 Every one of those people died. But they still had faith, even though they had not received what they had been promised. They were glad just to see these things from far away, and they agreed that they were only strangers and foreigners on this earth. 14When people talk this way, it is clear they are looking for a place to call their own. 15If they had been talking about the land where they had once lived, they could have gone back at any time. 16But they were looking forward to a better home in heaven. This is why God wasn't ashamed for them to call him their God. He even built a city for them.
17-18 Abraham had been promised that Isaac, his only son, would continue his family. But when Abraham was tested, he had faith and was willing to sacrifice Isaac, 19because he was sure that God could raise people to life. This was just like getting Isaac back from death.
20 Isaac had faith, and he promised blessings to Jacob and Esau. 21 Later, when Jacob was about to die, he leaned on his walking stick and worshiped. Then because of his faith he blessed each of Joseph's sons. 22 And right before Joseph died, he had faith that God would lead the people of Israel out of Egypt. So he told them to take his bones with them.
23 Because Moses' parents had faith, they kept him hidden until he was three months old. They saw that he was a beautiful child, and they were not afraid to disobey the king's orders. 24 Then after Moses grew up, his faith made him refuse to be called the king's grandson. 25 He chose to be mistreated with God's people instead of having the good time that sin could bring for a little while. 26Moses knew that the treasures of Egypt were not as wonderful as what he would receive from suffering for the Messiah, and he looked forward to his reward.
27Because of his faith, Moses left Egypt. Moses had seen the invisible God and wasn't afraid of the king's anger. 28 His faith also made him celebrate Passover. He sprinkled the blood of animals on the doorposts, so that the first-born sons of the people of Israel would not be killed by the destroying angel.
29 Because of their faith, the people walked through the Red Sea on dry land. But when the Egyptians tried to do it, they were drowned.
30 God's people had faith, and when they had walked around the city of Jericho for seven days, its walls fell down.
31 Rahab had been a prostitute, but she had faith and welcomed the spies. So she wasn't killed with the people who disobeyed.
32 What else can I say? There isn't enough time to tell about Gideon, Barak, Samson, Jephthah, David, Samuel, and the prophets. 33 Their faith helped them conquer kingdoms, and because they did right, God made promises to them. They closed the jaws of lions 34 and put out raging fires and escaped from the swords of their enemies. Although they were weak, they were given the strength and power to chase foreign armies away.
35 Some women received their loved ones back from death. Many of these people were tortured, but they refused to be released. They were sure they would get a better reward when the dead are raised to life. 36 Others were made fun of and beaten with whips, and some were chained in jail. 37 Still others were stoned to death or sawed in two or killed with swords. Some had nothing but sheep skins or goat skins to wear. They were poor, mistreated, and tortured. 38The world did not deserve these good people, who had to wander in deserts and on mountains and had to live in caves and holes in the ground.
39All of them pleased God because of their faith! But still they died without being given what had been promised. 40This was because God had something better in store for us. And he did not want them to reach the goal of their faith without us.
translation_text:
Kidiliɛ gi Kuziŋaa kunu i
1Abla ɛ, kuziŋaa kunu likla trɔtrɔ ní kidiliɛ gi kɛ kudeonu kwidi aʋa a, ani kukaka ni kidiliɛ gi kuʋímɔ ɛ aʋa. 2Lɛ ese linu si gani kuziŋa mɛ kanaa mɛ banima bɛzi kukpɔ̀kpɔ̀.
3Gani kuziŋa mɛ kwinu lɛ mɛ si Aya abitɛ kelea gani yɛ liboe panipani mɛ ɛ, gi lɛ loso o Aya abitɛ kidiliɛ gi kwimɔ ɛ dɔni kidiliɛ gi baʋímɔ ɛ mɛ.
4Gani kuziŋa mɛ Abel aki Aya bidɔpɔʋipɔʋi gi bipɛ sani Kain binɛ ʋɛ ɛ. Gani yɛ kuziŋaa mɛ ɛ, Aya akpɔ yɛ ŋwisi ɔnɔ gi yɛ bede ʋa bɛgyɔ ɔ, gi Aya akpɔ yɛ bidɔkɔkɔ ʋɛ ɛ. Gani yɛ kuziŋaa mɛ ɛ, Abɛl ɛɛpani omono gi etse kyɛ ɛ.
5Gani kuziŋa mɛ Aya efeke Henox asɛ nɔ gi ótse e, xe bámɔ yɛ tɔ ɔ, lɛ ese linu si Aya efeke ye asɛ nɔ. Kpɛ ɔtɔ kí lipoe lɛ gi biafeke Henox sɛnɔ ɛ, Aya akpɔ yɛ si onu ɔnɔ gi eku Ye aŋu u. 6Ɔnɔnɔ ɔ́tratani Aya aŋu kuku xegi kuziŋa kúma ní lɛ mɛ ɛ, lɛ se linu si ɔnɔ kakaa gi aaba Aya kɔ ɛ, lilɛ si ɔniliɛ klɔ ɔzɛ lɛ aʋa ŋa si Aya ɔlɛ, lɛ izɛɛtsa liʋɛ ɛki banama gi bɛpɛ́ yɛ veve e.
7Gani kuziŋa mɛ Noa gi Aya agba yɛ mɔmɔmɔ ní kidiliɛ gi kiʋíba kɛ mɛ sukɔ su i, enu kutu aki Aya lɛ awa onitsahu lo gi lɔ mɛ yɛ ni yɛ kepea petee bɛwɔ ligbã nɛ ɛ. Gani yɛ kuziŋaa mɛ ebu kelea petee lipɔ, lɛ Noa edzì ɔniliɛ gi gani yɛ kuziŋa mɛ ɛ, azɛ kaŋase ŋwisi ɔnɔ gi yɛ bede ʋa bɛgyɔ kinɛ ɛ.
8Gani kuziŋa mɛ Abraham enu kutu, lipoe lɛ gi Aya axwa yɛ ɛ, xe asi yɛ si, ɔsɛ tɔ ɔtrɛ ní kese dzedze e, gi kenu kaŋase e, gi Aya aka aplɛ si yɛ akɔ ki yɛ aʋa a. Asɛ lɛ atrɛ niklɔ gi óte e. 9Gani kuziŋa mɛ eze ŋwisi ɔbaba kinɛ ɛ, ní kesea lia klɔ gi Aya aka aplɛ aki yɛ aʋa a. Eze ní basa akpandɔ mɛ, nite ke Isak ni Yakob gi ba kyɛ biaŋa kaŋasea lia klɔ ɔ, gi ka Aya aka aplɛ ɛ, beze. 10Lɛ ese linu si Abraham azeedi kudeonu aki ɔmaa lɔ klɔ su gi Aya ekyi awla a, xe atsà a, ɔma gi lɔ ɔxɔ lɔ ɔ́trakyi liwewe e.
11Gani kuziŋa mɛ Abraham edzì obika gi nya yɛ Abraham etsi i, xe yedze Sara kyɛ eze okonɔ xe e, lɛ ese linu si ebù ɔniliɛ gi aka aplɛ aki yɛ si aabitɛ ní yɛ kakaplɛa aʋa a. 12Gi etsi i, xe ɔlɛ ní kutsede onu kyɛ ɛ, dɔnɔ ní ɔnɔ tole liɛ ya mɛ bebi gi bɛsrɛ ɛ, ŋwisi badzidziebi ʋa ní liwɔ lɛ mɛ ɛ, xe ba kiklakla mɛ kisrɛ ɛ, ŋwisi siklami sɛ ní axuta la a, gi batrátani kukla ɛ, mɛ bɛdɔ.
13Banɔ ba ya petee pɔ́ beze ní ba kuziŋaa aʋa a, xe betse e, gi ba ɔwla ɔ́ka kidiliɛ ɛ, gi Aya aka aplɛ ɛ. Pɔ̀ bɛmɔ ʋɛ dɔni ɔgblamadza kɔ, lɛ bɛkpɛ ʋɛ wolaa ɛdɔni ɔdzage no, lɛ bedra lɛ mɛ si benu bababa ni bedega ní kesea lia ya aʋa a. 14Lɛ banama gi bizɛɛpani eboa la ya elu i, bɛdra lɛ mɛ si bɛɛpɛ ba tutɔ ba kese. 15Baʋíbù kesea lia klɔ mɛ gi bedo ɛdɔ ɛ su, xe aní nite e, nya bemɔ kudeonu si biadzitrɛ ní niklɔ ɔ. 16Boŋu i, bɛɛpɛ kese gi kɛpɛ sanɔ ɛ, gi kenu lifunu ne kanɛ ɛ. Lɛ líku Aya kuye si baxwa yɛ si ba Aya a, lɛ ese linu si abla ɔma ʋidi aplɛ aki ba.
17Gani kuziŋa mɛ ɛ, Abraham akɔ Isak akyɛ ŋwisi bidɔpɔʋipɔʋi kí Aya kinɛ ɛ, gi Aya efeke ye edi i. Yɛ ɔniliɛ klɔ gi yɛ bɛka ɛplɛ kɔ ɛ, akɔ yebinyime tolee akyɛ ŋwisi bidɔpɔʋipɔʋi kinɛ ɛ, 18gi Aya asi yɛ si, “Gani Isak aʋa wɔamɔ wɔ besoebi ba a, xe wɔ linyi ne litrálila a.” 19Lɛ ese linu si akpɛ lɛ lixaŋu si, xegi Isak etse kyɛ ɛ, kigagla kilɛ ní Aya awla si, aayɔni Isak dɔni bekpo ʋa mɛ; lɛ ní anukwarɛɛ mɛ ɛ, kwiapani si Abraham anya azɛ Isak dɔni bekpo ʋa mɛ ɛ.
20Gani kuziŋaa mɛ ɛ, Isak aka kukrira aplɛ aki Yakob ni Esau ní ɔtɔ nɔ ewe la mɛ ɛ.
21Gani kuziŋaa mɛ ɛ Yakob, gi etsi xe atrátse e, akrira Yosef yɛ bebinyime tiaʋa ba gi aziɔni yɛ osevugavuga lɔ ɔ, xe esumu Aya a. 22Ní kuziŋa mɛ, lipoɛ lɛ gi Yosef ihatse e, apani ní Israel bebi ba kudodoo ní Egipte kesea mɛ su u, xe akpɛ ɔsã ní kiliɛ gi biagani yɛ axwa la su u.
23Kuziŋaa, kwikɔ si gi bɛpɔ Mose e, yɛ bapɔnɛ ba bekpo ye badzidzi tiata. Lɛ ese linu si bɛmɔ si, ónu obi ko, lɛ bése okusi ye yɛ ɔsã nɔ ɔ.
24Gani kuziŋaa mɛ Mose gi etsi i, ɔ́dimɛ si baxwa yɛ si Farao yebidzɛɛ Yebi tɔ ɔ; 25Lɛ loso akya si yɛ, yɛ amɔ ɔziã pɔni Aya yɛ banima sani si tɔ yimɔ isami lipoe kpekpemi itɔ ni eboe zimizi. 26Akpɛ lɛ lixaŋu si tɔ yimɔ kuye ní Kristo loso e, biŋa kedzi isani Egipteanɛ ma exo la. Lɛ ese linu si eedi kudeonu ki yɛ liʋɛtsatsa lɛ ní ɔtɔ nɔ mɛ ɛ. 27Gani kuziŋaa mɛ asɛ ní Egipte gi óse Egipteanɛ ma okusi ye yɛ kusuyeʋio o. Lize ŋwisi amɔ Aya gi baʋímɔ ɛ, lɛ loso ódi kede e. 28Gani kuziŋa mɛ, edu kutseo lidɔzawe le, xe befuflu ika lɛ ní ipupo le su u, gi lɛ loso kutseo ɔxwɛdzɛɛ óye Israel bebi ba ba bebinyime tɔpu ba a.
29Gani kuziŋa mɛ banima bɛga Axu kyikyi na mɛ ɛza ŋwisi kese kpaakpa aʋa kinɛ ɛ, lɛ gi Egipteanɛ ma fɛ bekeni edi si bɛɛbitɛ nite e, kuni yo kwivu ba. 30Gani kuziŋa mɛ Yerixo egli la ɛkpɔtɔ eku gi banima bɛga ɛklani la ewe tegloele e.
31Gani kuziŋa mɛ béye Raxab ɔwawadzee gi ápɔni banama gi bénu kutu aki Aya a, lɛ ese linu si yɛ azɛ Israel bebi beboesega ba ni iwla tiʋa a.
32Lɛ liboe woli maapani dzɛ ɛ? Lɛ ese linu si lipoe le lisrɛ ɛki mɛ tɔɔ si mipani ní Gideon, Barak, Simson, Yefta, Dawid, Samuel ani beboedoplɛ ba su u; 33Gani kuziŋa mɛ ɛ, bɛta bigu xe bɛŋa bekusi ba aʋa a. Bɛxwa etsi gyɔgyɔ ɛki banama gi ba linɛ ligyɔ ɛ, lɛ loso bɛzɛ kidiliɛ gi Aya aka aplɛ ɛ. Bɛkpɛ gyata ʋa inugu le bibɔ ʋɛ, 34beni bifu gagla ʋɛ, lɛ bedo ní banama gi bɛpɛ si beye ba ni ihɛɛ gblamadza ʋa iwla mɛ ɛ. Béze gagla pɔ̀ kigagla kiku ba mɛ, lɛ bedzi baguta ʋidi gi bɛŋa kùse dzedze baguta ba aʋa a. 35Badzɛ ba bɛzɛ ba baŋanɛa ba gi betse e, xe bedzini ba ɛyɔnɔ ɛ.
Banɔ dzedze fɛ gi bezee bu kini ikɔ ɔ, pɔ̀ xe badimɛ si bayurɔ ba a, lɛ loso betse ní kinie me, pɔ̀ bɛbitɛ nite tɔ biayɔ dzi ligbã́ gi lipɛ sanɔ ɔ. 36Batɔ fɛ bɛkpɛ ba kakpaa lɛ bekusi ba a, lɛ batɔ fɛ ɛ, bɛkpɛ ba bibɔ lɛ bɛgbani ba ɛtsinɔ ɛzɛkpɛ ligba a. 37Bɛsa ba akla, bɛlɛ ba ní kupɛ tuʋa mɛ, beye ba nì ihɛ gblamadza ʋa, bɛzɛga ɛklanɔ ní bamu ʋa ni iʋesi le kugaplekpaa mɛ, lɛ banima bezeebu kini ikí ba, lɛ bɛzɛɛwa ba nyɔnyɔɛ ɔkpɛ gi kididɔ kíze ní ba awla loso o, 38kelea mɛ kádɔ aki ba! Lɛ bɛzɛga ɛklanɔ ní ɔyaya klɔ ni kutokpaa aʋa a. Lɛ ipɛgla nɛ mɛ, ni ezokpo la mɛ eze ba izeklɔ ɔ.
39Lɛ banɔ ba ya petee bɛzɛ kukpɔ̀kpɔ̀ ɔkpɛ ba kuziŋaa, pɔ̀ kyɛ ɛ, bázɛ kidiliɛ gi kɛ Aya aka aplɛ ɛ. 40Lɛ ese linu si Aya abitɛ kutrɔtrɔ gbã aki blɔ. Yɛ kutrɔtrɔ kunu si blɔ kwiapɔni ba xe ableke biana a.

english_text:
A Large Crowd of Witnesses
1 Such a large crowd of witnesses is all around us! So we must get rid of everything that slows us down, especially the sin that just won't let go. And we must be determined to run the race that is ahead of us. 2 We must keep our eyes on Jesus, who leads us and makes our faith complete. He endured the shame of being nailed to a cross, because he knew later on he would be glad he did. Now he is seated at the right side of God's throne! 3So keep your mind on Jesus, who put up with many insults from sinners. Then you won't get discouraged and give up.
4None of you have yet been hurt in your battle against sin. 5 But you have forgotten that the Scriptures say to God's children,
“When the Lord punishes you,
don't make light of it,
and when he corrects you,
don't be discouraged.
6The Lord corrects the people
he loves
and disciplines those
he calls his own.”
7Be patient when you are being corrected! This is how God treats his children. Don't all parents correct their children? 8God corrects all his children, and if he doesn't correct you, then you don't really belong to him. 9Our earthly fathers correct us, and we still respect them. Isn't it even better to be given true life by letting our spiritual Father correct us?
10Our human fathers correct us for a short time, and they do it as they think best. But God corrects us for our own good, because he wants us to be holy, as he is. 11It is never fun to be corrected. In fact, at the time it is always painful. But if we learn to obey by being corrected, we will do right and live at peace.
12 Now stand up straight! Stop your knees from shaking 13 and walk a straight path. Then lame people will be healed, instead of getting worse.
Warning against Turning from God
14Try to live at peace with everyone! Live a clean life. If you don't, you will never see the Lord. 15 Make sure no one misses out on God's wonderful kindness. Don't let anyone become bitter and cause trouble for the rest of you. 16 Watch out for immoral and ungodly people like Esau, who sold his future blessing for only one meal. 17 You know how he later wanted it back. But there was nothing he could do to change things, even though he begged his father and cried.
18 You have not come to a place like Mount Sinai that can be seen and touched. There is no flaming fire or dark cloud or storm 19or trumpet sound. The people of Israel heard a voice speak. But they begged it to stop, 20 because they could not obey its commands. They were even told to kill any animal that touched the mountain. 21The sight was so frightening that Moses said he shook with fear.
22You have now come to Mount Zion and to the heavenly Jerusalem. This is the city of the living God, where thousands and thousands of angels have come to celebrate. 23Here you will find all of God's dearest children, whose names are written in heaven. And you will find God himself, who judges everyone. Here also are the spirits of those good people who have been made perfect. 24 And Jesus is here! He is the one who makes God's new agreement with us, and his sprinkled blood says much better things than the blood of Abel.
25 Make sure you obey the one who speaks to you. The people did not escape, when they refused to obey the one who spoke to them at Mount Sinai. Do you think you can possibly escape, if you refuse to obey the one who speaks to you from heaven? 26 When God spoke the first time, his voice shook only the earth. This time he has promised to shake the earth once again, and heaven too.
27The words “once again” mean that these created things will someday be shaken and removed. Then what cannot be shaken will last. 28We should be grateful we were given a kingdom that cannot be shaken. And in this kingdom we please God by worshiping him and by showing him great honor and respect. 29 Our God is like a destructive fire!
translation_text:
Aya yɛ kuheheo kɔ kuzɛɛbasɛ yɛ kunɔlɔ ɔ
1Lɛ loso gi banima lihakpɔ ʋidi ilɛ gi liiŋa katsalia ɛki blɔ liklani blɔ ɛ, mlaki tɔ kuyurɔ kidɔ kakaa gi kiitsí blɔ kudeonu ni eboe zimizi ana gi ɛnya blɔ gagla tɔ mlaki tɔ kuse nì kimbi kyíkyĩ kuseseo liɔ klɔ gi kulɛ ní blɔ ɔtɔ nɔ ɛ. 2Mlaki tɔ kukɔ blɔ aŋu na trɔ ní Yesu aʋa, gi yɛ onu blɔ liŋwitsɛ lɛ ɛ, xe yɛ aaki si blɔ kuziŋaa kwina ɛ. Lɛ ese linu si gi ete isamii nɛ ɛ, gi aamɔ dɔni Aya kɔ ɛ, evu ɔna akpɛ lipo lɛ amɔ ɔziã nɔ, lɛ osetsase lo su kutseo kúku ye kuye e. Lɛ eze ní Aya yɛ likusigbo le lɛ kaŋawla mɛ ɛ.
3Mlebù eboa la mɛ gi aga aza a, dɔni banɔ zimizi ba iwla mɛ, kiliɛ gi bɛtrɔ yɛ ahwafi i! atɔ mlakípè tɔ ɔna okído ní mlɔ lipo o.
4Lɛ ese linu si gani mlɔ kuŋwlɛŋwlɛɔ nì eboe zimizi na mɛ kyɛ ɛ, mlaʋína niklɔ gi biabali mlɔ ika lɛ ní kese sukɔ ɔ. 5Lɛ kidze mledze liboe lɛ gi Aya asi mlɔ gi akpɛ mlɔ sise ŋwisi yɛ bebi ba kinɛ na a? Gi lipani si,
“Mobi ye, wukíkɔ Ɔkaɛ yɛ kutuheheo ŋwisi kidɔ tsɛ́ɛ kinɛ ɛ,
lɛ likípè wo xegi aka aŋu mɛ aki wɔ ɔ.
6Lɛ ese linu si Ɔkaɛ izɛɛka aŋu mɛ ɛki ɔnɔ kakaa gi alɔ ɛ.
Lɛ izeehe kutu iiki ɔnɔ kakaa gi azɛ si Yebi i.”
7Mlevu ɔna ní ɔziã mɔmɔ mɛ ŋwisi kuhehe kinɛ ɛ, lɛ ese linu si Aya eehe mlo ŋwisi yɛ bebi kinɛ ɛ, obi woli ɔlɛ gi yɛka óʋihe ye e? 8Xegi baʋíhe kutu iiki mlɔ ɛ, ŋwisi kiliɛ gi bizehe ki ɔnɔ kakaa kinɛ ɛ, kɔ libasɛ si ɔma mɛ bebi mlenu i, kɔ lɔ mlénu yɛ ɔtutɔ lɔ yɛ bebii o. 9Kɔpɔni lɛ ya ɛ, blɔ kisugyaɛ mɛ baka ba gi bizeehe kutu iiki blɔ ɛ, dani kuzɛɛkpɛ kubùbù yo ní ba su i? Lɛ ege loso kutrábubɔ isu ya ɛkpɛ ní Aya blɔ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ mɛ Ɔkaɛ ese e, xe kwiaze ligbã́ nɛ ɛ? 10Blɔ kisugyaɛ mɛ baka ba behe kutu iiki blɔ lipoe kpekpemi itɔ ko, ní kiliɛ gi begyodi si kipɛ ɛ onu, pɔ̀ Aya kɔ ɛ izeehe kutu iiki blɔ ki blɔ kupɛ̀pɛ ɛ, atɔ blɔ kyɛ kudra ŋwisi yɛ kiniɛ. 11Xegi beehe kutu iiki blɔ ɛ, lizɛɛbitɛ ɛki blɔ si kwinu kuyeʋiyeʋi lɛ aní isami i; pɔ̀ lɛ kede e, banama gi bɛga kutuheheo liɔ ya mɛ ɛ, bizɛmɔ si balɛ ni kude gyɔgyɔ aʋa lɛ bizɛmɔ kusuyɔ ni ligbã́zeze gbã.
12Lɛ loso e, mlakpɛ mlɔ iwla lɛ gi ipe e, kigagla tɔ mlaki mlɔ aklamasi ana gi la mɛ eesugu i kigagla, 13atɔ mlebu bede gyɔgyɔ ʋa aki mlɔ ikli lɛ, tɔ banama gi bɛgbà fɛ ɛ, bakígleʋe pɔ̀ tɔ biayɔ ɔ.
Mlata Aya kutu
14Mlapɛ tɔ mleze ní kusuyɔ nì ɔnɔ kakaa, atɔ mleze ligbã́ dradra fɛ; lɛ ese linu si ɔnɔnɔ ɔ́tratani Ɔkaɛ kumɔ ɔ, xegi ɔ́dra a. 15Mledi mumɔ, tɔ ɔnɔnɔ ɔkidzi kede ní Aya yɛ lipomepɛpɛ nɛ su u. Mlɔmɛ ɔnɔnɔ okídzi osegu tiitii gi aakpɛ kunugu mlɔ kusi i, xe lɔ aɖi ye aazizi banɔ glegle e. 16Mledi le aʋa si ɔnɔnɔ okídzi ɔhasi nɛ, loo ɔnɔ gi óse Aya a, ŋwisi Esau kinɛ ɛ, gi adà yɛ otsitsizeklɔ kɔkpɛ bidɔŋaŋa kezi tiele loso o. 17Lɛ ese linu si mlete si, lɛ kede e, agi apɛ si yizɛ yɛ kaŋasea nì kukrira ɛdɔni yɛka klɔ ɛ, ɔ́kɔ ʋɛ ɔ́ki yɛ ɛ, lɛ ese linu si ɔ́tani kidiliɛ gi abitɛ ɛ, kukyi gi nya apɛ kukriraa nì kube xe e.
18Lɛ ese linu si aní Sinai lito le gi biagbɔnɔ ɔ, loo lito gi liisɔ ɔ, kanɔmɛ gi kɛbitɛ yɔɔ ɔ, loo litumu ni liwɔ gagla su mlaba lɛ ɛ. 19Mlénu kakuklui ya ole gagla lɔ, loo ole gi ɛɛpani eboe gi banama gi benu la ɛ, bɛki litukpo si bakípani liboeboe asi ba tɔɔ ɔ. 20Lɛ ese linu si bátani ɔsã nɔ gi akpɛ aki ba ɛ, lɔ onu kulɛ. “Xegi ɔga asasani lito le fɛ ɛ, lilɛ si basa yɛ akla tɔ otse.” 21Kidiliɛ gi amɔ níklɔ ʋa ɛ, kikɔ si Mose apani si: “Lifu liku me gi meesugu u.”
