Lɛ ese linu si ɔnɔ onu ɔnɔdzidzi ki kidɔ kakaa gi kiŋa yɛ aʋa ɛ. 20Lɛ xe banima bese ní kelea eboe zimizi na onu gani blɔ Ɔkaɛ ni ɔnɔbu ye Yesu Kristo kutete me e, xe bedzini ba kunya ɛkpɛ ní eboe zimizi na mɛ dzɛ ɛ, xe ɛɛŋa ba aʋa ɛ, banɔ ba klɔ ba ba kizezee me kizɛnyani sani kiliɛ gi kiize tsã ɛ, xe ableke bedzi baʋaziŋa ɛ. 21Liapɛ ki ba sanɔ xegi béte kude gyɔgyɔɔ sani si betè ko o, pɔ̀ tɔ bekyi kede akpɛ kutrɔtrɔ dradra liɔ klɔ gi bɛki ba ɛ. 22Kidiliɛ gi kɛ kiba ba aʋa ɛ, kibasɛ si abee ʋa gi bizebu si “Kadroʋia kize edzini zemini ka sihlàhlà sɛ,” ni “ɔha gi bewlo no e, izedzi zewlo lɔ livɔ lɛ ɛ,” bali ní lɛ mɛ.

english_text:
The Lord Will Return
1My dear friends, this is the second letter I have written to encourage you to do some honest thinking. I don't want you to forget 2what God's prophets said would happen. You must never forget what the holy prophets taught in the past. And you must remember what the apostles told you our Lord and Savior has commanded us to do.
3 But first you must realize that in the last days some people won't think about anything except their own selfish desires. They will make fun of you 4and say, “Didn't your Lord promise to come back? Yet the first leaders have already died, and the world hasn't changed a bit.”
5 They will say this because they want to forget that long ago the heavens and the earth were made at God's command. The earth came out of water and was made from water. 6 Later it was destroyed by the waters of a mighty flood. 7But God has commanded the present heavens and earth to remain until the day of judgment. Then they will be set on fire, and ungodly people will be destroyed.
8 Dear friends, don't forget that for the Lord one day is the same as 1,000 years, and 1,000 years is the same as one day. 9The Lord isn't slow about keeping his promises, as some people think he is. In fact, God is patient, because he wants everyone to turn from sin and no one to be lost.
10 The day of the Lord's return will surprise us like a thief. The heavens will disappear with a loud noise, and the heat will melt the whole universe. Then the earth and everything on it will be seen for what they are.
11Everything will be destroyed. So you should serve and honor God by the way you live. 12You should look forward to the day when God judges everyone, and you should try to make it come soon. On that day the heavens will be destroyed by fire, and everything else will melt in the heat. 13 But God has promised us a new heaven and a new earth, where justice will rule. We are really looking forward to this!
14My friends, while you are waiting, you should make certain the Lord finds you pure, spotless, and living at peace. 15Don't forget that the Lord is patient because he wants people to be saved. This is also what our dear friend Paul said when he wrote you with the wisdom God had given him. 16Paul talks about these same things in all his letters, but part of what he says is hard to understand. Some ignorant and unsteady people even destroy themselves by twisting what he said. They do the same thing with other Scriptures too.
17My dear friends, you have been warned ahead of time! So don't let the errors of evil people lead you down the wrong path and make you lose your balance. 18Let the gift of undeserved grace and the understanding that come from our Lord and Savior Jesus Christ help you keep on growing. Praise Jesus now and forever! Amen.
translation_text:
Kakaplɛ ní blɔ Ɔkaɛ yɛ kubabaa su
1Mɛ baʋia dimɛnɔ ba, keplekpa ʋlaa gi mɛɛŋwlimi kimlɔ ɛ, ka ka lia ya abla. Maŋwlimi kuplekpa tuʋa liɔ ya si mihwani mlɔ begyodi ʋa tɔ ba mɛ badra ni eboa la ya su u. 2Mapɛ si mlaplɛ aŋu ní eboa la gi beboedoplɛ dradra ba bɛpani plɛ koko aʋa a, ni ɔsãa nɔ gi blɔ Ɔkaɛ ni Ɔnɔbu ye akɔ ɔ, gi banɔkpɛkpɛ ba bɛki mlɔ ɛ aʋa.
3Ní ɔtɔpu kɔ ɛ, lilɛ si mlaplɛ aŋu si, ní ewe gɔgɔ la mɛ ɛ, banama gi bizɛmwɛ banima ɛ, biaba. Banama gi biazɛkpɛ anukwarɛɛ kakpaa, lɛ biazeni ba tutɔ ba bapɛ́pɛ zimizi ba kude e. 4Biaʋi si, “Aka aplɛ si yɛ aaba, nifɔ yɛ kakaplɛ alia ya kalɛ ɛ? Dɔni lipoe lɛ ke gi blɔ baka ba betse e, kɔkpɛni abla ɛ, kidɔ kakaa kiitrɛ ní kɛ aʋa ŋwisi kiliɛ gi lize dɔni kelea likpese le kinɛ ɛ.” 5Pɔ̀ ɔŋwadzuu bedze si gani Aya yɛ liboe le onu lifunu ne lize e, xe akɔ kuni yo abitɛ kesea a, xe aki si kesea kedo edɔ ni kuni yo me e. 6Lɛ kuni yiyio ke akɔ akyrɔ kesea lipoe lɛ klɔ mɛ. 7Lɛ gani Aya yɛ liboe le me ke lifunu ne ni kesea lia gi kalɛ abla ɛ, bɛbla bɛ ɛki kifu yɛ, gi bɛbla bɛ ɛki litsixwaxwa ni kukyrɔkyrɔ ki banama gi bése Aya ɛ.
8Pɔ̀ mlakídze liboe tile lɛ ya a, mɛ baʋia ba! Ni Ɔkaɛ klɔ ɛ, liwe tile lilɛ ŋwisi agɔ akpe tole kinɛ ɛ, lɛ agɔ akpe tole alɛ ŋwisi liwe tile kinɛ ɛ. 9Aní si Ɔkaɛ ɔ́ʋikɔsi ní yɛ kakaplɛa su u, ŋwisi kiliɛ gi banɔatɔ beegyo le edi kinɛ ɛ. Boŋu i, akɔ litɔ plɛplɛ kese aki mlɔ lɛ ese linu si ɔ́pɛ si ɔnɔnɔ ɔkyurɔ, pɔ̀ apɛ́ si ɔnɔ kakaa okyi ní yɛ eboe zimizi na mɛ ɛ.
10Pɔ̀ Ɔkaɛ yɛ Liwe le litrába ní lipoe lɛ mɛ gi mlatráze ní aŋu plɛplɛ mɛ ɛ. Ní liwe lɛ klɔ aʋa ɛ, lifunu ne lialila ni ozi gagla, lɛ liwɔ lɛ mɛ bidɔbitɛbitɛ ʋɛ petee biazizi lɛ biakani, lɛ kesea ni kidɔ kakaa ní ka aʋa ɛ, biakani. Lɛ banima biamɔ kidɔ kakaa kiliɛ gi kilɛ ɛ.
11Agi kidɔ kakaa kitrázizi gani kudeo liɔ ya aʋa ɛ, banɔ wolilu lilɛ si mlezee? Lilɛ si mleze ligbã́ gi lidra tɔ mlakɔ isu aki Aya a, 12gi mleepe Aya yɛ Liwe le e, tɔ mlekeni edi tɔ liba kpekpee. Liwe lɛ klɔ litramani bidɔ zizizizi gi lifunu ne liakani i, xe liwɔ lɛ mɛ kudɔkpaa kwiawlɔ ní kifu yɛ kɛ kuhuhu yo loso o. 13Pɔ̀ ní yɛ kakaplɛa onu i, kwidi kudeonu iiki lifunu vuvu ni kese vuvu, niklɔ gi aaze kepe ki kude gyɔgyɔ ɛ.
14Lɛ loso mɛ baʋia ba, agi mleedi kudeonu iiki lɛ ya ɛ, mlekeni edi tɔ mladra, tɔ kunutsatsaa tɔtɔ kukíze ní mlɔ su u, ní Aya aŋu mɛ tɔ mleze ní kusuyɔ mɛ ni yɛ. 15Mlaplɛ aŋu si blɔ Ɔkaɛ yɛ litɔplɛplɛkese kese e, kunu kudeonu gi aki mlɔ tɔ mlamɔ kubúbú ŋwisi kiliɛ gi blɔ onemi dimɛnɔɛ Paulo aŋwlimi aki mlɔ ɛ, gani kuboete uliɔ gi Aya aki yɛ mɛ ɛ. 16Lɛ ya su apani ní yɛ kuplekpaa petee gi aŋwlimi aki mlɔ mɛ ɛ. Eboe atɔ alɛ ní yɛ kuplekpaa mɛ gi la ese kununu yo kuli gagla, lɛ banama gi béte e, ni banama gi ba begyodi ʋa bamá ní ɔkɔ tole e, ba eboa la klɔ ʋa ese kububu i, binu asia, ŋwisi kiliɛ gi bizɛbitɛ nì Aya yɛ liboe gɔgɔ lɛ kyɛ ɛ. Gani lɛ ya mɛ ɛ, bizɛmani kuzizizizi ni ba isu ya aʋa a.
17Lɛ loso mɛ baʋia ba, agi mlete lɛ ya kooko e, mleze ni aŋu, tɔ banama gi bizɛɛta isã nɛ ese e, bakígbani mlɔ akpɛ ní kesetata mɛ tɔ mlakílila mlɔ ozekɔ lɔ gi mlalɛ ɛ. 18Boŋu i, mlatrɛ lɛ aʋa tɔ mletsi ní lipomepɛpɛ ni kuboete ní blɔ Ɔkaɛ ani blɔ Ɔnɔbu ye Yesu Kristo me. Kusudraa kuze yɛ kunɛ dɔni abla kɔtsinɔ kanɔmɛ anɔmɛ! Atɔ liba lɛ mɛ.

english_text:
1 The Word that gives life
was from the beginning,
and this is the one
our message is about.
Our ears have heard,
our eyes have seen,
and our hands have touched
this Word.
2 The one who gives life appeared! We saw it happen, and we are witnesses to what we have seen. Now we are telling you about this eternal life that was with the Father and appeared to us. 3We are telling you what we have seen and heard, so you may share in this life with us. And we share in it with the Father and with his Son Jesus Christ. 4We are writing to tell you these things, because this makes us truly happy.
God Is Light
5Jesus told us God is light and doesn't have any darkness in him. Now we are telling you.
6If we say we share in life with God and keep on living in the dark, we are lying and are not living by the truth. 7 But if we live in the light, as God does, we share in life with each other. And the blood of his Son Jesus washes all our sins away. 8If we say we have not sinned, we are fooling ourselves, and the truth isn't in our hearts. 9But if we confess our sins to God, he can always be trusted to forgive us and take our sins away.
10If we say we have not sinned, we make God a liar, and his message isn't in our hearts.
translation_text:
Yesu Kristo onu Liboe lɛ gi lizɛɛki Ligbã
1Kwipani kimlɔ ní Liboe lɛ gi lizɛɛki ligbã a, gi lize dɔni okpese klɔ ke su u. Kwinu le, lɛ kwimɔ lɛ ni blɔ bimbi ʋe e, kwimɔ lɛ, lɛ kwigbɔni lɛ ni blɔ awla la a. 2Gi ligbã nɛ liŋwi i, kwimɔ lɛ, lɛ́ kwipani si mlɔ ní ligbãmoblo le su gi lize ní Kaka Aya klɔ ɛ, xe ebu le abasɛ blɔ ɛ. 3Kidiliɛ gi kwimɔ xe kwinu i, kɛ kwiipani si mlɔ fɛ, tɔ mlakpɛ likpɔ ni blɔ ní likpɔkpɛkpɛ lɛ gi lilɛ ní blɔ nì Kakaa Aya ani Yebi ye Yesu Kristo iwla mɛ ɛ. 4Lɛ kwiiŋwlimi eboa la ya tɔ blɔ isami nɛ iflɔ ɔ.
Kugaga ní Kuklekleo onu
5Abla ɛ, ole ɔlɔ klɔ gi kwinu dɔni Yebi ye klɔ ɔ, xe kwiipani i si mlɔ onu si, Aya onu kuklekle, lɛ litumu itɔtɔ lima ní yɛ mɛ ɛ. 6Xegi kwipani si likpɔkpɛkpɛ lilɛ blɔ ni yɛ kusi i, pɔ̀ xe lipoe lɛ klɔ mɛ ke e, kukpasi ní litumu me, kwiisɛ asia lɛ anukwarɛɛ ɔ́ma ni blɔ mɛ. 7Pɔ̀ xe kwiga ní kuklekle me ŋwisi kiliɛ gi Yɛ ɔlɛ ní kuklekle me kinɛ ɛ, kɔlɔ kulɛ ní likpɔkpɛkpɛ mɛ ni blɔ ahanuglɔ ba lɛ Yebi ye Yesu ika lɛ iaplo eboe zimizi kakaa ní blɔ su u.
8Xe kwipani si kesetata atɔtɔ káma ní blɔ su i, kwimeni isu lɛ anukwarɛɛ ɔ́ma ní blɔ mɛ ɛ. 9Pɔ̀ xe kwidra blɔ ina nɛ mɛ ɛki Aya ɛ, onu anukwarɛ onete, aalɛ ní yɛ kakaplɛa aʋa, aabitɛ liboe gbã, aakɔ blɔ eboe zimizi na kyɛ blɔ, lɛ aaplo eboe zimizi kakaa gi kwibitɛ ní blɔ su u. 10Xe kwisi si eboe zimizi áma blɔ su i, kwibitɛ si Aya odzi ɔsia nɛ, lɛ yɛ liboe le líma ní blɔ ligbã́ nɛ mɛ ɛ.

english_text:
Christ Helps Us
1My children, I am writing this so you won't sin. But if you do sin, Jesus Christ always does the right thing, and he will speak to the Father for us. 2Christ is the sacrifice that takes away our sins and the sins of all the world's people.
3When we obey God, we are sure we know him. 4But if we claim to know him and don't obey him, we are lying and the truth isn't in our hearts. 5We truly love God only when we obey him as we should, and then we know we belong to him. 6If we say we are his, we must follow the example of Christ.
The New Commandment
7 My dear friends, I am not writing to give you a new commandment. It is the same one you were first given, and it is the message you heard. 8But it really is a new commandment, and you know its true meaning, just as Christ does. You can see the darkness fading away and the true light already shining.
9If we claim to be in the light and hate someone, we are still in the dark. 10But if we love others, we are in the light, and we don't cause problems for them. 11If we hate others, we are living and walking in the dark. We don't know where we are going, because we can't see in the dark.
12Children, I am writing you,
because your sins
have been forgiven
in the name of Christ.
13Parents, I am writing you,
because you have known
the one who was there
from the beginning.
Young people, I am writing you,
because you have defeated
the evil one.
14Children, I am writing you,
because you have known
the Father.
Parents, I am writing you,
because you have known
the one who was there
from the beginning.
Young people, I am writing you,
because you are strong.
God's message is firm
in your hearts,
and you have defeated
the evil one.
15Don't love the world or anything that belongs to the world. If you love the world, you cannot love the Father. 16Our foolish pride comes from this world, and so do our selfish desires and our desire to have everything we see. None of this comes from the Father. 17The world and the desires it causes are disappearing. But if we obey God, we will live forever.
The Enemy of Christ
18Children, this is the last hour. You heard that the enemy of Christ would appear at this time, and many of Christ's enemies have already appeared. So we know the last hour is here. 19These people came from our own group, yet they were not really part of us. If they had been part of us, they would have stayed with us. But they left, which proves they did not belong to our group.
20Christ, the Holy One, has blessed you, and now all of you understand. 21I did not need to write you about the truth, since you already know it. You also know that liars do not belong to the truth. 22And a liar is anyone who says Jesus isn't truly Christ. Anyone who says this is an enemy of Christ and rejects both the Father and the Son. 23If we reject the Son, we reject the Father. But if we say we accept the Son, we have the Father. 24Keep thinking about the message you first heard, and you will always be one in your heart with the Son and with the Father. 25The Son has promised us eternal life.
26I am writing to warn you about those people who are misleading you. 27But Christ has blessed you with the Holy Spirit. Now the Spirit stays in you, and you don't need any teachers. The Spirit is truthful and teaches you everything. So stay one in your heart with Christ, just as the Spirit has taught you to do.
Children of God
28Children, stay one in your hearts with Christ. Then when he returns, we will have confidence and won't have to hide in shame. 29You know Christ always does right and everyone who does right is a child of God.
translation_text:
Kristo izɛzi eboe ní blɔ onu
1Mɛɛŋwlimi eboa la ya ɛki mlɔ mɛ bebi ba, tɔ mlakíbitɛ eboe zimizi i, pɔ̀ xe ɔnutɔ abitɛ liboe zimizi i, ɔnɔ ɔlɛ ní blɔ awla gi izɛɛpani ní blɔ onu ki blɔ Ɔka Aya a, gi onu Yesu Kristo e, ɔnɔ gi kesetata káma yɛ su i. 2Lɛ Kristo ɔtutɔ akɔ̀ isu akɔ lɛ gani yɛ aʋa ɛ, Aya akɔ blɔ eboe zimizi kakaa akyɛ ɛ, lɛ aní blɔ tètè pɔ̀ kelea petee ka eboe zimizi na fɛ.
3Xe kwibitɛ ní Aya yɛ ɔsã nɔ aʋa ɛ, kɔ kwika lɛ aʋa si kwite ye e. 4Banama gi bizɛɛpani si bete ye e, pɔ̀ xe baʋíbitɛ ní yɛ ɔsã nɔ aʋa ɛ, benu basia nɛ lɛ anukwarɛ ɔtɔtɔ ɔ́ma ní bamɛ ɛ. 5Pɔ̀ xe kwibitɛ yɛ eboe la aʋa ɛ, kɔ blɔ kunu banama mɛ gi Aya kulɔlɔ kwiflɔ tututu i. Lɛ mɛ kwiaka ní lɛ aʋa si kulɛ ní likpɔkpɛkpɛ mɛ ni Aya. 6Banama gi bizɛɛpani si balɛ ní likpɔkpɛkpɛ mɛ ni Aya ɛ, lilɛ si beze ligbã́ ŋwisi kiliɛ gi Yesu Kristo eze kinɛ ɛ.
Mleze Ligbã́ ni Kuklekle me
7Mɛ benemi lɔlɔ nɛ ba, aní ɔsã vuvu mɛɛŋwlimi iiki mlɔ, pɔ̀ ɔsã koko onu u, gi bɛki mlɔ ɛdɔni okpese kɔ ɔ. Ɔsã koko lo onu liboe lɛ gi mlenu koko o. 8Pɔ̀ fɛ mɛɛŋwlimi ɔsã vuvu iiki mlɔ, gi lɔ mɛ anukwarɛɛ ɛɛŋwi ní Kristo me anì mlɔ mɛ ɛ, lɛ ese linu si litumu ne liiza, lɛ kuklekleo ɔtutɔ lɔ kwikle koko o.
9Banama gi bɛɛpani si balɛ ní kuklekleo me e, pɔ̀ bevu ba benemi ba ɛkpɛ lipo me e, balɛ litumu ne me kɔkpɛ ni abla. 10Banama gi bɛdimɛ ni ba benemi ba ɛ, bá balɛ ní kuklekleo me, lɛ kididɔ kíma ní ba mɛ gi kiaki si biabitɛ eboe zimizi i. 11Pɔ̀ banama gi bevu ba benemi ba ɛkpɛ ni lipo me e, balɛ ní litumu ne me, bɛɛga lɛ mɛ lɛ béte niklɔ gi bɛɛtrɛ ɛ, lɛ ese linu si litumu ne lini ba bimbi ʋe.
12Mɛ bebi ba, mɛɛŋwlimi iiki mlɔ, lɛ ese linu si Aya akɔ mlɔ eboe zimizi na akyɛ mlɔ kɔkpɛ Kristo loso o. 13Baka ba, mɛɛŋwlimi iiki mlɔ, lɛ ese linu si mlete ye gi ɔlɛ dɔni okpese klɔ ɛ. Ogbógboha lɔ, mɛɛŋwlimi iiki mlɔ, lɛ ese linu si mlɛŋa Ozimizi ye aʋa.
14Mɛ bebi ba, mɛɛŋwlimi iiki mlɔ, lɛ ese linu si mlete blɔ Ɔka Aya. Baka ba, mɛɛŋwlimi ki mlɔ, lɛ ese linu si mlete ɔniliɛ klɔ gi ɔlɛ dɔni okpese klɔ ɛ. Ogbógboha lɔ, mɛɛŋwlimi iiki mlɔ, lɛ ese linu si mlalɛ gagla, lɛ Aya yɛ Liboe le likpasi ní mlɔ mɛ, lɛ mlɛŋa ozimizi ye aʋa a.
Mlakídimɛ ni Kelea mɛ
15Mlakídimɛ ni kelea mɛ loo kidɔ kakaa gi kilɛ ní kelea mɛ ɛ. Xe mlɛdimɛ ni kelea mɛ ɛ, kɔlɔ mládimɛ ni blɔ Ɔka Aya. 16Kidɔ kakaa ní kelea mɛ ŋwisi kulekuku nyani nyani, kidiliɛ gi banima bɛmɔ xe bɛɛpɛ ɛ, ani kidɔ kakaa ní kelea mɛ gi kɛ aʋa banima bizɛɛdɔ̀ isu ya ɛ, la litɔtɔ lídɔ Aya kɔ ɔ; la petee ɛdɔ ní kelea mɛ. 17Kelea mɛ ni ka kidɔ kakaa gi banima bizɛpɛ ɛ, biiza; pɔ̀ banama gi bizɛbitɛ ní Aya yɛ kupɛ́pɛɔ aʋa ɛ, biaze tsini kanɔmɛ anɔmɛ.
Kugbagba Ni Kristo Yɛ Ahwafianɛ ma Su
18Mɛ bebi ba, kelea ligù nɛ lina pɔ́! Bɛ si mlɔ si Kristo yɛ ahwafiɔnɛɛ aaba, lɛ abla ɛ, Kristo yɛ ahwafianɛ glegle bɛba kooko, lɛ loso kwite si kelea ligù nɛ lina pɔ́. 19Banɔ ba ya ɛ, béze ní blɔ likpɔkpɛkpɛ lɛ mɛ, lɛ loso bɛyurɔ blɔ, atɛɛsi balɛ ní blɔ likpɔkpɛkpɛ lɛ mɛ ɛ, nya beze ni blo, pɔ̀ bɛsɛ tɔ libasɛ si ba mɛ ɔtɔtɔ ɔ́nyanu blɔ ɔnɛ ɛ.
20Pɔ mlɛzɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ Dradra lɔ gi Kristo aki mlɔ ɛ, lɛ mlɔ petee mlete anukwarɛɛ ɛ. 21Mɛɛŋwlimi iiki mlɔ, aní sì mléte anukwarɛɛ, boŋu i, mlete ye, lɛ mlete fɛ si asia atɔtɔ aʋídɔ ní anukwarɛɛ mɛ ɛ.
22Kɔ nyaŋwɛ onu ɔsia nɛ ɛ? Yɛ onu ɔniliɛ gi ɛɛpani si aní Yesu onu Kristo ye e. Ɔniliɛ klɔ elu i yɛ onu Kristo yɛ ahwafiɔnɛɛ. Yɛ anada Blɔ Ɔkaɛ Aya ni Yebi ye. 23Lɛ banama gi bɛnada Obi ye e, ba ké bɛnada blɔ Ɔka Aya, banama gi bɛzi Obi ye e, ba ké bɛzi blɔ Ɔka Aya fɛ.
24Mledi lɛ aʋa, tɔ mlabla liboe lɛ gi mlenu idɔni okpese klɔ ní mlɔ ina nɛ mɛ ɛ. Xe mlɛbla liboe lɛ klɔ ɛ, kɔlɔ mlaze ní likpɔkpɛkpɛ mɛ ni Obi ye ani blɔ Ɔka Aya. 25Lɛ lɛ ya Kristo ɔtutɔ aka aplɛ si, yɛ aki blɔ ligbã moblo le e.
26Mɛɛŋwlimi la ya ɛki mlɔ ní banama gi bɛɛpɛ si beemeni mlo su u. 27Pɔ̀ mlɔ kɔ ɛ, Kristo enye yɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ abali ní mlɔ aʋa kooko. Kɔkpɛ gi yɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ ɔkpasi mlɔ mɛ ɛ, mlázia ɔnɔnɔ si ɔbasɛ mlɔ bidɔ ɔ. Lɛ ese linu si yɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ izɛbasɛ mlɔ kidɔ kakaa, lɛ kidiliɛ gi izɛbasɛ ɛ, kinu anukwarɛ aní asia a. Lɛ loso mlabitɛ ní Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ bidɔbasɛbasɛ ʋɛ aʋa, tɔ mleze ní likpɔkpɛkpɛ mɛ ni Kristo.
28Lɛ abla ɛ, mɛ bebi ba, mleze ní likpɔkpɛkpɛ mɛ ni yɛ, tɔ xe aaŋwi i, ɔna aaze ní blɔ lipo tɔ likízia si kukpo ní kuye me ní yɛ su ní yɛ liba we le aʋa a. 29Xe mlete si kesetata káma ní Kristo su i, lilɛ si mlete si ɔnɔ kakaa gi izɛɛbitɛ bidɔ gbã ɛ, onu Aya Yebi.

english_text:
1 Think how much the Father loves us. He loves us so much that he lets us be called his children, as we truly are. But since the people of this world did not know who Christ is, they don't know who we are. 2My dear friends, we are already God's children, though what we will be hasn't yet been seen. But we do know when Christ returns, we will be like him, because we will see him as he truly is. 3This hope makes us keep ourselves holy, just as Christ is holy.
4Everyone who sins breaks God's law, because sin is the same as breaking God's law. 5 You know Christ came to take away our sins. He isn't sinful, 6and people who stay one in their hearts with him won't keep on sinning. If they do keep on sinning, they don't know Christ, and they have never seen him.
7Children, don't be fooled. Anyone who does right is good, just like Christ himself. 8Anyone who keeps on sinning belongs to the devil. He has sinned from the beginning, but the Son of God came to destroy all that he has done. 9God's children cannot keep on being sinful. His life-giving power lives in them and makes them his children, so they cannot keep on sinning. 10You can tell God's children from the devil's children, because those who belong to the devil refuse to do right or to love each other.
Love Each Other
11 From the beginning you were told we must love each other. 12 Don't be like Cain, who belonged to the devil and murdered his own brother. Why did he murder him? He did it because his brother was good, and he was evil. 13My friends, don't be surprised if the people of this world hate you. 14 Our love for each other proves we have gone from death to life. But if you don't love each other, you are still under the power of death.
15If you hate each other, you are murderers, and we know murderers do not have eternal life. 16We know what love is because Jesus gave his life for us. This is why we must give our lives for each other. 17If we have all we need and see one of our own people in need, we must have pity on that person, or else we cannot say we love God. 18Children, you show love for others by truly helping them, and not merely by talking about it.
19When we love others, we know we belong to the truth, and we feel at ease in the presence of God. 20But even if we don't feel at ease, God is greater than our feelings, and he knows everything. 21Dear friends, if we feel at ease in the presence of God, we will have the courage to come near him. 22He will give us whatever we ask, because we obey him and do what pleases him. 23 God wants us to have faith in his Son Jesus Christ and to love each other. This is also what Jesus taught us to do. 24If we obey God's commandments, we will stay one in our hearts with him, and he will stay one with us. The Spirit he has given us is proof that we are one with him.
translation_text:
Aya Yɛ bebi kunu
1Mledi kiliɛ gbɛgbɛ gi Aya alɔ blɔ ɛ! Yɛ kulɔlɔ kwisrɛ si baxwa blɔ si Aya yɛ bebi ba, lɛ ní anukwarɛ mɛ ɛ, yɛ bebi kunu, lɛ ya loso kelea mɛ kéte blo, lɛ ese linu si kéte Aya a. 2Baʋia lɔlɔ ba, abla ɛ, kunu Aya yɛ bebi, pɔ̀ lɛ mɛ lídra mumɔ kidiliɛ gi kwiadzi i. Pɔ̀ kwite si xe Kristo aaŋwi i, kwiaze ŋwisi yɛ kiniɛ ɛ, lɛ ese linu si kwiamɔ yɛ kiliɛ ɖii gi ɔlɛ ɛ. 3Ɔnɔ kakaa awla gi kudeonudidi uliɔ ya kulɛ ní Kristo me e, izɛɛbitɛ isu ɔnɔ dradra ŋwisi kiliɛ gi Kristo adra kinɛ ɛ.
4Ɔnɔ kakaa gi abitɛ eboe zimizi i, yɛ eboe zimizi na ɛta ɔsã nɔ ese, lɛ ese linu si eboe zimizi kubitɛ kunu ɔsã ese kutata. 5Mlete si Kristo aŋwi tɔ yibu eboe zimizi na ɛzanɔ, lɛ liboe zimizii tɔtɔ líma ní yɛ mɛ ɛ. 6Lɛ loso ɔnɔ kakaa gi ɔlɛ ní likpɔkpɛkpɛ mɛ ni Kristo e, ɔʋíze eboe zimizi aʋa a; pɔ̀ ɔniliɛ gi ɔlɛ ní eboe zimizi aʋa ɛ, ɔ́mɔ yɛ odi i, loo óte ye e.
7Mɛ bebi ba! Mlakíki tɔ ɔnɔnɔ omeni mlo o. Ɔniliɛ klɔ gi izɛbitɛ eboe gbã ɛ, ɔnɔ dradra onu, ŋwisi kiliɛ gi Kristo onu ɔnɔ dradra kinɛ ɛ. 8Ɔniliɛ gi ɔtini eboe zimizi aʋa ɛ, onu Ozimizi ye ɔnɛ, lɛ ese linu si Ozimizi ye abitɛ eboe zimizi dɔni okpese klɔ ke. Aya Yebi ye aba ní liboe lɛ ya ɖii loso, tɔ yizizi kidiliɛ gi Ozimizi ye abitɛ ɛ.
9Aya yɛ bebi ba mɛ ɔtɔtɔ ɔ́trazɛɛbitɛ eboe zimizi tsinɔ ɔ, lɛ ese linu si Aya yɛ kilu ye kilɛ ní ba mɛ, lɛ agi Aya onu ba Ɔka onu i, bátratani eboe zimizi aʋa kutrɛ ɛ. 10Lɛ libu lidzegaga nɛ ɛbasɛ ní Aya yɛ bebi ba ni Ozimizi ye yɛ bebi ba kusi, banama petee gi baʋíbitɛ eboe gbã loo baʋídimɛ ni ba ahanuglɔ ba ɛ, bénu Aya yɛ bebi i.
Mlalɔ mlɔ ahanuglɔ ba
11Liboe lɛ gi mlenu idɔni okpese klɔ ɛ, lɛ linu si; lilɛ si kulɔ blɔ ahanuglɔ ba. 12Líma si kuze ŋwisi Kain gi, eze Ozimizi ye ɔnɛ kinɛ, xe eye yɛ ɔtutɔ yenemi ye Abel. Ege lɛ loso Kain eye ye e? Lɛ ese linu si yɛ ɔtutɔ yɛ kiboebitɛɛ kípɛ ɛ, pɔ̀ yenemi ye Abel yɛ kiboebitɛɛ kipɛ ɛ.
13Loso e, mɛ benemi ba, likítu mlo kugyɔ ɔ, xe kelea mɛ banima bevu mlo ekpɛ lipo me e. 14Kwite si kwisɛ ní kutse su lɛ kwiba ligbã́ nɛ mɛ ɛ, kwite le nite lɛ ese linu si kwidimɛ ni blɔ benemi ba. Ɔniliɛ gi ɔ́dimɛ ni banɔ ɛ, yɛ ɔkpasi ní kutseo kigaglaɛ ese kokooko. 15Banama pɔ́ gi bevu banima ɛkpɛ lipo me e, ba benu banɔye ba, lɛ mlete si banɔye ba ɛ ligbã́moblo le limá ní ba mɛ ɛ.
16Nite kwite kiliɛ gi kunɔlɔɔ kulɛ ɛ: si Yesu Kristo akɔ yɛ ligbã́ nɛ akyɛ kí blɔ. Blɔ fɛ, lilɛ si kukɔ blɔ agbã́ na akyɛ aki blɔ benemi ba. 17Xe bexonete bɛmɔ benemi gi balɛ ní kuziãziã mɛ ɛ, xe bákɔ litɔtɔ ní lɛ mɛ aki ba ɛ, batrátani kupani si bɛlɔ Aya a. 18Mɛ bebi dimɛnɔ ba, líma si blɔ kunɔlɔɔ kuze kupani ko o, lilɛ si kuze anukwarɛ kunɔlɔ gi kwibasɛ isu ní axwɛwawa mɛ ɛ.
Ɔna ɔlɛ ní Blɔ Lipo ní Aya Aŋu mɛ
19Nite kwiate si kulɛ ní anukwarɛɛ mɛ, lɛ nite kwiatrɔ likla lɛ aʋa ki isu ya ní Aya aŋu mɛ. 20Xe blɔ ina nɛ ibu blɔ lipɔ ɛ, kwite si Aya ekeme asani blɔ ina nɛ, lɛ ete liboe kakaa a.
21Lɛ loso e, mɛ baʋia dimɛnɔ ba, xe blɔ ina nɛ íbu blo lipɔ ɛ, kɔlɔ ɔna ɔlɛ ní blɔ lipo ní Aya aŋu mɛ. 22Kuzɛɛzɛ kidɔ kakaa gi kwiʋi dɔni yɛ iwla mɛ ɛ, lɛ ese linu si kuzɛɛbitɛ ní yɛ isã nɛ aʋa, lɛ kuzɛɛbitɛ kidiliɛ gi kiku yɛ liŋu i. 23Yɛ ɔsã nɔ onu si kuzɛ Yebi ye Yesu Kristo aʋa ŋa, tɔ kudimɛ ni blɔ ahanuglɔ ba, kiliɛ gi Yesu akpɛ ɔsã nɔ aki blɔ ɛ. 24Banama gi bizɛga ní Aya ɔsã nɔ aʋa ɛ, balɛ ní likpɔkpɛkpɛ mɛ ni Aya. Lɛ Aya fɛ ɛ, ɔlɛ ní likpɔkpɛkpɛ mɛ ni ba. Lɛ kɔkpɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ gi Aya aki blɔ onu i, kwite si Aya ɔlɛ ní likpɔkpɛkpɛ mɛ ni blɔ.

english_text:
God Is Love
1Dear friends, don't believe everyone who claims to have the Spirit of God. Test them all to find out if they really do come from God. Many false prophets have already gone out into the world, 2and you can know which ones come from God. His Spirit says that Jesus Christ had a truly human body. 3But when someone doesn't say this about Jesus, you know this person has a spirit that doesn't come from God and is the enemy of Christ. You knew this enemy was coming into the world and now is already here.
4Children, you belong to God, and you have defeated these enemies. God's Spirit is in you and is more powerful than the one who is in the world. 5These enemies belong to this world, and the world listens to them, because they speak its language. 6We belong to God, and everyone who knows God will listen to us. But the people who don't know God won't listen to us. This is how we can tell the Spirit that speaks the truth from the one that tells lies.
7My dear friends, we must love each other. Love comes from God, and when we love each other, it shows we have been given new life. We are now God's children, and we know him. 8God is love, and anyone who doesn't love others has never known him. 9God showed his love for us when he sent his only Son into the world to give us life. 10Real love isn't our love for God, but his love for us. God sent his Son to be the sacrifice by which our sins are forgiven. 11Dear friends, since God loved us this much, we must love each other.
12 No one has ever seen God. But if we love each other, God lives in us, and his love is truly in our hearts.
13God has given us his Spirit. This is how we know we are one with him, just as he is one with us. 14God sent his Son to be the Savior of the world. We saw his Son and are now telling others about him. 15God stays one with everyone who openly says Jesus is the Son of God. This is how we stay one with God 16and are sure God loves us.
God is love. If we keep on loving others, we will stay one in our hearts with God, and he will stay one with us. 17If we truly love others and live as Christ did in this world, we won't be worried about the day of judgment. 18A real love for others will chase those worries away. The thought of being punished is what makes us afraid. It shows we have not really learned to love.
19We love because God loved us first. 20But if we say we love God and don't love each other, we are liars. We cannot see God. So how can we love God, if we don't love the people we can see? 21The commandment that God has given us is: “Love God and love each other!”
translation_text:
Anukwarɛɛ ni Asia na Ifuɛfuɛ nɛ
1Mɛ baʋia dimɛnɔ ba, mlakízɛ banama petee gi bɛɛpani si Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ ɔlɛ ní ba awla ɛ aʋa ɛɛŋa, pɔ̀ mlapami lɛ mɛ tɔ mledi xe Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ ɛdɔ ní Aya kɔ ɔ. Lɛ ese linu si asia na beboedoplɛ glegle bɛlɛ eku kelea mɛ ɔkɔ kakaa. 2Gani kudeo liɔ ya onu i, mlate xe onu Aya yɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ: ɔnɔ kakaa gi azɛ lɛ ɛ, xe ebu le edo si Yesu Kristo aba ŋwisi ɔnɔ gbagbe kinɛ ɛ, yɛ mɛ Aya Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ ɔkpasi. 3Pɔ̀ ɔnɔ kakaa gi anada liboe lɛ ya ní Yesu su i, yɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ ɔdɔ Aya kɔ ɔ. Ɔfuɛfuɛ ɔnɔ gi ɔlɛ ní yɛ mɛ ɛ, ɛdɔ ní Kristo yɛ ahwafiɔnɛɛ kɔ; mlenu si aaba, lɛ abla ɛ ɔlɛ ní kelea mɛ kooko.
4Pɔ̀ mlɔ mɛ bebi dimɛnɔ ba, mlenu Aya banɛ, lɛ mlɛŋa asia na beboedoplɛ ba aʋa, lɛ ese linu si Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ gi ɔkpasi ní mlɔ mɛ ɛ, ɔlɛ gagla sani Ɔfuɛfuɛ ɔnɔ gi ɔkpasi ní kelea mɛ banima mɛ ɛ. 5Asia na beboedoplɛ ba klɔ ba ɛ, bizɛɛpani ní kelea mɛ eboe la su, lɛ kelea mɛ kizɛta ba kutu, lɛ ese linu si bɛdɔ kelea mɛ. 6Pɔ̀ blɔ kunu Aya banɛ. Ɔniliɛ klɔ gi ete Aya ɛ, izɛɛta blɔ kutu, ɔniliɛ klɔ gi ónu Aya ɔnɛ ɛ, ɔʋíta blɔ kutu u. Gani lɛ mɛ ɛ, kwiate lidzegaga ni anukwarɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ ni asia na Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ kusi.
Aya onu Kulɔlɔ
7Mɛ baʋia dimɛnɔ ba, mlaki tɔ kulɔ blɔ ahanuglɔ ba, lɛ ese linu si kulɔlɔ kwidɔ ní Aya klɔ. Ɔnɔ kakaa gi izɛɛlɔ banɔ ɛ, onu Aya Yebi, lɛ etè Aya. 8Ɔniliɛ gi ɔ́lɔ banɔ ɛ, óte Aya a, lɛ ese linu si Aya onu kulɔlɔ. 9Lɛ Aya ebu yɛ kulɔlɔɔ abasɛ blɔ, gani kude uliɔ ya onu gi atsisi Yebi tolee ní kelea mɛ, tɔ kwiamɔ ligbã́ gani yɛ aʋa a. 10Lɛ ya linu kulɔlɔ. Aní si kwilɔ Aya, pɔ̀ yɛ alɔ blɔ, gi akɔ̀ Yebi ye akɔ ní blɔ eboe zimizi na litukpo tɔ gani yɛ aʋa ɛ, biakɔ blɔ eboe zimizi na kɔkyɛ. 11Mɛ baʋia dimɛnɔ ba, xe nite Aya alɔ blɔ ɛ, kɔlɔ lilɛ si kulɔ blɔ ahanuglɔ ba. 12Ɔnɔnɔ ɔ́mɔ Aya oʋídi i, pɔ̀ xe kwilɔ blɔ ahanuglɔ ba ɛ, Aya ɔlɛ ní likpɔkpɛkpɛ mɛ nì blɔ, lɛ yɛ kulɔlɔɔ kwiflɔ ní blɔ mɛ.
13Kwitrɔ likla lɛ aʋa si kulɛ ní likpɔkpɛkpɛ mɛ nì Aya, lɛ ɔlɛ ní likpɔkpɛkpɛ mɛ ni blɔ, lɛ ese linu si aki blɔ yɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ. 14Lɛ kwimɔ ɔ, lɛ kwisi banɔ gɔgɔ ba si, Aya atsisi Yebi ye si ɔga aabu kelea mɛ. 15Xe ɔnutɔ ebu le edo si Yesu yɛ onu Aya Yebi ye e, ɔlɛ ní likpɔkpɛkpɛ mɛ ni Aya, lɛ Aya ɔlɛ ní likpɔkpɛkpɛ mɛ ni yɛ ɛ. 16Lɛ blɔ ɔtutɔ kwite e, lɛ kwizi Aya yɛ kulɔlɔ aki blɔ aʋa ɛɛŋa a.
Aya onu kulɔlɔ, lɛ banama gi balɛ ní kulɔlɔ mɛ ni ba ahanuglɔ ba ɛ, balɛ ní likpɔkpɛkpɛ mɛ ni Aya, lɛ Aya ɔlɛ ní likpɔkpɛkpɛ mɛ ni ba a. 17Gani kude uliɔ ya onu i, bɛbitɛ kulɔlɔɔ si kuflɔ ní blɔ kusi, tɔ ɔna aaze blɔ lipo ní Litsixwawe le aʋa, lɛ ɔna aaze blɔ lipo lɛ ese linu si blɔ ligba nɛ ní kelea mɛ ɛ, lilɛ ŋwisi Kristo yɛ lini nɛ kinɛ ɛ. 18Lifu kusese líma ní kulɔlɔ mɛ ɛ, kulɔlɔ gi kwiflɔ ɛ, kuzɛɛkpikpini lifu kusese kakaa. Loso e, kulɔlɔɔ kúflɔ ní ɔnɔ gi onu ofufluse me e, lɛ ese linu si kutuhehe kuzɛ mani lifu.
19Kuzɛɛlɔ lɛ ese linu si Aya yɛ etu blo kulɔ. 20Xe ɔnutɔ apani si malɔ Aya a, pɔ̀ evu yenemi nɛ akpɛ lipo me e, onu ɔsia nɛ. Lɛ ese linu si ɔnɔnɔ ɔ́tratani Aya gi baʋímɔ ɛ kulɔ, xe ɔ́lɔ yenemi ye gi ɛɛmɔ ɔ. 21Ɔsã lɔ klɔ gi Yesu aki blɔ onu si, banama petee gi bɛlɔ Aya ɛ, lilɛ si balɔ ba benemi ba fɛ.

english_text:
Victory over the World
1If we believe Jesus is truly Christ, we are God's children. Everyone who loves the Father will also love his children. 2If we love and obey God, we know we will love his children. 3 We show our love for God by obeying his commandments, and they are not hard to follow.
4Every child of God can defeat the world, and our faith is what gives us this victory. 5No one can defeat the world without having faith in Jesus as the Son of God.
Who Jesus Is
6Water and blood came out from the side of Jesus Christ. It wasn't just water, but water and blood. The Spirit tells about this, because the Spirit is truthful. 7In fact, there are three who tell about it. 8They are the Spirit, the water, and the blood, and they all agree.
9We believe what people tell us. But we can trust what God says even more, and God is the one who has spoken about his Son. 10If we have faith in God's Son, we have believed what God has said. But if we don't believe what God has said about his Son, it is the same as calling God a liar. 11 God has also said he gave us eternal life and this life comes to us from his Son. 12And so, if we have God's Son, we have this life. But if we don't have the Son, we don't have this life.
Knowing about Eternal Life
13All of you have faith in the Son of God, and I have written to let you know you have eternal life. 14We are certain God will hear our prayers when we ask for what pleases him. 15And if we know God listens when we pray, we are sure our prayers have already been answered.
16Suppose you see one of our people commit a sin that isn't a deadly sin. You can pray, and this person will be given eternal life. But the sin must not be one that is deadly. 17Everything that is wrong is sin, but not all sins are deadly.
18We are sure God's children do not keep on sinning. God's own Son protects them, and the devil cannot harm them.
19We are certain we come from God and the rest of the world is under the power of the devil.
20We know that Jesus Christ the Son of God has come and has shown us the true God. And because of Jesus, we now belong to the true God who gives eternal life.
21Children, you must stay away from idols.
translation_text:
Kwiŋa Kelea mɛ Aʋa
1Ɔniliɛ gi onu Aya Yebi i, yɛ azɛ lɛ aʋa aŋa si, Yesu yɛ onu Kristo ye, ɔniliɛ gi Aya atsisi i, lɛ ɔniliɛ klɔ gi alɔ Aya ɛ, alɔ Yebi ye fɛ. 2Nite kuzeete si kwilɔ Aya yɛ bebi ba, gani Aya kulɔlɔ mɛ nì yɛ ɔsã nɔ aʋa kubitɛ mɛ. 3Lɛ blɔ kulɔlɔ ki Aya ɛ, kɔ ese kunu si kuzɛɛbitɛ ní yɛ isã nɛ aʋa. Lɛ yɛ isã nɛ ɛ, ísã ɛgagla ɛki blɔ ɔ, 4lɛ ese linu si Aya Yebi kakaa aatani kelea aʋa kuŋa. Lɛ gani blɔ kuziŋaa mɛ ɛ, kuzɛɛŋa kelea aʋa. 5Nyaŋwɛ aatani kelea aʋa kuŋa a? Ɔniliɛ klɔ ko gi azɛ lɛ aʋa aŋa si Yesu yɛ onu Aya Yebi ye e.
Kidiliɛ gi kinu Katsalia ní Yesu su i
6Yesu Kristo yɛ onu ɔniliɛ gi aba a, gani kunikpɛkpɛ ni kutse gani ika kubali mɛ ɛ. Ɔ́ba gani kuni tete me e, pɔ̀ aba gani kuni ni ika mɛ ɛ. Lɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ ɔtutɔ ɛɛpani si linu anukwarɛ, lɛ ese linu si Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ onu anukwarɛɛ. 7Bidɔ tuta biŋa Katsalia. 8Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ, kuni yo, ani ika lɛ, lɛ bɛ tuta pɔ́ biipani liboe de tile e. 9Kuzɛɛzɛ banima eboe la aʋa ɛɛŋa, pɔ̀ Aya yɛ eboe la emu isanɔ ɔ, lɛ ese linu si yɛ apani eboa la ya ní Yebi ye su. 10Lɛ loso banama gi bɛzɛ Aya Yebi ye aʋa ɛɛŋa ɛ, likla trɔtrɔ ɛlɛ ya lilɛ ní ba ina nɛ mɛ, pɔ̀ banama gi bázɛ Aya aʋa aŋa ɛ, ba bɛbitɛ yɛ ɔsia nɛ, lɛ ese linu si bázɛ Aya yɛ Liboe lɛ gi apani ní Yebi ye su aʋa ɛɛŋa a. 11Likla trɔtrɔ lɛ lɛya: Aya aki blɔ ligbã moblo le, lɛ ligbã́ lɛya lilɛ ní Yebi ye me e. 12Ɔniliɛ awla gi Aya Yebi ye ɔlɛ ɛ, ligbã lilɛ ní yɛ awla, ɔniliɛ awla gi Aya Yebi ye ɔ́ma ɛ, ligbã́ limá ní yɛ awla a.
Ligbã́ Moblo le lilɛ ní Baʋaziŋa ba Awla
13Mɛɛŋwlimi eboa la ya ɛki mlɔ banama gi mlɛzɛ Aya Yebi ye linyi ne aʋa ɛɛŋa ɛ, tɔ mlete si ligbã́ moblo le lilɛ ní mlɔ awla. 14Ɔna ɔlɛ ní blɔ lipo ní Aya aŋu mɛ, lɛ ese linu si kwitrɔ likla lɛ aʋa si, izeenu xe kwiʋi ye kidɔ kakaa ní yɛ kupɛ́pɛɔ onu i. 15Lɛ xe kwite si, izɛɛnu kidɔ kakaa gi kwiʋi ye e, kɔlɔ kwite si, kuzɛɛzɛ blɔ beʋiʋi ʋa ɛdɔni yɛ klɔ.
16Xe wɔmɔ wonemi gi ɛɛbitɛ liboe zimizi gi lítragbani yɛ ɛkpɛ kutse me e, xwa Aya ní yɛ litukpo tɔ ɔki yɛ ligbã́. Lɛ ya litrɛ ɛki banama gi ba eboe zimizi na atrámani kutse e. Pɔ̀ eboe zimizi atɔ alɛ gi izɛɛmani kutse e; maʋísi si xwa Aya ní la litukpo o. 17Eboe nyani nyani kakaa enu kuzimizi, pɔ̀ kuzimizi utɔ kulɛ, gi kútramani kutse e.
18Kwite si, Aya yɛ bebiatɔtɔ baʋíze kuzimizi aʋa kɔ tsinɔ ɔ. Lɛ ese linu si Aya Yebi ye izɛɛkpɛ kuka ɛklani ba lɛ Ozimizi ye ɔ́tratani ba kidɔ nyani nyani tɔtɔ kubitɛ ɛ.
19Kwite si, kunu Aya yɛ banɛ, gi kelea petee kalɛ ní Ozimizi ye kaʋaŋaa ese fɛ ɛ. 20Lɛ kwite si, Aya Yebi ye aba a, lɛ aki blɔ kuboesenu, tɔ kwiate Aya gi yɛ onu anukwarɛɛ ɔtutɔ lɔ ɛ. Kulɛ ní likpɔkpɛkpɛ mɛ ni Aya ɔtutɔ ni likpɔkpɛkpɛ mɛ ni Yebi ye Yesu Kristo. Yɛ onu Aya ɔtutɔ lɛ Yɛ onu ligbã moblo le.
21Mɛ bebi ba, mledi isu aʋa mumɔ ní kubúbitɛ su!

english_text:
1From the church leader.
To a very special woman and her children. I truly love all of you, and so does everyone else who knows the truth. 2We love you because the truth is now in our hearts, and it will be there forever.
3I pray that God the Father and Jesus Christ his Son will be kind and merciful to us! May they give us peace and truth and love.
Truth and Love
4I was very glad to learn that some of your children are obeying the truth, as the Father told us to do. 5 Dear friend, I am not writing to tell you and your children to do something you have not done before. I am writing to tell you to love each other, which is the first thing you were told to do. 6Love means we do what God tells us. And from the beginning, he told you to love him.
7Many liars have gone out into the world. These deceitful liars are saying Jesus Christ did not have a truly human body. But they are liars and the enemies of Christ. 8So be sure not to lose what we have worked for. If you do, you won't be given your full reward. 9Don't keep changing what you were taught about Christ, or else God will no longer be with you. But if you hold firmly to what you were taught, both the Father and the Son will be with you. 10 If people won't agree to this teaching, don't welcome them into your home or even greet them. 11Greeting them is the same as taking part in their evil deeds.
Final Greetings
12I have much more to tell you, but I don't want to write it with pen and ink. I want to come and talk to you in person, because this will make us really happy.
13Greetings from the children of your very special sister.
translation_text:
Kunɔ Panɔ Dɔni Ɔhalɔ Mɛ Ɔnɔtsitsi ye Klɔ
1Ɔhalɔ mɛ ɔnɔtsitsi ye yɛ ɛɛŋwlimi iiki wɔ ɔdisami nɔ gi Aya akya a, ni wɔ bebi ba gi malɔ ní anukwarɛ mɛ ɛ. Lɛ aní mɛ tole e, pɔ̀ banama petee gi bete anukwarɛɛ ɛ, bɛlɔ mlɔ, 2lɛ ese linu si anukwarɛɛ ɔkpasi ní blɔ mɛ, lɛ aaze nì blo kɔtsini kanɔmɛ anɔmɛ ɛ.
3Atɔ Kakaa Aya yɛ ni Yesu Kristo Kakaa Yebi ye baki blɔ lipomepɛpɛ, asigagadidi ni kusuyɔ, tɔ bize blɔ binɛ ní anukwarɛ ni kulɔlɔ mɛ ɛ.
Mleze ní Kulɔlɔ Pɔ̀ Mleze ni Aŋu ní Banɔmeno Ba Su
4Isami iiku me gi mamɔ si wɔ bebi batɔ balɛ ní anukwarɛ aʋa a, kiliɛ ɖii gi Aya akpɛ ɔsã akí blɔ ɛ. 5Lɛ abla ɛ, meeʋi wɔ ɔdisami dimɛnɔɛ si, mlaki tɔ blɔ petee kudimɛ ni blɔ ahanuglɔ ba. Aní ɔsã vuvu onu gi mɛɛŋwlimi iiki mlɔ ɔ; onu ɔsã gi eze blɔ awla dɔni okpese kɔ ɔ. 6Kulɔlɔɔ su gi mɛɛpani i, kɔ ese kunu si, lilɛ si kunu Aya yɛ ɔsã nɔ kutu. Ɔsã nɔ ŋwisi kiliɛ gi mlɔ petee mlenu i dɔni okpese kɔ kinɛ ɛ, si, lilɛ si mlɔ petee mleze ní kulɔlɔ mɛ.
7Banɔmeno glegle bɛlɛ eku kelea petee me, banɔ gi bázɛ lɛ si Yesu Kristo aba ŋwisi ɔnɔ gbagbe kinɛ ɛ. Ɔniliɛ klɔ elu onu ɔnɔmeno ani Kristo yɛ ahwafiɔnɛ. 8Mleze ní aŋu plɛplɛ mɛ tɔ mlakílila kidiliɛ gi kwiwa axwɛ na ɛkɔ ɔ, pɔ̀ tɔ kwiamɔ blɔ ɔŋakɔ ní kisrɛsrɛ mɛ ɛ.
9Ɔniliɛ klɔ gi ɔ́traze Kristo bidɔbasɛbasɛ ʋɛ mɛ ɛ, pɔ̀ xe aadɔ ʋɛ kuza ɛ, ónu Aya ɔnɛ ɛ. Ɔniliɛ klɔ gi aaze bidɔbasɛbasɛ ʋɛ mɛ ɛ, yɛ ɔlɛ likpɔ ni Aya ani Obi ye. 10Kɔlɔ xe ɔnutɔ aba mlɔ klɔ ɔ, pɔ̀ yɛ bidɔbasɛbasɛ ʋɛ biga lidze ní bɛ ya su i, mlakízɛ yɛ akpɛ mlɔ kupeo me e; mlakípani yɛ fɛ. 11Lɛ ɔnɔ kakaa gi aazɛ ba ɛ, aadzi ɔʋatrɔ ki ba ní ba eboe zimizi ana gi bɛɛbitɛ mɛ ɛ.
Eboe gɔgɔ la
12Eboe alɛ mɛ awla kpaŋu gi nya maaŋwlimi ki mlɔ ɛ, pɔ̀ mátraŋwlimi la a, boŋu i, mɛɛpɛ kudeonu si miba mlɔ klɔ, tɔ kwiapani aŋu mɛ ni aŋu mɛ atɔ kwiamɔ isami mɔmɔmɔ.
13Wɔ onemidzɛ gi Aya akya ɛ, yɛ bebi ba bɛpani mlɔ.

english_text:
1 From the church leader.
To my dear friend Gaius.
I love you because we follow the truth, 2dear friend, and I pray all goes well for you. I hope you are as strong in body, as I know you are in spirit. 3It makes me very happy when the Lord's followers come by and speak openly of how you obey the truth. 4Nothing brings me greater happiness than to hear that my children are obeying the truth.
Working Together
5Dear friend, you have always been faithful in helping other followers of the Lord, even the ones you didn't know before. 6They have told the church about your love. They say you were good enough to welcome them and to send them on their mission in a way God's servants deserve. 7When they left to tell others about the Lord, they decided not to accept help from anyone who wasn't a follower. 8We must support people like them, so we can take part in what they are doing to spread the truth.
9I wrote to the church. But Diotrephes likes to be the number-one leader, and he won't pay any attention to us. 10So if I come, I will remind him of how he has been attacking us with gossip. Not only has he been doing this, but he refuses to welcome any of the Lord's followers who come by. And when other church members want to welcome them, he puts them out of the church.
11Dear friend, don't imitate the evil deeds of others, but follow the example of people who do kind deeds. They are God's children, but those who are always doing evil have never seen God.
12Everyone speaks well of Demetrius, and so does the true message that he teaches. I also speak well of him, and you know what I say is true.
Final Greetings
13I have much more to say to you, but I don't want to write it with pen and ink. 14I hope to see you soon, and then we can talk in person.
15I pray that God will bless you with peace!
Your friends send their greetings. Please give a personal greeting to each of our friends.
translation_text:
Kunɔ panɔ
1Ɔhalɔ mɛ ɔnɔtsitsi yɛ ɛɛŋwlimi iiki Gayo gi malɔ ní anukwarɛ mɛ ɛ.
2Mɛ ɔʋia dimɛnɔ ɛ, mɛɛxwa Aya si kidɔ kakaa kidɔ kɛ aʋa ki wɔ, tɔ zení isu mɛ pɛ̀pɛ̀ mɛ, agi mete si wɔlɛ mumɔ ní Ɔfuɛfuɛ mɛ ɛ. 3Isami iiku me koŋu gi benemi batɔ bɛba a, xe bɛsi mɛ kiliɛ gi wovu anukwarɛɛ ɛ, ŋwisi kiliɛ gi wɔlɛ ní anukwarɛɛ mɛ lipoe kakaa kinɛ ɛ. 4Kididɔ kíku me isami isanɔ, si tɔ minu si mɛ bebi ba balɛ ligbã ní anukwarɛɛ mɛ ɛ.
Bɛkpɔ̀ Gayo
5Mɛ ɔʋia dimɛnɔ ɛ, wonu anukwarɛ onete ní axwɛ ana gi wɔɛwa mɛ ɛki benemi ba, ki banama gi benu bababa ki wɔ kyɛ ɛ. 6Bɛpani lɛ su isi ɔsɔli ha lɔ ní ya ní wɔ kulɔlɔ su. Mɛɛki wɔ litukpo si pɔni ba tɔ batrɛ ni ba kudegaga aʋa gani kizeze liɛ mɛ gi kiaku Aya liŋu i. 7Lɛ ese linu si bekpese ba kudegaga ní axwɛwawa ki Kristo me, gi bázɛ kupɔnɔ kutɔtɔ ɛdɔni babubitɛ ba kɔ ɔ. 8Lilɛ si blɔ basɔlisɔli ba, kupɔni banɔ ba ya tɔ kwiamɔ ɔŋakɔ ní ba axwɛwawa la ki anukwarɛɛ mɛ.
Diotrefo ani Demetrie ba Kiboebitɛɛ
9Maŋwlimi keplekpa kpekpemi atɔ aki ɔsɔli ha lɔ, pɔ̀ Diotrefo gi izɛɛpɛ si yɛ yize ba onu i, ɔ́kɔ lɛmɛ ɔki mɛ liboe le e. 10Xe maba ɛ, maaki si mlate kidɔ kakaa gi abitɛ ɛ, eboe nyani nyani ana gi ɛɛpani ní blɔ su i, ni asia ana gi ɛɛsɛ ɛ! Pɔ̀ éke ye e; ɔ́ʋizɛ blɔ benemi ba a, xe bɛba ɛ, kɔpɔnɔ ɛ, izeetsi kudeonu iiki banama gi bɛpɛ si bɛɛzɛ ba ɛ, lɛ izeekeni idi si yikpikpini ba ebu ní ɔsɔli ha lɔ mɛ ɛ!
11Mɛ ɔʋia dimɛnɔ ɛ, wukígla kidɔ nyani nyani, pɔ̀ gla kidɔ gbã. Ɔniliɛ klɔ gi izɛɛbitɛ kidɔ gbã onu Aya yɛ ɔnɛ ɛ, ɔniliɛ gi izɛɛbitɛ kidɔ nyani nyani i yɛ ɔ́ʋimɔ Aya.
12Ɔnɔ kakaa ɛɛpani eboe gbã ní Demetrio su, anukwarɛɛ ɔtutɔ fɛ ɛɛpani eboe gbã ní yɛ su. Lɛ kwitrɔ likla lɛ aʋa, lɛ mlete si liboe lɛ gi kwipani i, linu anukwarɛ.
Kunɔpanɔ gɔgɔ ɔ
13Eboe alɛ mɛ awla kpaŋu gi maasi mlɔ ɛ, pɔ̀ mápɛ si migani la ní keplekpa mɛ ɛ. 14Meedi kudeonu si maamɔ mlɔ kpekpemii, tɔ kwiapani aŋu mɛ ni aŋu mɛ. 15Kusuyɔ kuze ni mlo. Mlɔ baʋia ba pɔ́ bɛpani mlɔ. Mlapani blɔ baʋia ba tole tole aki blɔ.

english_text:
1 From Jude, a servant of Jesus Christ and the brother of James.
To all who are chosen and loved by God the Father and are kept safe by Jesus Christ.
2I pray that God will greatly bless you with mercy, peace, and love!
False Teachers
3My dear friends, I really wanted to write you about God's saving power at work in our lives. But instead, I must write and ask you to defend the faith that God has once for all given to his people. 4Some godless people have sneaked in among us and are saying, “God treats us much better than we deserve, and so it is all right to be immoral.” They even deny we must obey Jesus Christ as our only Master and Lord. But long ago the Scriptures warned that these godless people were doomed.
5 Don't forget what happened to those people the Lord rescued from Egypt. Some of them did not have faith, and he later destroyed them. 6You also know about the angels who didn't do their work and left their proper places. God chained them with everlasting chains and is now keeping them in dark pits until the great day of judgment. 7 We should also be warned by what happened to the cities of Sodom and Gomorrah and the nearby towns. Their people became immoral and committed all sorts of sexual sins. Then God made an example of them and punished them with eternal fire.
8The people I am talking about are behaving just like those dreamers who destroyed their own bodies. They reject all authority and insult angels. 9 Even Michael, the chief angel, didn't dare to insult the devil, when the two of them were arguing about the body of Moses. All Michael said was, “The Lord will punish you!”
10But these people insult powers they don't know anything about. They are like senseless animals that end up getting destroyed, because they live only by their feelings. 11 Now they are in for real trouble. They have followed Cain's example and have made the same mistake that Balaam did by caring only for money. They have also rebelled against God, just as Korah did. Because of all this, they will be destroyed.
12These people are filthy minded, and by their shameful and selfish actions they spoil the meals you eat together. They are like clouds blown along by the wind, but never bringing any rain. They are like leafless trees, uprooted and dead, and unable to produce fruit. 13Their shameful deeds show up like foam on wild ocean waves. They are like wandering stars forever doomed to the darkest pits of hell.
14 Enoch was the seventh person after Adam, and he was talking about these people when he said:
Look! The Lord is coming with thousands and thousands of holy angels 15to judge everyone. He will punish all those ungodly people for all the evil things they have done. The Lord will surely punish those ungodly sinners for every evil thing they have ever said about him.
16These people grumble and complain and live by their own selfish desires. They brag about themselves and flatter others to get what they want.
More Warnings
17My dear friends, remember the warning you were given by the apostles of our Lord Jesus Christ. 18 They told you that near the end of time, selfish and godless people would start making fun of God. 19And now these people are already making you turn against each other. They think only about this life, and they don't have God's Spirit.
20Dear friends, keep building on the foundation of your most holy faith, as the Holy Spirit helps you to pray. 21And keep in step with God's love, as you wait for our Lord Jesus Christ to show how kind he is by giving you eternal life. 22Be helpful to all who may have doubts. 23Rescue any who need to be saved, as you would rescue someone from a fire. Then with fear in your own hearts, have mercy on everyone who needs it. But hate even the clothes of those who have been made dirty by their filthy deeds.
Final Prayer
24-25Offer praise to God our Savior because of our Lord Jesus Christ! Only God can keep you from falling and make you pure and joyful in his glorious presence. Before time began and now and forever, God is worthy of glory, honor, power, and authority. Amen.
translation_text:
1Keplekpaa lia kɛdɔ ní Yuda gi onu Yesu Kristo yɛ ɔnɔsumu ye ni Yakobo yenemi yɛ klɔ ɔ,
ɛki banama gi Ɔka Aya axwa a, gi alɔ ɔ, xe balɛ ní Yesu Kristo yɛ kukakpɛkpɛɔ mɛ ɛ.
2Atɔ Aya ɔki mlɔ yɛ asigagadidi, kusuyɔ ni kulɔlɔ ní kisrɛsrɛ mɛ.
Eboa la gi ɛtra gbani Asia na badɔbasɛ ba ɛkpɛ ɛ
3Mɛ baʋia veve ba, gi mazekeni edi si miŋwlimi i iki mlɔ ní kubúbú liɔ gi kulɛ ní blɔ awla ní likpɔkpɛkpɛ mɛ kyɛ ɛ, mamɔ si, liziã si miŋwlimi tɔ mikpɛ mlɔ kigagla atɔ mlaŋwlɛ mlɔ kuziŋaa gi Aya aki banama a, gi bɛzɛ yɛ aʋa ɛɛŋa ɛ, litukpo. 4Lɛ banɔatɔ gi ba kuzizi yo su bɛŋwlimi plɛ koko e, bɛza eku ní mlɔ kusi ní kikpokpo me gi mléte e. Benu banɔ gi béte Aya a, ba bɛtsrɛ blɔ Aya yɛ lipomepɛpɛ nɛ liboe le tɔ biamɔ kude tɔ biawa ba kihasi ye, lɛ bɛnada Yesu Kristo blɔ Ɔʋadi ye ni Ɔkaɛ.
5Lɛ gi mlete la ya petee fɛ ɛ, mapɛ si miplɛ aŋu iiki mlɔ si Ɔkaɛ ebu yɛ banima adɔni Egipte, pɔ̀ lɛ kede e, ezizi banama gi bázɛ yɛ aʋa aŋa ɛ. 6Aya yɛ baxwɛdzɛa ba gi béze ní kigaglaɛ liɛ gi Aya aki ba ese e, pɔ̀ xe bɛsɛ ní ba izeklɔ ɛ, bɛkpɛ ba litumu ne me gi bɛkpɛ ba bibɔ kɔtsinɔ kanɔmɛ anɔmɛ mɛ ki litsixwaxwa ní Liwe ʋidi le aʋa a. 7Nite me ke Sodom ni Gomora kɔpɔni ima sisami i nɛ gi iklani ba kyɛ ɛ, bɛyurɔ isu ya ɛki adzɛboe ni enyimeboe ni anyaniboe kakaa elu. Benu liŋwitsɛ aki banama gi betrámɔ kutuhehe ni kifu mobloe me e.
8Nite ke banɔ ba ya gi bizɛɛza balila ʋa a, bizɛbitɛ eboe zimizi ni ba tutɔ ba bisugya ʋɛ, lɛ begbe kutununu ikí Aya xe bizezizi Aya yɛ baxwɛdzɛ ba ní lifunu ne su i. 9Aya yɛ baxwɛdzɛ ba ɔka Mixael kura kyɛ ɔ́mani onugu ní ozimizi ye su, lipoe lɛ gi yɛ ni ozimizi ye behe eboe ní ɔniliɛ gi yɛ linu si ɔzɛ Mose yɛ okpo ye e su. Mixael ókeni ódi si yibu ozimizi ye lipɔ nì kukpini, kipani ko apani si “Ɔkaɛ ɔká aŋu mɛ ɔki wɔ.” 10Pɔ̀ banɔ ba ya kɔ ɛ, bizekɔ kukpini iiki kidɔ kakaa gi kɛ ese gi bénu i, lɛ ŋwisi liŋwafu mɛ baga gi baʋíbu litukpo me kinɛ ɛ, bizɛbitɛ kidɔ kakaa gi kiba ba begyodi ʋa mɛ ɛ, gi kɛ ese benu i, lɛ bidɔ bɛ klɔ ʋa ke bizɛki si bizɛkyrɔ.
11Baba ki ba! Bɛga Kain yɛ kugakli yɔ mɛ. Kɔkpɛ kibɔɛ loso e, bɛta kese ŋwisi Baalam kiniɛ, lɛ bɛyɔ kedu ya ní Aya su, lɛ bɛkyrɔ ŋwisi Kora kiniɛ.
12Banɔ ba ya benu kinyani ní mlɔ kunɔlɔɔ kɔ likpɔkpɛkpɛ lɛ bidɔ mɛ ɔŋa klɔ gi bɛŋa bidɔ mɛ kuyemose e. Benu beʋesigbanɔ gi ba tutɔ isu ya ko bɛganɔ ɔ. Balɛ ko ŋwisi aplɛ gi liwɔ lɛ lifu i klanɔ ɔ, pɔ̀ aʋídɔ dzeni i. Balɛ ko ŋwisi ise gi iʋiyo esebi liwewe ní la liyopoe le aʋa a. Ise gi bɛkpaʋi ni lɛ igu ne e, xe itse petee e. 13Balɛ ŋwisi kuni akpɔ ní axu la mɛ ɛ, gi ɛɛwa anyra na a, lɛ ba eyeyeboe la ɛɛfiʋi ŋwisi ɔfɔ ni kuni yo aŋu mɛ ɛ. Balɛ ko ŋwisi aŋwi gi ɛɛyɔ ní la ozeklɔ ɔ, xe ɛɛlila kinɛ ɛ, gi Aya abla ozekɔ akɔ ní litumu gluglu moblo le me e.
14Henox gi onu Adam yɛ osobi gloele e, edo liboe aplɛ ní banɔ ba ya su si, “Dike Ɔkaɛ atrába nì yɛ baxwɛdzɛ dradra akpe akpe ʋa, 15gi biaxwa litsi ni banima petee e, lɛ biabu banama petee gi bése Aya ɛ lipɔ, kɔkpɛ ba eboe zimizi ana gi bɛbitɛ ní kude uliɔ ɔ, gi kúma ni Aya yɛ kudeo onu u loso. Lɛ biabu enugumeboe ala gi banama klɔ ɔ, gi bizɛbitɛ eboe zimizi na ɛ, bɛpani manɔ ní yɛ su.”
16Banɔ ba ya ɛ, benu banɔ gi bizɛɛtɛ̃ ɛ, xe bizebu banɔdzedze ba lipɔ ɔ; bizeezeni ba tutɔ ba bapɛ́pɛ zimizi ba kude; bizɛɛdɔ̀ isu ya, lɛ bizɛkpɔ̀ banima kiliɛ gi biamɔ kudeonu ki ba ɔtutɔ lɔ isu ya loso o.
Bagbágba ʋa ni batrɔtrɔ ʋa
17Mɛ baʋia dimɛnɔ ba, mlaplɛ aŋu ní liboe lɛ gi blɔ Ɔkaɛ Yesu Kristo yɛ banɔkpɛkpɛ ba bɛpani aʋa a. 18Bɛsi mlɔ si, “Ní ewe gɔgɔ la mɛ ɛ, banima biaba gi biakpɛ mlɔ kakpaa a, lɛ biazeni ba ɔtutɔ ba bapɛ́pɛ gi bamá ni Aya yɛ kupɛpɛ onu i kude.”
19Banɔ ba ya ɛ, bizɛmani kulɛlɛ, gi balɛ ni batutɔ ba isu mɛ bapɛ́pɛ ʋa kude e, ɔkpɛ gi Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ ɔma ní ba mɛ loso o.
20Pɔ̀ mlɔ, mɛ baʋia dimɛnɔ ba ɛ, mlatre ní lɛ aʋa tɔ mlaʋɔ̀ mlɔ isu ya pɔnɔ ní mlɔ kuziŋa dradraa mɛ, tɔ mlaxwa Aya ní Ɔfuɛfuɛ Dradra lɔ mɛ. 21Mleze ní Aya yɛ kunɔlɔɔ mɛ, gi mleepe blɔ Ɔkaɛ Yesu Kristo yɛ asigagadidi la a, gi la aamani mlɔ ligbã́ moblo le e.
22Mledi baʋaziŋaa ba gi bápɛ si bɛɛzɛ liboe le e, asigaga, 23mlaʋa banɔ dzedze ba tɔ mlebu ba ní kifu yɛ mɛ, lɛ kí batɔ fɛ ɛ, mledi ba asigaga pɔ̀ mlabitɛ lɛ ni litɔplɛplɛkese kese ni lifu, tɔ mlakíki si ba ligbã́ zimizi ne e, lità eku ní mlɔ mɛ ɛ.
Aya Kuxwaxwa ni yɛ Kukpɔ̀kpɔ̀
24Ki ɔniliɛ gi yɛ ko aatani kuki si mlatrágleʋe e, xe aamani mlɔ kesemɔta ni isami ní yɛ kuklekleo aŋu mɛ ɛ, 25kí Aya tolee gi onu blɔ Obúbú ye e, gani Yesu Kristo, blɔ Ɔkaɛ aʋa ɛ, yɛ ko kwiakɔ kubùbù kɔ. Yɛ onu kigaglaɛ petee onete, lɛ yɛ ɛɛŋa kukusi yo ní kidɔ kakaa aʋa dɔni kanɔa mɛ kɔtsini kanɔmɛ! Atɔ liba lɛ mɛ.

english_text:
1This is what God showed to Jesus Christ, so that he could tell his servants what must happen soon. Christ then sent his angel with the message to his servant John. 2And John told everything he had seen about God's message and about what Jesus Christ had said and done.
3God will bless everyone who reads this prophecy to others, and he will bless everyone who hears and obeys it. The time is almost here.
4 From John to the seven churches in Asia.
I pray that you
will be blessed
with kindness and peace
from God, who is and was
and is coming.
May you receive
kindness and peace
from the seven spirits
before the throne of God.
5 May kindness and peace
be yours
from Jesus Christ,
the faithful witness.
Jesus was the first
to conquer death,
and he is the ruler
of all earthly kings.
Christ loves us,
and by his blood
he set us free
from our sins.
6 He lets us rule as kings
and serve God his Father
as priests.
To him be glory and power
forever and ever! Amen.
7 Look! He is coming
with the clouds.
Everyone will see him,
even the ones who stuck
a sword through him.
All people on earth
will weep because of him.
Yes, it will happen! Amen.
8 The Lord God says, “I am Alpha and Omega, the one who is and was and is coming. I am God All-Powerful!”
A Vision of the Risen Lord
9I am John, a follower together with all of you. We suffer because Jesus is our king, but he gives us the strength to endure. I was sent to Patmos Island, because I had preached God's message and had told about Jesus. 10On the Lord's day the Spirit took control of me, and behind me I heard a loud voice that sounded like a trumpet. 11The voice said, “Write in a book what you see. Then send it to the seven churches in Ephesus, Smyrna, Pergamum, Thyatira, Sardis, Philadelphia, and Laodicea.”
12When I turned to see who was speaking to me, I saw seven gold lampstands. 13 There with the lampstands was someone who seemed to be the Son of Man. He was wearing a robe that reached down to his feet, and a gold cloth was wrapped around his chest. 14 His head and his hair were white as wool or snow, and his eyes looked like flames of fire. 15 His feet were glowing like bronze being heated in a furnace, and his voice sounded like the roar of a waterfall. 16He held seven stars in his right hand, and a sharp double-edged sword was coming from his mouth. His face was shining as bright as the sun at noon.
17 When I saw him, I fell at his feet like a dead person. But he put his right hand on me and said:
Don't be afraid! I am the first, the last, 18and the living one. I died, but now I am alive forever, and I have the keys to death and the world of the dead. 19Write what you have seen and what is and what will happen after these things. 20I will explain the mystery of the seven stars you saw at my right side and the seven gold lampstands. The seven stars are the angels of the seven churches, and the lampstands are the seven churches.
translation_text:
1Eboe ala gi Aya ebu ɛbasɛ gani Yesu Kristo aʋa a, tɔ ɔbasɛ yɛ banɔsumu ba, eboe ala gi lilɛ si aba la mɛ ní lipoe kpekpemi itɔ mɛ ɛ. Aki si bete la gani yɛ ɔxwɛdzɛ gi akpɛ ní Yohanes yɛ ɔnɔ sumu ye klɔ ɔ. 2Lɛ Yohanes aŋa katsalia, lɛ apani ní kidɔ kakaa gi amɔ ɛ su, si lɛ ya linu Aya yɛ liboe le ni liboe lɛ gi lido edɔ ní Yesu Kristo onu u. 3Biakrira ɔniliɛ gi izɛkla kuboedoplɛɔ liɔ ya mɛ eboe la ɛ, lɛ biakrira banama gi beenu kɔ mɛ eboe la a, xe bizeenu kutu iiki eboe ala gi bɛŋwlimi kpɛ ní kɔ mɛ ɛ. Lɛ ese linu si lipoe le lina pɔ.
Kunɔpanɔ kwidɔ ní Yohanes klɔ ɛki isɔli ha tigloele le
4Keplekpaa lia kɛdɔ ní mɛ Yohanes klɔ ɛki Isɔli ha tigloele le ní Asia yɛ keweku ya:
Lipomɛpɛpɛ ni kusuyɔ ki mlɔ dɔni yɛ ɔniliɛ gi ɔlɛ ɛ, gi eze e, xe atrába ɛ klɔ, ni dɔni ifuɛfuɛ tigloele le gi ilɛ ní yɛ likusigbo le ɔtɔ nɔ ɛ klɔ, 5ani dɔni Yesu Kristo, gi onu anukwarɛɛ yɛ katsalia klɔ ɔ, yɛ gi onu obi tɔpu u, yɛ gi ayɔ adɔ ni bekpo ʋa mɛ ɛ, kɔpɔni kesea ʋa bekusi ba ba ɔʋaŋaɛ klɔ!
Yɛ ɔniliɛ gi alɔ blɔ ɔ, xe akɔ yɛ ìka lɛ eplo blɔ eboe zimizi na ní blɔ su i, 6Yɛ abitɛ blɔ si kuze yɛ kaʋaŋaa ni yɛ kusumu yo baka gi kwiazesumu Yɛka Aya a, yɛ linɛ linu kusudra ani kigagla dɔni kanɔmɛ tsinɔ kanɔmɛ! Atɔ liba lɛ mɛ.
7Dike, atrába ní aplɛ la mɛ,
lɛ ɔnɔ kakaa ámɔ yɛ,
banama gi bɛsɔ́ yɛ bidɔ mɛ kyɛ ɛ,
lɛ banima petee ní kesea aʋa biabemi ɔkpɛ yɛ! Ee, atɔ liba lɛ mɛ.
8“Mɛ menu Alfa ni Omega.” Nite Ɔka Aya apani, “yɛ gi ɔlɛ ɛ, xe eze e, xe atrába ɛ, kigaglaɛ petee onetee.”
Bebu Kristo ebasɛ Yohanes
9Mɛ menu Yohanes, mlɔ onemi lɛ ŋwisi ɔnɔ gi ɔlɛ ni Yesu kude e, menu mlɔ ɔgaglɔnɛ ní litɔplɛplɛkese ni ikù kpɛkpɛ ní ɔziã mɔmɔ nɔ gi izɛɛba banama gi benu yɛ kaʋaŋaa mɛ banɛ ba aʋa ɛ. Meze ní Patmos kunisi yo kese katɔ aʋa kɔkpɛ Aya yɛ liboe le ani katsalia kuŋaŋa ki Yesu loso o. 10Meze ní Ɔfuɛfuɛ Dradra lɔ mɛ ní Ɔkaɛ yɛ liwe le aʋa, lɛ menu ole gagla ɔtɔ ní mɛ kedea, ŋwisi kakuklui keeyi kinɛ, lɛ ɛpani si: 11“Kidiliɛ gi wɔmɔ ɛ, ŋwlimi kɛ kpɛ ní keplekpa mɛ, tɔ kɔ ka tsisi isɔli ha tigloele le gi ilɛ ní Efeso ni Smirna ni Pergamo kɔpɔni Tiatira ni Sardis ani Filadelfia kɔpɔni Laodikea.”
12Mekyi, si mídi ɔniliɛ gi eegu kunugu yo ni me e. Agi mekyi i, mamɔ sika kani ise tigloele, 13lɛ ní kani yɛ ise le kusi i, mamɔ ɔnutɔ gi ɔlɛ ŋwisi “Ɔnɔɛ Yebi ye” kinɛ ɛ, gi akpɛ liwu gblamadzaɛtɔ ɔ, gi liplee na yɛ kuklikpɔɔ ɔ, lɛ akɔ sika yɛ lisaba ɛtɔ akɔ anya yɛ kakɔa mɛ ɛ. 14Yɛ litukpo le ni yɛ siya sɛ sixwa pititi ŋwisi ɖeti pititi yɛ kinɛ ɛ, ŋwisi amukla kinɛ ɛ, lɛ yɛ bimbi ʋe bilɛ ŋwisi kifu binemi kinɛ ɛ, 15lɛ yɛ ikli lɛ izeŋle ŋwisi ɔla gi bebu ní kifu ligble le me kinɛ ɛ, lɛ yɛ ole lo eeyì ŋwisi inipe glegle gi iidɔ limu ne lɛ ozi lo kinɛ ɛ. 16Lɛ evu badzidziebi tiegloele ní yɛ kaŋawla mɛ ɛ, lɛ ihɛ nunu gblamadza inugu tiʋa edo edɔni yɛ onugu lo me. Yɛ aŋu na mɛ eekle ŋwisi kemizea liwo le kinɛ ɛ.
17Gi mamɔ yɛ ɛ, madɔ ní yɛ ikli lɛ onu ŋwisi ɔnɔ gi etse kinɛ ɛ. Lɛ akɔ yɛ ɔŋawla lɔ atrɔ ní mɛ aʋa lɛ apani si, “Wukíse lifu u, mɛ menu Ɔtɔpu yɛ ni Ɔgɔgɔɛ. 18Mɛ malɛ ligbã́, metse, pɔ̀ di ke, malɛ ligbã́ nɛ kɔtsinɔ ní kanɔmɛ anɔmɛ, lɛ safi ʋa gi bɛki mɛ kigagla ní kutseo ni bekpo ʋa mɛ aʋa ɛ, balɛ ní mɛ awla.
19“Lɛ loso ŋwlimi plɛ kidiliɛ gi wɔmɔ ɔ, gi kilɛ abla a ni kidiliɛ gi kitraba kɛ mɛ lɛ kede e. 20Badzidziebi tiegloele ba gi wɔmɔ ní mɛ kaŋawla mɛ ɛ, ani sika yɛ kani yɛ ise tigloele le e, lɛ eboe kpokpo la ese enu si: Badzidziebi tiegloele ba benu Aya yɛ baxwɛdzɛ kí isɔli ha tigloele le, lɛ kani ise tigloele le inu isɔli ha tigloele e.

english_text:
The Letter to Ephesus
1This is what you must write to the angel of the church in Ephesus:
I am the one who holds the seven stars in my right hand, and I walk among the seven gold lampstands. Listen to what I say.
2I know everything you have done, including your hard work and how you have endured. I know you won't put up with anyone who is evil. When some people pretended to be apostles, you tested them and found out they were liars. 3You have endured and gone through hard times because of me, yet you have not given up.
4But I do have something against you! And it is this: You don't have as much love as you used to. 5Think about where you have fallen from, and then turn back and do as you did at first. If you don't turn back, I will come and take away your lampstand. 6But there is one thing you are doing right. You hate what the Nicolaitans are doing, and so do I.
7 If you have ears, listen to what the Spirit says to the churches. I will let everyone who wins the victory eat from the life-giving tree in God's wonderful garden.
The Letter to Smyrna
8 This is what you must write to the angel of the church in Smyrna:
I am the first and the last. I died, but now I am alive! Listen to what I say.
9I know how much you suffer and how poor you are, but you are rich. I also know the cruel things being said about you by people who claim to be God's people. But they are not really God's people. They are a group that belongs to Satan.
10Don't worry about what you will suffer. The devil will throw some of you into jail, and you will be tested and made to suffer for ten days. But if you are faithful until you die, I will reward you with a glorious life.
11 If you have ears, listen to what the Spirit says to the churches. Whoever wins the victory will not be hurt by the second death.
The Letter to Pergamum
12This is what you must write to the angel of the church in Pergamum:
I am the one who has the sharp double-edged sword! Listen to what I say.
13I know you live where Satan has his throne. But you have kept true to my name. Right there where Satan lives, my faithful witness Antipas was taken from you and put to death. Even then you did not give up your faith in me.
14 I do have a few things against you. Some of you are following the teaching of Balaam. Long ago he told Balak to teach the people of Israel to eat food that had been offered to idols and to be immoral. 15Now some of you are following the teaching of the Nicolaitans. 16Turn back! If you don't, I will come quickly and fight against these people. And my words will cut like a sword.
17 If you have ears, listen to what the Spirit says to the churches. To everyone who wins the victory, I will give some of the hidden food. I will also give each one a white stone with a new name written on it. No one will know that name except the one who is given the stone.
The Letter to Thyatira
18This is what you must write to the angel of the church in Thyatira:
I am the Son of God! My eyes are like flames of fire, and my feet are like bronze. Listen to what I say.
19I know everything about you, including your love, your faith, your service, and how you have endured. I know you are doing more now than you have ever done before. 20 But I still have something against you because of that woman Jezebel. She calls herself a prophet, and you let her teach and mislead my servants to do immoral things and to eat food offered to idols. 21I gave her a chance to turn from her sins, but she did not want to stop doing these immoral things.
22I am going to strike down Jezebel. Everyone who does these immoral things with her will also be punished, if they don't stop. 23 I will even kill her followers. Then all the churches will see that I know everyone's thoughts and feelings. I will treat each of you as you deserve.
24Some of you in Thyatira don't follow Jezebel's teaching. You don't know anything about what her followers call the “deep secrets of Satan.” So I won't burden you down with any other commands. 25But until I come, you must hold firmly to the teaching you have.
26 I will give power over the nations to everyone who wins the victory and keeps on obeying me until the end. 27-28I will give each of them the same power my Father has given me. They will rule the nations with an iron rod and smash those nations to pieces like clay pots. I will also give them the morning star.
29If you have ears, listen to what the Spirit says to the churches.
translation_text:
Liboe lɛ gi aŋwlimi i aki Aya yɛ ɔxwɛdzɛɛ gi eedi Efeso ɔsɔli ha lɔ aʋa ɛ
1“Ŋwlimi kí Aya yɛ ɔxwɛdzɛɛ gi eedi Efeso ɔsɔli ha lɔ aʋa si i:
Ɔniliɛ gi evu badzidziebi tiegloele ʋa ní yɛ kaŋawla mɛ ɛ, xe ɛɛga ní sika kani yɛ ise tigloele le kusi yo e, yɛ apani eboe ala ya si: 2Mete mlɔ kiboebitɛɛ ni mlɔ axwɛwawa gagla la, ani mlɔ igyi dodo le. Lɛ mete si mlaʋítani banɔ zimizi ba eboe la kudimɛ nɔ ɔ, lɛ mlefeke banama gi bizɛɛpani si i, ba benu banɔkpɛkpɛ ba, pɔ̀ bénu idi, lɛ mlɛmɔ ba si basia nɛ benu u. 3pɔ̀ mlɛkpɛ iku nɛ, lɛ mlɛga kuyeʋiyeʋi me kɔkpɛ mɛ linyi ne loso, pɔ̀ fɛ mlépe e.
4Pɔ̀ mamɔ kiditɔ gi kípɛ ní mlɔ su u, mlɛyurɔ kulɔlɔ tɔpu liɔ gi kwize ní mlɔ awla ɛki mɛ ɛ. 5Mlaplɛ aŋu ní niklɔ gi mlɛwɔli dɔnɔ ɛ, mlekyi ɔna mɛ tɔ mlabitɛ kidiliɛ gi mlɛzɛɛbitɛ tsa ɛ, pɔ̀ xegi mlékyi ɔna mɛ ɛ, maaba mlɔklɔ lɛ maaba efeke mlɔ kani yɛ ose lo ní lɔ ozeklɔ. 6Pɔ̀ liboe lɛ ya litrɔ ní mlɔ aʋa si, mlɛtrɔ Nikolaitianɛ ma ba kiboebitɛɛ ahwafi, gi mɛkyɛ matrɔ ahwafi la ɛ.
7Ɔniliɛ awla gi kutu kulɛ ɛ, onu liboe lɛ gi Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ ɛɛpani ki isɔliha lɛ ɛ si: Ɔniliɛ gi aŋa aʋa ɛ, maaki yɛ kude si ɔŋa ligbã́ nɛ ose lo lɔ ebi yoyo la, gi ɔlɛ ní Aya yɛ Paradiso ye me e.
Liboe lɛ gi aŋwlimi i aki Aya yɛ ɔxwɛdzɛɛ gi eedi Smirna ɔsɔli ha lɔ aʋa ɛ
8“Ŋwlimi ki Aya yɛ ɔxwɛdzɛɛ gi eedi Smirna ɔsɔli ha lɔ aʋa si i: Ɔniliɛ gi yɛ onu ɔtɔpu yɛ ni ɔgɔgɔɛ ɛ, ɔniliɛ gi etse e, pɔ̀ xe ayɔ edzì ligbã́ nɛ ɛ, yɛ eboe la la ya si: 9Mete wɔ ɔzia nɔ ani wɔ lipi le pɔ̀ oxonete wonu! Mete banama gi bizɛxwa isu ya si Yudanɛ bénu u, gi ba bizɛɛpani enugumeboe mani wɔ su tɔ beezizi wɔ su u, pɔ̀ bénu Yudanɛ ɛ, gi ba ɔtsaklɔ onu Satana yɛ ɔnɛ ɛ. 10Wukíse ɔziã nɔ klɔ gi wɔtramɔ ɔ. Dike, Ozimizi ye atrákpɛ mlɔ mɛ banɔ batɔ ligba, atɔ yikeni mlo edi, lɛ mlamɔ kutuhehe ewe liɔfɔ. Pɔ̀ mleze ní anukwarɛɛ aʋa zɛkɔna ni kutse me ke e, tɔ maakí mlɔ ligbã́ nɛ likusikuto le.
11“Ɔniliɛ gi kutu kulɛ kí yɛ ɛ, onu liboe lɛ gi Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ ɛɛpani iiki Isɔliha lɛ ɛ. Ɔniliɛ gi aŋa aʋa ɛ, ɔ́tramɔ kutse ʋlaa kɔ ɔziã a.
Liboe lɛ gi aŋwlimi i aki Aya yɛ ɔxwɛdzɛɛ gi eedi Pergamo ɔsɔli ha lɔ aʋa ɛ
12“Ŋwlimi tsisi Aya yɛ ɔxwɛdzɛɛ gi eedi Pergamo ɔsɔli ha lɔ aʋa si i: Ɔniliɛ awla gi ihɛ nunu gblamadzaɛ inugu tiʋa lɛ ɔlɛ ɛ, yɛ eboe la la ya si: 13Mete ɔma nɔ klɔ mɛ gi wɔlɛ́ ɛ, niklɔ gi Ozimizi ye edu yɛ likusigbo le e. Pɔ̀ wovu mɛ linyi ne ní awla gagla a, lɛ wɔ́nada wɔ kuziŋa ní mɛ mɛ̀, ní ewe a la mɛ gi beye Antipa fɛ ɛ, gi yɛ aŋa anukwarɛɛ katsalia aki mɛ ɛ, ní mlɔ ɔma ʋidi lo me gi níklɔ Ozimizi ye ɔlɛ ɛ.
14Pɔ̀ eboe atɔ alɛ ní wɔsu u; lɛ ese linu si banama gi bevu Baalam kudɔbasɛ yɔ ní awla ɛ, balɛ ní wɔ su, gi yɛ abasɛ Balak si, omeni Israel bebi ba onu tɔ babitɛ eboe zimizi gani bidɔ ŋaŋa gi bɛki babu ʋa mɛ ɛ, ani tɔ bawa kihasi ye. 15Niteme ke banama gi bevu Nikolaitia nɛ ma bidɔbasɛbasɛ mɛ ní awla ɛ, kyɛ bákpasi ní mlɔ kusi. 16Lɛ loso kyi ní wɔ ɔna mɛ; xe aníte e, maaba wɔ klɔ kpekpe, lɛ maakɔ ihɛ liɛ gi ɔlɛ ní mɛ onugu lo me e, maakɔ yɛ ɔta kigũ nì ba.
17Ɔniliɛ gi kutu kulɛ yɛ ɛ, onu liboe lɛ gi Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ ɛɛpani iiki Isɔliha lɛ ɛ. Ɔniliɛ gi aŋa aʋa ɛ, maaki yɛ mana liɛ gi bekpo e yɛ ɔtɔ, lɛ maaki yɛ likla pititi, gi bɛŋwlimi linyi vuvu itrɔ lɛ aʋa a, linyi gi ɔnɔ dzedze ɔtɔtɔ ɔ́trate e, sani ɔniliɛ gi yɛ azɛ lɛ ɛ.
Liboe lɛ gi aŋwlimi i aki Aya yɛ ɔxwɛdzɛɛ gi eedi Tiatira ɔsɔli ha lɔ aʋa ɛ
18“Lɛ ŋwlimi ki aya yɛ ɔxwɛdzɛɛ gi eedi Tiatira ɔsɔli ha lɔ aʋa si i:
Aya Yebi ye, ɔniliɛ gi yɛ bimbi ʋe bilɛ ŋwisi kifu u, gi kiisɔ kinɛ ɛ, xe yɛ ikli lɛ ilɛ ŋwisi ɔlaakyikyi gi bɛkpɛ kifu mɛ kinɛ ɛ, yɛ apani eboa la si: 19Mete wɔ axwɛwawa la, wɔ kunɔlɔɔ, ni wɔ kuziŋaa ani wɔ kunɔsumu yo kɔpɔni wɔ kukenidi yo, lɛ wɔɛkpɛ ikù nɛ abla ɛsani kiliɛ gi lize kutɔpu yɔ ɔ.
20Pɔ̀ mamɔ liboe lɛ ya ní wɔ su si, wɔki ɔdzɛ liɛ klɔ Isabel gi ɛɛxwa isu ya si ye nu oboedoplɛ ɛ, kudeonu. Gani yɛ bidɔbasɛbasɛ ʋɛ mɛ ɛ, eemeni mɛ banɔsumu ba ɛkpɛ ní kihasi ye me, ani tɔ baŋa bidɔ bɛ ya gi bɛki babu ʋa ɛ. 21Maki yɛ lipoe, si okyi yɛ ɔna mɛ dɔni yɛ kihasiwawaɛ mɛ; pɔ̀ ɔ́dimɛ ɛ. 22Lɛ loso, matrasa yɛ plɛ ɔnyanya ɔtɔsi aʋa, lɛ banama gi bɛwa kihasi ye ni yɛ ɛ, xegi békyi adɔ ni ba kiboebitɛ liɛ ya mɛ ɛ, maakɔ kupepe ʋidi ukpɛ ní ba mɛ ɛ. 23Maaye yɛ bebi ba. Lɛ Isɔliha lɛ petee iate si, mɛ menu ɔniliɛ gi izɛɛpami ina nɛ ni begyodi ʋa mɛ, lɛ maatsa lɛ ɔki mlɔ mɛ ɔkakaa ní yɛ kiboebitɛɛ onu u. 24Abla mɛɛsi mlɔ banɔ gɔgɔ ba ní Tiatira, mlɔ banama gi mlévu bidɔ basɛbasɛɛ bɛ ya ní awla a, xe bákpasi Ozimizi ye yɛ eboe kpokpo la a, gi áma ní la mɛ ɛ. Mátratrɔ lidɔtu dzedze tɔtɔ ní mlɔ aʋa tɔ ɔ. 25Kidiliɛ gi kilɛ ní mlɔ awla ɛ, mlevu kɛ mɛ gagla a, kɔzɛna ni lipoe lɛ gi maaba ɛ.
26Lɛ ɔniliɛ gi aŋa aʋa a, xe izɛɛbitɛ ní mɛ kupɛpɛɔ aʋa a, zɛkɔna ni ɔgù kɔ ɛ, maaki yɛ kigagla ní amade na aʋa.
27‘Aagbani ba ni ɔwla gagla,
lɛ ayaʋi ba xwitsixwitsi ŋwisi kufukpo kinɛ’,
ŋwisi kiliɛ gi mɛ fɛ mamɔ kigagla dɔni Mɔka klɔ kinɛ ɛ. 28Lɛ maakɔ livlɛ lɛ ɔdzidziobi ye aki yɛ. 29Ɔniliɛ gi kutu kulɛ yɛ ɛ, onu liboe lɛ gi Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ ɛɛpani iiki Isɔliha lɛ ɛ.

english_text:
The Letter to Sardis
1This is what you must write to the angel of the church in Sardis:
I have the seven spirits of God and the seven stars. Listen to what I say.
I know what you are doing. Everyone may think you are alive, but you are dead. 2Wake up! You have only a little strength left, and it is almost gone. So try to become stronger. I have found that you are not completely obeying God. 3 Remember the teaching you were given and heard. Hold firmly to it and turn from your sins. If you don't wake up, I will come when you least expect it, just as a thief does.
4A few of you in Sardis have not dirtied your clothes with sin. You will walk with me in white clothes, because you are worthy. 5 Everyone who wins the victory will wear white clothes. Their names will not be erased from the book of life, and I will tell my Father and his angels that they are my followers.
6If you have ears, listen to what the Spirit says to the churches.
The Letter to Philadelphia
7 This is what you must write to the angel of the church in Philadelphia:
I am the one who is holy and true, and I have the keys that belonged to David. When I open a door, no one can close it. And when I close a door, no one can open it. Listen to what I say.
8I know everything you have done. And I have placed before you an open door no one can close. You were not very strong, but you obeyed my message and did not deny you are my followers. 9 Now you will see what I will do with those people who belong to Satan's group. They claim to be God's people, but they are liars. I will make them come and kneel down at your feet. Then they will know that I love you.
10You obeyed my message and endured. So I will protect you from the time of testing everyone in all the world must go through. 11I am coming soon. So hold firmly to what you have, and no one will take away the crown you will be given as your reward.
12 Everyone who wins the victory will be made into a pillar in the temple of my God, and they will stay there forever. I will write on each of them the name of my God and the name of his city. It is the new Jerusalem my God will send down from heaven. I will also write on them my own new name.
13If you have ears, listen to what the Spirit says to the churches.
The Letter to Laodicea
14 This is what you must write to the angel of the church in Laodicea:
I am the one called Amen! I am the faithful and true witness and the source of God's creation. Listen to what I say.
15I know everything you have done, and you are not cold or hot. I wish you were either one or the other. 16But since you are lukewarm and neither cold nor hot, I will spit you out of my mouth. 17You claim to be rich and successful and to have everything you need. But you don't know how bad off you really are. You are pitiful, poor, blind, and naked.
18Buy your gold from me. It has been refined in a fire, and it will make you rich. Buy white clothes from me. Wear them and you can cover up your shameful nakedness. Buy medicine for your eyes, so you will be able to see.
19 I correct and punish everyone I love. So make up your minds to turn away from your sins. 20Listen! I am standing and knocking at your door. If you hear my voice and open the door, I will come in and we will eat together. 21Everyone who wins the victory will sit with me on my throne, just as I won the victory and sat with my Father on his throne.
22If you have ears, listen to what the Spirit says to the churches.
translation_text:
Liboe lɛ gi aŋwlimi i aki Aya yɛ ɔxwɛdzɛɛ gi eedi Sardis ɔsɔli ha lɔ aʋa ɛ
1“Ŋwlimi ki Aya yɛ ɔxwɛdzɛɛ gi eedi Sardis ɔsɔli ha lɔ aʋa si i: Ɔniliɛ awla gi Aya yɛ ifuɛfuɛ tigloele le ni badzidziebi tigloele ba balɛ ɛ, yɛ ɛɛpani eboe ala ya si: Mete wɔ axwɛwawa la, linyi lilɛ wɔ awla si, wɔlɛ ligbã́ pɔ̀ wotse e. 2Nyime tɔ kpɛ bidɔ ɛmɛ gi bigɔ ɔ, xe biatse e, kigagla, lɛ ese linu si mámɔ si wɔ axwɛwawa la ɛna ní mɛ Aya aŋu mɛ ɛ. 3Lɛ loso e, plɛ aŋu ní kidiliɛ gi wɔzɛ ɛ, xe wonu i aʋa, atɔ bitɛ lɛ aʋa, tɔ kyi ɔna mɛ. Xegi wɔ́ma ní aŋu plɛplɛ mɛ ɛ, maaba do ní wɔ aʋa ŋwisi oze kinɛ ɛ, lɛ wɔtrate ɔnyɔsasa nɔ mɛ gi maado ní wɔ aʋa a.
4Pɔ̀ banɔ klakla atɔ balɛ ní Sardis, gi bélulu ba ewu la a. Ba biakpɛ ewu pititi lɛ biaga ni mɛ: lɛ ese linu si ba begyeno o. 5Ɔniliɛ gi aŋa aʋa ɛ, biakpɛ liwu pititi kí yɛ ŋwisi ba kiniɛ ɛ, lɛ mátratsoli yɛ linyi ne ni ligbã́ nɛ keplekpaa mɛ liwewe, pɔ̀ madra yɛ linyi ne su ní Mɔka ni yɛ baxwɛdzɛ ba aŋu mɛ ɛ. 6Ɔniliɛ gi kutu kuli yɛ ɛ, onu liboe lɛ gi Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ ɛɛpani iiki Isɔliha lɛ ɛ.
Liboe lɛ gi aŋwlimi i aki Aya yɛ ɔxwɛdzɛɛ gi eedi Filadelfia ɔsɔli ha lɔ aʋa ɛ
7“Ŋwlimi ki Aya yɛ ɔxwɛdzɛɛ liɛ gi eedi Filadelfia ɔsɔli ha lɔ aʋa si i: Ɔniliɛ gi onu ɔdradra yɛ ni anukwarɛɛ onete ye, ɔniliɛ gi yɛ awla Dawid yɛ safi ye ɔlɛ ɛ, ɔniliɛ gi izɛdra xe ɔnɔnɔ ɔʋítani kudà a, xe izɛda a, xe ɔnɔnɔ ɔʋítani kudra ɛ, yɛ eboe la la ya si: 8Mete wɔ axwɛwawa la! Dike, madra opupo gi ɔnɔnɔ ɔ́tratani kudà aki wɔ ɔ: lɛ ese linu si mete si kigagla sisami itɔ ko kilɛ ní wɔ awla a, pɔ̀ wɔbitɛ ní mɛ liboe le aʋa a, lɛ wɔ́nada si wote mɛ linyi ne e. 9Dike, matraki si banama gi benu Satana yɛ ɔtsaklɔ banima ɛ, banama gi bizɛxwa isu ya si benu Yudanɛ ɛ, pɔ̀ xe bénu u, boŋu i benu basia nɛ, dike maaki si biabakpɛ litukpo kese aki wɔ, lɛ biazɛ lɛ si malɔ wɔ. 10Kiliɛ gi wovu mɛ isã nɛ ní awla a, xe wɔkpɛ ikù nɛ ni litɔplɛplɛkese loso e, mɛfɛ maawe wo ní litsixwaxwa lɛ lipoe lɛ gi litrába kelea petee aʋa mɛ ɛ, gi lɛ biafeke banama a, gi balɛ ní kesea aʋa odi i me.
11Matraba kpekpemii. Vu kidiliɛ gi kilɛ ní wɔ awla mɛ gagla a, tɔ ɔnɔnɔ ɔkizɛ wɔ likusikuto le ní wɔ awla a. 12Ɔniliɛ gi aŋa aʋa ɛ, maabitɛ yɛ tɔ aaze ɔgbase ní mɛ Aya yɛ lisɔligba lɛ mɛ. Lɛ ɔ́trasɛ ní niklɔ liwewe tɔ ɔ. Maaŋwlimi mɛ Aya yɛ linyi ne trɔ ní yɛ aʋa. Lɛ maaŋwlimi mɛ Aya yɛ ɔma ʋidi no lɔ linyi ne, gi onu Yerusalem vuvu yɛ gi eeple dɔ ní mɛ Aya gi ɔlɛ ní lifunu ne klɔ trɔ ní yɛ aʋa a. Lɛ maaŋwlimi mɛ linyi vuvu nɛ trɔ ni yɛ aʋa a. 13Ɔniliɛ gi kutu kulɛ yɛ ɛ, onu liboe lɛ gi Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ ɛɛpani iiki isɔliha lɛ ɛ.
Liboe lɛ gi aŋwlimi aki Aya yɛ ɔxwɛdzɛɛ gi eedi Laodikea ɔsɔli ha lɔ aʋa ɛ
14“Ŋwlimi ki Aya yɛ ɔxwɛdzɛɛ gi eedi Laodikea ɔsɔli ha lɔ aʋa si i: Ɔniliɛ gi bizɛxwa si Amen i, gi yɛ onu anukwarɛɛ ni katsalia ɔtutɔ lɔ ɛ, ɔniliɛ gi gani yɛ aʋa Aya abitɛ kidɔ kakaa ɛ, yɛ eboe la la ya si: 15Mete wɔ axwɛwawa la si, wɔ́yɔ fɛ, lɛ wɔ́zɛ kifu fɛ. Nya mapɛ si tɔ wɔayɔ loo wɔazɛ kifu. 16Pɔ̀ gi wɔgblɔ̀ ɛ, wɔ́zɛ kifu, xe wɔ́yɔ fɛ loso e, matrapi wɔ ebu ní mɛ onugu lo me. 17Lɛ ese linu si, wuzɛɛpani si, “Menu oxonete, lɛ bidɔ bilɛ ní mɛ awla a, lɛ kididɔ kiziã mɛ ɛ.” pɔ̀ wóte si, ɔzianete ni ɔsigaganete, opiplinete, kaŋupo ani ɔnɔ gi awɔ liyà yɛ wònu u. 18Migba wɔ tɔ dzi sika, gi bɛwlɔ ní kifu mɛ ɛ, xe yɛ su adra a, ní mɔklɔ, tɔ wɔadzi oxonete, ni kusa pititi gi wɔanyime e, atɔ wɔakɔ okpo wɔ liyà lɛ mɛ, ani kimbi ye kuwa, tɔ wɔala ní wɔ bimbi ʋe aʋa tɔ wɔamɔ bidɔ.
19Mizɛɛtɛni banama gi malɔ ɛ, lɛ mizeehe kutu iiki ba; lɛ loso e, kpɛ ikù nɛ tɔ kyí ɔna mɛ. 20Dike, malɛɛ ní opupo lo onu gi mɛɛsa opupo lo e; xe ɔnutɔ enu mɛ ole lo o, xe adra opupo lo e, maati iku yɛ aʋa, lɛ maaŋa bidɔ nì yɛ ɛ, lɛ yɛ fɛ aaŋa bidɔ nì mɛ ɛ.
21Ɔniliɛ gi aŋa aʋa ɛ, maaki yɛ kudeonu gi aaze pɔni mɛ ní mɛ likusigbo le aʋa a, ŋwisi kiliɛ gi mɛ fɛ maŋa aʋa a, xe medi ni Mɔka ní yɛ likusigbo le aʋa kinɛ ɛ. 22Ɔniliɛ gi kutu kuli yɛ ɛ, onu liboe lɛ gi Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ ɛɛpani ki Isɔliha lɛ ɛ.”

english_text:
Worship in Heaven
1After this, I looked and saw a door that opened into heaven. Then the voice that had spoken to me at first and that sounded like a trumpet said, “Come up here! I will show you what must happen next.” 2 Right then the Spirit took control of me, and there in heaven I saw a throne and someone sitting on it. 3The one who was sitting there sparkled like precious stones of jasper and carnelian. A rainbow that looked like an emerald surrounded the throne.
4Twenty-four other thrones were in a circle around that throne. And on each of these thrones there was an elder dressed in white clothes and wearing a gold crown. 5 Flashes of lightning and roars of thunder came out from the throne in the center of the circle. Seven torches, which are the seven spirits of God, were burning in front of the throne. 6 Also in front of the throne was something that looked like a glass sea, clear as crystal.
Around the throne in the center were four living creatures covered front and back with eyes. 7The first creature was like a lion, the second one was like a bull, the third one had the face of a human, and the fourth was like a flying eagle. 8 Each of the four living creatures had six wings, and their bodies were covered with eyes. Day and night they never stopped singing,
“Holy, holy, holy is the Lord,
the all-powerful God,
who was and is
and is coming!”
9The living creatures kept praising, honoring, and thanking the one who sits on the throne and who lives forever and ever. 10At the same time the 24 elders knelt down before the one sitting on the throne. And as they worshiped the one who lives forever, they placed their crowns in front of the throne and said,
11 “Our Lord and God,
you are worthy
to receive glory,
honor, and power.
You created all things,
and by your decision they are
and were created.”
translation_text:
Likusigbo le ní lifunu ne
1Lɛ ya kede e, gi meedi i, dike mamɔ opupo ɔtɔ gi ɛdra edu ní lifunu ne e. Lɛ ole tɔpu ɔnɔ gi menu i, egu kunugu yo ni me ŋwisi kakuklui kinɛ si: Ga ba lifunu ne ní ya, tɔ mibasɛ wɔ eboe ala gi ɛtraba la mɛ ní la ya kede e. 2Kyɔŋ klɔ ko e Aya yɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ eeku ní mɛ mɛ̀, lɛ mamɔ likusigbo gi bedu ní lifunu ne, lɛ ɔnutɔ odi ní lɛ aʋa ɛ. 3Ɔniliɛ klɔ gi odi ní lɛ aʋa ɛ, yɛ isu ya iitsi kifu yɛ, kiwɔwɔ ni kikyikyi ŋwisi akla zi kedzi gi bizɛɛxwa si yaspis likla ani kanelia likla kinɛ ɛ, lɛ ɔkũ yɛ, gi ɔlɛ ŋwisi emerald likla loo ŋwisi liwlakpa wɔwɔ kinɛ ɛ, aklani likusigbo le e. 4Lɛ ekusigbo avitaʋa ni tene dzedze ɛklani likusigbo le, lɛ banɔtsitsi avitaʋa ni tiene bedi ní ekusigbo ala klɔ aʋa ɛ. Bɛkpɛ bidɔ pititi, lɛ bekume sika ekusikuto ní ba etukpo la a. 5Lɛ dzeni ye azeebu kifu yɛ xe azaalaʋi i, xe azeegu adɔni likusigbo le me e. Lɛ ní likusigbo le ɔtɔ nɔ ɛ, kani tiegloele bɛɛsɔ. Ba benu Aya yɛ ifuɛfuɛ tigloele le. 6Ní likusigbo le ɔtɔ nɔ ɛ, kiditɔ gi kilɛ ŋwisi axu la a, afini gi la mɛ ɛzrɔ ɔ, xe eekle nianiania ŋwisi azinzi kinɛ ɛ.
Lɛ bidɔ gbagbe tune, gi biyi bimbi ʋe, ɔtɔ ni kede, bize ní kusio ani klani likusigbo le. 7Lɛ kidɔgbagbe tɔpu yɛ kilɛ lɛ ko ŋwisi gyata yɛ kinɛ ɛ, lɛ kidɔ gbagbe ʋla ɛ kilɛ ŋwisi ligumenyime kinɛ ɛ, lɛ kidɔ gbagbɛ tanɛ ɛ kɛ aŋu na mɛ alɛ ŋwisi ɔnɔ aŋu mɛ kinɛ ɛ, lɛ kidɔ gbagbe nene ke e, kilɛ ŋwisi kadzi gi keepruru kinɛ ɛ. 8Bidɔ gbagbe tune ʋe bikákaa siwlasasa tuglo silɛ ki bɛ, lɛ bimbi ʋe bilɛ klani bɛsu ní bɛ siwlasasa sɛ ese fɛ. Lɛ biʋízemɔni elesi ni kemize, bizɛɛkpɔ̀ Aya si:
“Kusudra, kusudra, kusudra kunu Ɔkaɛ, Aya kigaglaɛ petee Ɔkaɛ kunɛ, Yɛ gi eze e, xe ɔlɛ ɛ, xe atrába ɛ!”
9Lɛ xe bidɔ gbagbe ʋe biki ɔniliɛ gi odi ní likusigbo le aʋa kusudra kubùbù yo ni ɖasedodo e, xe ɔlɛ ligbã́ nɛ dɔni kanɔmɛ tsini kanɔmɛ ɛ, 10kɔlɔ banɔtsitsi avitaʋa ni tiene ba bɛdɔ ní kesea ní ɔniliɛ klɔ gi odi ní likusigbo le aʋa yɛ aŋu mɛ ɛ, kɔ bɛkpɔ̀ ɔniliɛ gi ɔlɛ ligbã́ nɛ tsini kanɔmɛ anɔmɛ ɛ. Kɔ bɛkɔ ba ekusikuto la ɛplɛ ní likusigbo le ɔtɔ nɔ, kɔ bɛpani si:
11“Blɔ Ɔkaɛ nì Aya, wɔ wogyeno si, wɔazɛ kusudra ni kubùbù yo ani kigaglaɛ.
Lɛ ese linu si wɔ wɔbitɛ kidɔ kakaa, lɛ bilɛ lɛ ese linu si wɔbitɛ bɛ ní wɔ kupɛ́pɛɔ onu.”

english_text:
The Scroll and the Lamb
1 In the right hand of the one sitting on the throne I saw a scroll that had writing on the inside and on the outside. And it was sealed in seven places. 2I saw a mighty angel ask with a loud voice, “Who is worthy to open the scroll and break its seals?” 3No one in heaven or on earth or under the earth was able to open the scroll or look inside it.
4I cried hard because no one was found worthy to open the scroll or look inside it. 5 Then one of the elders said to me, “Stop crying and look! The one who is called both the ‘Lion from the Tribe of Judah’ and ‘King David's Great Descendant’ has won the victory. He will open the scroll and its seven seals.”
6 Then I looked and saw a Lamb standing in the center of the throne surrounded by the four living creatures and the elders. The Lamb looked as if it had once been killed. It had seven horns and seven eyes, which are the seven spirits of God, sent out to all the earth.
7The Lamb went over and took the scroll from the right hand of the one who sat on the throne. 8 After he had taken it, the four living creatures and the 24 elders knelt down before him. Each of them had a harp and a gold bowl full of incense, which are the prayers of God's people. 9 Then they sang a new song,
“You are worthy
to receive the scroll
and open its seals,
because you were killed.
And with your own blood
you bought for God
people from every tribe,
language, nation, and race.
10 You let them become kings
and serve God as priests,
and they will rule on earth.”
11 As I looked, I heard the voices of a large number of angels around the throne and the voices of the living creatures and of the elders. There were millions and millions of them, 12and they were saying in a loud voice,
“The Lamb who was killed
is worthy to receive power,
riches, wisdom, strength,
honor, glory, and praise.”
13Then I heard all beings in heaven and on the earth and under the earth and in the sea offer praise. Together, all of them were saying,
“Praise, honor, glory,
and strength
forever and ever
to the one who sits
on the throne
and to the Lamb!”
